Finally, in order for transactional guarantees\n183:  * to be realized from end-to-end, the consumers must be configured to read only committed messages as well.\n184:  * </p>\n185:  * <p>\n186:  * The purpose of the <code>transactional.id</code> is to enable transaction recovery across multiple sessions of a\n187:  * single producer instance. It would typically be derived from the shard identifier in a partitioned, stateful, application.\n188:  * As such, it should be unique to each producer instance running within a partitioned application.\n189:  * </p>\n190:  * <p>All the new transactional APIs are blocking and will throw exceptions on failure. The example\n191:  * below illustrates how the new APIs are meant to be used. It is similar to the example above, except that all\n192:  * 100 messages are part of a single transaction.\n193:  * </p>\n194:  * <p>\n195:  * <pre>\n196:  * {@code\n197:  * Properties props = new Properties();\n198:  * props.put(\"bootstrap.servers\", \"localhost:9092\");\n199:  * props.put(\"transactional.id\", \"my-transactional-id\");\n200:  * Producer<String, String> producer = new KafkaProducer<>(props, new StringSerializer(), new StringSerializer());\n201:  *\n202:  * producer.initTransactions();\n203:  *\n204:  * try {\n205:  *     producer.beginTransaction();\n206:  *     for (int i = 0; i < 100; i++)\n207:  *         producer.send(new ProducerRecord<>(\"my-topic\", Integer.toString(i), Integer.toString(i)));\n208:  *     producer.commitTransaction();\n209:  * } catch (ProducerFencedException | OutOfOrderSequenceException | AuthorizationException e) {\n210:  *     // We can't recover from these exceptions, so our only option is to close the producer and exit.\n211:  *     producer.close();\n212:  * } catch (KafkaException e) {\n213:  *     // For all other exceptions, just abort the transaction and try again.\n214:  *     producer.abortTransaction();\n215:  * }\n216:  * producer.close();\n217:  * } </pre>\n218:  * </p>\n219:  * <p>\n220:  * As is hinted at in the example, there can be only one open transaction per producer. All messages sent between the\n221:  * {@link #beginTransaction()} and {@link #commitTransaction()} calls will be part of a single transaction. When the\n222:  * <code>transactional.id</code> is specified, all messages sent by the producer must be part of a transaction.\n223:  * </p>\n224:  * <p>\n225:  * The transactional producer uses exceptions to communicate error states. In particular, it is not required\n226:  * to specify callbacks for <code>producer.send()</code> or to call <code>.get()</code> on the returned Future: a\n227:  * <code>KafkaException</code> would be thrown if any of the\n228:  * <code>producer.send()</code> or transactional calls hit an irrecoverable error during a transaction. See the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)}\n229:  * documentation for more details about detecting errors from a transactional send.\n230:  * </p>\n231:  * </p>By calling\n232:  * <code>producer.abortTransaction()</code> upon receiving a <code>KafkaException</code> we can ensure that any\n233:  * successful writes are marked as aborted, hence keeping the transactional guarantees.\n234:  * </p>\n235:  * <p>\n236:  * This client can communicate with brokers that are version 0.10.0 or newer. Older or newer brokers may not support\n237:  * certain client features.  For instance, the transactional APIs need broker versions 0.11.0 or later. You will receive an\n238:  * <code>UnsupportedVersionException</code> when invoking an API that is not available in the running broker version.\n239:  * </p>\n240:  */\n241: public class KafkaProducer<K, V> implements Producer<K, V> {\n242: \n243:     private final Logger log;\n244:     private static final String JMX_PREFIX = \"kafka.producer\";\n245:     public static final String NETWORK_THREAD_PREFIX = \"kafka-producer-network-thread\";\n246:     public static final String PRODUCER_METRIC_GROUP_NAME = \"producer-metrics\";\n247: \n248:     private final String clientId;\n249:     // Visible for testing\n250:     final Metrics metrics;\n251:     private final KafkaProducerMetrics producerMetrics;\n252:     private final Plugin<Partitioner> partitionerPlugin;\n253:     private final int maxRequestSize;\n254:     private final long totalMemorySize;\n255:     private final ProducerMetadata metadata;\n256:     private final RecordAccumulator accumulator;\n257:     private final Sender sender;\n258:     private final Sender.SenderThread ioThread;\n259:     private final Compression compression;\n260:     private final Sensor errors;\n261:     private final Time time;\n262:     private final Plugin<Serializer<K>> keySerializerPlugin;\n263:     private final Plugin<Serializer<V>> valueSerializerPlugin;\n264:     private final ProducerConfig producerConfig;\n265:     private final long maxBlockTimeMs;\n266:     private final boolean partitionerIgnoreKeys;\n267:     private final ProducerInterceptors<K, V> interceptors;\n268:     private final ApiVersions apiVersions;\n269:     private final TransactionManager transactionManager;\n270:     // Init value is needed to avoid NPE in case of exception raised in the constructor\n271:     private Optional<ClientTelemetryReporter> clientTelemetryReporter = Optional.empty();\n272: \n273:     /**\n274:      * A producer is instantiated by providing a set of key-value pairs as configuration. Valid configuration strings\n275:      * are documented <a href=\"http://kafka.apache.org/documentation.html#producerconfigs\">here</a>. Values can be\n276:      * either strings or Objects of the appropriate type (for example a numeric configuration would accept either the\n277:      * string \"42\" or the integer 42).\n278:      * <p>\n279:      * Note: after creating a {@code KafkaProducer} you must always {@link #close()} it to avoid resource leaks.\n280:      * @param configs   The producer configs\n281:      *\n282:      */\n283:     public KafkaProducer(final Map<String, Object> configs) {\n284:         this(configs, null, null);\n285:     }\n286: \n287:     /**\n288:      * A producer is instantiated by providing a set of key-value pairs as configuration, a key and a value {@link Serializer}.\n289:      * Valid configuration strings are documented <a href=\"http://kafka.apache.org/documentation.html#producerconfigs\">here</a>.\n290:      * Values can be either strings or Objects of the appropriate type (for example a numeric configuration would accept\n291:      * either the string \"42\" or the integer 42).\n292:      * <p>\n293:      * Note: after creating a {@code KafkaProducer} you must always {@link #close()} it to avoid resource leaks.\n294:      * @param configs   The producer configs\n295:      * @param keySerializer  The serializer for key that implements {@link Serializer}. The configure() method won't be\n296:      *                       called in the producer when the serializer is passed in directly.\n297:      * @param valueSerializer  The serializer for value that implements {@link Serializer}. The configure() method won't\n298:      *                         be called in the producer when the serializer is passed in directly.\n299:      */\n300:     public KafkaProducer(Map<String, Object> configs, Serializer<K> keySerializer, Serializer<V> valueSerializer) {\n301:         this(new ProducerConfig(ProducerConfig.appendSerializerToConfig(configs, keySerializer, valueSerializer)),\n302:                 keySerializer, valueSerializer, null, null, null, new ApiVersions(), Time.SYSTEM);\n303:     }\n304: \n305:     /**\n306:      * A producer is instantiated by providing a set of key-value pairs as configuration. Valid configuration strings\n307:      * are documented <a href=\"http://kafka.apache.org/documentation.html#producerconfigs\">here</a>.\n308:      * <p>\n309:      * Note: after creating a {@code KafkaProducer} you must always {@link #close()} it to avoid resource leaks.\n310:      * @param properties   The producer configs\n311:      */\n312:     public KafkaProducer(Properties properties) {\n313:         this(properties, null, null);\n314:     }\n315: \n316:     /**\n317:      * A producer is instantiated by providing a set of key-value pairs as configuration, a key and a value {@link Serializer}.\n318:      * Valid configuration strings are documented <a href=\"http://kafka.apache.org/documentation.html#producerconfigs\">here</a>.\n319:      * <p>\n320:      * Note: after creating a {@code KafkaProducer} you must always {@link #close()} it to avoid resource leaks.\n321:      * @param properties   The producer configs\n322:      * @param keySerializer  The serializer for key that implements {@link Serializer}. The configure() method won't be\n323:      *                       called in the producer when the serializer is passed in directly.\n324:      * @param valueSerializer  The serializer for value that implements {@link Serializer}. The configure() method won't\n325:      *                         be called in the producer when the serializer is passed in directly.\n326:      */\n327:     public KafkaProducer(Properties properties, Serializer<K> keySerializer, Serializer<V> valueSerializer) {\n328:         this(Utils.propsToMap(properties), keySerializer, valueSerializer);\n329:     }\n330: \n331:     // visible for testing\n332:     @SuppressWarnings({\"unchecked\", \"this-escape\"})\n333:     KafkaProducer(ProducerConfig config,\n334:                   Serializer<K> keySerializer,\n335:                   Serializer<V> valueSerializer,\n336:                   ProducerMetadata metadata,\n337:                   KafkaClient kafkaClient,\n338:                   ProducerInterceptors<K, V> interceptors,\n339:                   ApiVersions apiVersions,\n340:                   Time time) {\n341:         try {\n342:             this.producerConfig = config;\n343:             this.time = time;\n344: \n345:             String transactionalId = config.getString(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTIONAL_ID_CONFIG);\n346: \n347:             this.clientId = config.getString(ProducerConfig.CLIENT_ID_CONFIG);\n348: \n349:             LogContext logContext;\n350:             if (transactionalId == null)\n351:                 logContext = new LogContext(String.format(\"[Producer clientId=%s] \", clientId));\n352:             else\n353:                 logContext = new LogContext(String.format(\"[Producer clientId=%s, transactionalId=%s] \", clientId, transactionalId));\n354:             log = logContext.logger(KafkaProducer.class);\n355:             log.trace(\"Starting the Kafka producer\");\n356: \n357:             Map<String, String> metricTags = Collections.singletonMap(\"client-id\", clientId);\n358:             MetricConfig metricConfig = new MetricConfig().samples(config.getInt(ProducerConfig.METRICS_NUM_SAMPLES_CONFIG))\n359:                     .timeWindow(config.getLong(ProducerConfig.METRICS_SAMPLE_WINDOW_MS_CONFIG), TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\n360:                     .recordLevel(Sensor.RecordingLevel.forName(config.getString(ProducerConfig.METRICS_RECORDING_LEVEL_CONFIG)))\n361:                     .tags(metricTags);\n362:             List<MetricsReporter> reporters = CommonClientConfigs.metricsReporters(clientId, config);\n363:             this.clientTelemetryReporter = CommonClientConfigs.telemetryReporter(clientId, config);\n364:             this.clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(reporters::add);\n365:             MetricsContext metricsContext = new KafkaMetricsContext(JMX_PREFIX,\n366:                     config.originalsWithPrefix(CommonClientConfigs.METRICS_CONTEXT_PREFIX));\n367:             this.metrics = new Metrics(metricConfig, reporters, time, metricsContext);\n368:             this.producerMetrics = new KafkaProducerMetrics(metrics);\n369:             this.partitionerPlugin = Plugin.wrapInstance(\n370:                     config.getConfiguredInstance(\n371:                         ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_CLASS_CONFIG,\n372:                         Partitioner.class,\n373:                         Collections.singletonMap(ProducerConfig.CLIENT_ID_CONFIG, clientId)),\n374:                     metrics,\n375:                     ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_CLASS_CONFIG);\n376:             this.partitionerIgnoreKeys = config.getBoolean(ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_IGNORE_KEYS_CONFIG);\n377:             long retryBackoffMs = config.getLong(ProducerConfig.RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG);\n378:             long retryBackoffMaxMs = config.getLong(ProducerConfig.RETRY_BACKOFF_MAX_MS_CONFIG);\n379:             if (keySerializer == null) {\n380:                 keySerializer = config.getConfiguredInstance(ProducerConfig.KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, Serializer.class);\n381:                 keySerializer.configure(config.originals(Collections.singletonMap(ProducerConfig.CLIENT_ID_CONFIG, clientId)), true);\n382:             } else {\n383:                 config.ignore(ProducerConfig.KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG);\n384:             }\n385:             this.keySerializerPlugin = Plugin.wrapInstance(keySerializer, metrics, ProducerConfig.KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG);\n386: \n387:             if (valueSerializer == null) {\n388:                 valueSerializer = config.getConfiguredInstance(ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG, Serializer.class);\n389:                 valueSerializer.configure(config.originals(Collections.singletonMap(ProducerConfig.CLIENT_ID_CONFIG, clientId)), false);\n390:             } else {\n391:                 config.ignore(ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG);\n392:             }\n393:             this.valueSerializerPlugin = Plugin.wrapInstance(valueSerializer, metrics, ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG);\n394: \n395: \n396:             List<ProducerInterceptor<K, V>> interceptorList = (List<ProducerInterceptor<K, V>>) ClientUtils.configuredInterceptors(config,\n397:                     ProducerConfig.INTERCEPTOR_CLASSES_CONFIG,\n398:                     ProducerInterceptor.class);\n399:             if (interceptors != null)\n400:                 this.interceptors = interceptors;\n401:             else\n402:                 this.interceptors = new ProducerInterceptors<>(interceptorList, metrics);\n403:             ClusterResourceListeners clusterResourceListeners = ClientUtils.configureClusterResourceListeners(\n404:                     interceptorList,\n405:                     reporters,\n406:                     Arrays.asList(this.keySerializerPlugin.get(), this.valueSerializerPlugin.get()));\n407:             this.maxRequestSize = config.getInt(ProducerConfig.MAX_REQUEST_SIZE_CONFIG);\n408:             this.totalMemorySize = config.getLong(ProducerConfig.BUFFER_MEMORY_CONFIG);\n409:             this.compression = configureCompression(config);\n410: \n411:             this.maxBlockTimeMs = config.getLong(ProducerConfig.MAX_BLOCK_MS_CONFIG);\n412:             int deliveryTimeoutMs = configureDeliveryTimeout(config, log);\n413: \n414:             this.apiVersions = apiVersions;\n415:             this.transactionManager = configureTransactionState(config, logContext);\n416:             // There is no need to do work required for adaptive partitioning, if we use a custom partitioner.\n417:             boolean enableAdaptivePartitioning = partitionerPlugin.get() == null &&\n418:                 config.getBoolean(ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_ADPATIVE_PARTITIONING_ENABLE_CONFIG);\n419:             RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig partitionerConfig = new RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig(\n420:                 enableAdaptivePartitioning,\n421:                 config.getLong(ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_AVAILABILITY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n422:             );\n423:             // As per Kafka producer configuration documentation batch.size may be set to 0 to explicitly disable\n424:             // batching which in practice actually means using a batch size of 1.\n425:             int batchSize = Math.max(1, config.getInt(ProducerConfig.BATCH_SIZE_CONFIG));\n426:             this.accumulator = new RecordAccumulator(logContext,\n427:                     batchSize,\n428:                     compression,\n429:                     lingerMs(config),\n430:                     retryBackoffMs,\n431:                     retryBackoffMaxMs,\n432:                     deliveryTimeoutMs,\n433:                     partitionerConfig,\n434:                     metrics,\n435:                     PRODUCER_METRIC_GROUP_NAME,\n436:                     time,\n437:                     transactionManager,\n438:                     new BufferPool(this.totalMemorySize, batchSize, metrics, time, PRODUCER_METRIC_GROUP_NAME));\n439: \n440:             List<InetSocketAddress> addresses = ClientUtils.parseAndValidateAddresses(config);\n441:             if (metadata != null) {\n442:                 this.metadata = metadata;\n443:             } else {\n444:                 this.metadata = new ProducerMetadata(retryBackoffMs,\n445:                         retryBackoffMaxMs,\n446:                         config.getLong(ProducerConfig.METADATA_MAX_AGE_CONFIG),\n447:                         config.getLong(ProducerConfig.METADATA_MAX_IDLE_CONFIG),\n448:                         logContext,\n449:                         clusterResourceListeners,\n450:                         Time.SYSTEM);\n451:                 this.metadata.bootstrap(addresses);\n452:             }\n453:             this.errors = this.metrics.sensor(\"errors\");\n454:             this.sender = newSender(logContext, kafkaClient, this.metadata);\n455:             String ioThreadName = NETWORK_THREAD_PREFIX + \" | \" + clientId;\n456:             this.ioThread = new Sender.SenderThread(ioThreadName, this.sender, true);\n457:             this.ioThread.start();\n458:             config.logUnused();\n459:             AppInfoParser.registerAppInfo(JMX_PREFIX, clientId, metrics, time.milliseconds());\n460:             log.debug(\"Kafka producer started\");\n461:         } catch (Throwable t) {\n462:             // call close methods if internal objects are already constructed this is to prevent resource leak. see KAFKA-2121\n463:             close(Duration.ofMillis(0), true);\n464:             // now propagate the exception\n465:             throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to construct kafka producer\", t);\n466:         }\n467:     }\n468: \n469:     // visible for testing\n470:     KafkaProducer(ProducerConfig config,\n471:                   LogContext logContext,\n472:                   Metrics metrics,\n473:                   Serializer<K> keySerializer,\n474:                   Serializer<V> valueSerializer,\n475:                   ProducerMetadata metadata,\n476:                   RecordAccumulator accumulator,\n477:                   TransactionManager transactionManager,\n478:                   Sender sender,\n479:                   ProducerInterceptors<K, V> interceptors,\n480:                   Partitioner partitioner,\n481:                   Time time,\n482:                   Sender.SenderThread ioThread,\n483:                   Optional<ClientTelemetryReporter> clientTelemetryReporter) {\n484:         this.producerConfig = config;\n485:         this.time = time;\n486:         this.clientId = config.getString(ProducerConfig.CLIENT_ID_CONFIG);\n487:         this.log = logContext.logger(KafkaProducer.class);\n488:         this.metrics = metrics;\n489:         this.producerMetrics = new KafkaProducerMetrics(metrics);\n490:         this.partitionerPlugin = Plugin.wrapInstance(partitioner, metrics, ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_CLASS_CONFIG);\n491:         this.keySerializerPlugin = Plugin.wrapInstance(keySerializer, metrics, ProducerConfig.KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG);\n492:         this.valueSerializerPlugin = Plugin.wrapInstance(valueSerializer, metrics, ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG);\n493:         this.interceptors = interceptors;\n494:         this.maxRequestSize = config.getInt(ProducerConfig.MAX_REQUEST_SIZE_CONFIG);\n495:         this.totalMemorySize = config.getLong(ProducerConfig.BUFFER_MEMORY_CONFIG);\n496:         this.compression = configureCompression(config);\n497:         this.maxBlockTimeMs = config.getLong(ProducerConfig.MAX_BLOCK_MS_CONFIG);\n498:         this.partitionerIgnoreKeys = config.getBoolean(ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_IGNORE_KEYS_CONFIG);\n499:         this.apiVersions = new ApiVersions();\n500:         this.transactionManager = transactionManager;\n501:         this.accumulator = accumulator;\n502:         this.errors = this.metrics.sensor(\"errors\");\n503:         this.metadata = metadata;\n504:         this.sender = sender;\n505:         this.ioThread = ioThread;\n506:         this.clientTelemetryReporter = clientTelemetryReporter;\n507:     }\n508: \n509:     // visible for testing\n510:     Sender newSender(LogContext logContext, KafkaClient kafkaClient, ProducerMetadata metadata) {\n511:         int maxInflightRequests = producerConfig.getInt(ProducerConfig.MAX_IN_FLIGHT_REQUESTS_PER_CONNECTION);\n512:         int requestTimeoutMs = producerConfig.getInt(ProducerConfig.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG);\n513:         ProducerMetrics metricsRegistry = new ProducerMetrics(this.metrics);\n514:         Sensor throttleTimeSensor = Sender.throttleTimeSensor(metricsRegistry.senderMetrics);\n515:         KafkaClient client = kafkaClient != null ? kafkaClient : ClientUtils.createNetworkClient(producerConfig,\n516:                 this.metrics,\n517:                 \"producer\",\n518:                 logContext,\n519:                 apiVersions,\n520:                 time,\n521:                 maxInflightRequests,\n522:                 metadata,\n523:                 throttleTimeSensor,\n524:                 clientTelemetryReporter.map(ClientTelemetryReporter::telemetrySender).orElse(null));\n525: \n526:         short acks = Short.parseShort(producerConfig.getString(ProducerConfig.ACKS_CONFIG));\n527:         return new Sender(logContext,\n528:                 client,\n529:                 metadata,\n530:                 this.accumulator,\n531:                 maxInflightRequests == 1,\n532:                 producerConfig.getInt(ProducerConfig.MAX_REQUEST_SIZE_CONFIG),\n533:                 acks,\n534:                 producerConfig.getInt(ProducerConfig.RETRIES_CONFIG),\n535:                 metricsRegistry.senderMetrics,\n536:                 time,\n537:                 requestTimeoutMs,\n538:                 producerConfig.getLong(ProducerConfig.RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG),\n539:                 this.transactionManager);\n540:     }\n541: \n542:     private static Compression configureCompression(ProducerConfig config) {\n543:         CompressionType type = CompressionType.forName(config.getString(ProducerConfig.COMPRESSION_TYPE_CONFIG));\n544:         switch (type) {\n545:             case GZIP: {\n546:                 return Compression.gzip()\n547:                         .level(config.getInt(ProducerConfig.COMPRESSION_GZIP_LEVEL_CONFIG))\n548:                         .build();\n549:             }\n550:             case LZ4: {\n551:                 return Compression.lz4()\n552:                         .level(config.getInt(ProducerConfig.COMPRESSION_LZ4_LEVEL_CONFIG))\n553:                         .build();\n554:             }\n555:             case ZSTD: {\n556:                 return Compression.zstd()\n557:                         .level(config.getInt(ProducerConfig.COMPRESSION_ZSTD_LEVEL_CONFIG))\n558:                         .build();\n559:             }\n560:             default:\n561:                 return Compression.of(type).build();\n562:         }\n563:     }\n564: \n565:     private static int lingerMs(ProducerConfig config) {\n566:         return (int) Math.min(config.getLong(ProducerConfig.LINGER_MS_CONFIG), Integer.MAX_VALUE);\n567:     }\n568: \n569:     private static int configureDeliveryTimeout(ProducerConfig config, Logger log) {\n570:         int deliveryTimeoutMs = config.getInt(ProducerConfig.DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG);\n571:         int lingerMs = lingerMs(config);\n572:         int requestTimeoutMs = config.getInt(ProducerConfig.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG);\n573:         int lingerAndRequestTimeoutMs = (int) Math.min((long) lingerMs + requestTimeoutMs, Integer.MAX_VALUE);\n574: \n575:         if (deliveryTimeoutMs < lingerAndRequestTimeoutMs) {\n576:             if (config.originals().containsKey(ProducerConfig.DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)) {\n577:                 // throw an exception if the user explicitly set an inconsistent value\n578:                 throw new ConfigException(ProducerConfig.DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG\n579:                     + \" should be equal to or larger than \" + ProducerConfig.LINGER_MS_CONFIG\n580:                     + \" + \" + ProducerConfig.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG);\n581:             } else {\n582:                 // override deliveryTimeoutMs default value to lingerMs + requestTimeoutMs for backward compatibility\n583:                 deliveryTimeoutMs = lingerAndRequestTimeoutMs;\n584:                 log.warn(\"{} should be equal to or larger than {} + {}. Setting it to {}.\",\n585:                     ProducerConfig.DELIVERY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG, ProducerConfig.LINGER_MS_CONFIG,\n586:                     ProducerConfig.REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG, deliveryTimeoutMs);\n587:             }\n588:         }\n589:         return deliveryTimeoutMs;\n590:     }\n591: \n592:     private TransactionManager configureTransactionState(ProducerConfig config,\n593:                                                          LogContext logContext) {\n594:         TransactionManager transactionManager = null;\n595: \n596:         if (config.getBoolean(ProducerConfig.ENABLE_IDEMPOTENCE_CONFIG)) {\n597:             final String transactionalId = config.getString(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTIONAL_ID_CONFIG);\n598:             final boolean enable2PC = config.getBoolean(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTION_TWO_PHASE_COMMIT_ENABLE_CONFIG);\n599:             final int transactionTimeoutMs = config.getInt(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTION_TIMEOUT_CONFIG);\n600:             final long retryBackoffMs = config.getLong(ProducerConfig.RETRY_BACKOFF_MS_CONFIG);\n601:             \n602:             transactionManager = new TransactionManager(\n603:                 logContext,\n604:                 transactionalId,\n605:                 transactionTimeoutMs,\n606:                 retryBackoffMs,\n607:                 apiVersions,\n608:                 enable2PC\n609:             );\n610: \n611:             if (transactionManager.isTransactional())\n612:                 log.info(\"Instantiated a transactional producer.\");\n613:             else\n614:                 log.info(\"Instantiated an idempotent producer.\");\n615:         } else {\n616:             // ignore unretrieved configurations related to producer transaction\n617:             config.ignore(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTION_TIMEOUT_CONFIG);\n618:         }\n619:         return transactionManager;\n620:     }\n621: \n622:     /**\n623:      * Needs to be called before any other methods when the {@code transactional.id} is set in the configuration.\n624:      * This method does the following:\n625:      * <ol>\n626:      * <li>Ensures any transactions initiated by previous instances of the producer with the same\n627:      *      {@code transactional.id} are completed. If the previous instance had failed with a transaction in\n628:      *      progress, it will be aborted. If the last transaction had begun completion,\n629:      *      but not yet finished, this method awaits its completion.</li>\n630:      * <li>Gets the internal producer id and epoch, used in all future transactional\n631:      *      messages issued by the producer.</li>\n632:      * </ol>\n633:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transactional state cannot\n634:      * be initialized before expiration of {@code max.block.ms}. Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException}\n635:      * if interrupted. It is safe to retry in either case, but once the transactional state has been successfully\n636:      * initialized, this method should no longer be used.\n637:      *\n638:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no {@code transactional.id} has been configured\n639:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n640:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n641:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException error indicating that the configured\n642:      *         transactional.id is not authorized, or the idempotent producer id is unavailable. See the exception for\n643:      *         more details.  User may retry this function call after fixing the permission.\n644:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal error or for any other unexpected error\n645:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for initialize the transaction has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n646:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n647:      */\n648:     public void initTransactions() {\n649:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n650:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n651:         long now = time.nanoseconds();\n652:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.initializeTransactions(false);\n653:         sender.wakeup();\n654:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n655:         producerMetrics.recordInit(time.nanoseconds() - now);\n656:         transactionManager.maybeUpdateTransactionV2Enabled(true);\n657:     }\n658: \n659:     /**\n660:      * Should be called before the start of each new transaction. Note that prior to the first invocation\n661:      * of this method, you must invoke {@link #initTransactions()} exactly one time.\n662:      *\n663:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no {@code transactional.id} has been configured or if {@link #initTransactions()}\n664:      *         has not yet been invoked\n665:      * @throws ProducerFencedException if another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n666:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n667:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n668:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n669:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n670:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n671:      *         {@code transactional.id} is not authorized. See the exception for more details\n672:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal error or for any other unexpected error\n673:      */\n674:     public void beginTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\n675:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n676:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n677:         throwIfInPreparedState();\n678:         long now = time.nanoseconds();\n679:         transactionManager.beginTransaction();\n680:         producerMetrics.recordBeginTxn(time.nanoseconds() - now);\n681:     }\n682: \n683:     /**\n684:      * Sends a list of specified offsets to the consumer group coordinator, and also marks\n685:      * those offsets as part of the current transaction. These offsets will be considered\n686:      * committed only if the transaction is committed successfully. The committed offset should\n687:      * be the next message your application will consume, i.e. {@code nextRecordToBeProcessed.offset()}\n688:      * (or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}). You should also add the leader epoch as commit metadata,\n689:      * which can be obtained from {@link ConsumerRecord#leaderEpoch()} or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}.\n690:      * <p>\n691:      * This method should be used when you need to batch consumed and produced messages\n692:      * together, typically in a consume-transform-produce pattern. Thus, the specified\n693:      * {@code groupMetadata} should be extracted from the used {@link KafkaConsumer consumer} via\n694:      * {@link KafkaConsumer#groupMetadata()} to leverage consumer group metadata. This will provide\n695:      * stronger fencing than just supplying the {@code consumerGroupId} and passing in {@code new ConsumerGroupMetadata(consumerGroupId)},\n696:      * however note that the full set of consumer group metadata returned by {@link KafkaConsumer#groupMetadata()}\n697:      * requires the brokers to be on version 2.5 or newer to understand.\n698:      *\n699:      * <p>\n700:      * This method is a blocking call that waits until the request has been received and acknowledged by the consumer group\n701:      * coordinator; but the offsets are not considered as committed until the transaction itself is successfully committed later (via\n702:      * the {@link #commitTransaction()} call).\n703:      *\n704:      * <p>\n705:      * Note, that the consumer should have {@code enable.auto.commit=false} and should\n706:      * also not commit offsets manually (via {@link KafkaConsumer#commitSync(Map) sync} or\n707:      * {@link KafkaConsumer#commitAsync(Map, OffsetCommitCallback) async} commits).\n708:      * This method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the producer cannot send offsets before expiration of {@code max.block.ms}.\n709:      * Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\n710:      *\n711:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started.\n712:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n713:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n714:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0) or\n715:      *         the broker doesn't support the latest version of transactional API with all consumer group metadata\n716:      *         (i.e. if its version is lower than 2.5.0).\n717:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedForMessageFormatException fatal error indicating the message\n718:      *         format used for the offsets topic on the broker does not support transactions\n719:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n720:      *         transactional.id is not authorized, or the consumer group id is not authorized.\n721:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException if the commit failed and cannot be retried\n722:      *         (e.g. if the consumer has been kicked out of the group). Users should handle this by aborting the transaction.\n723:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this producer instance gets fenced by broker due to a\n724:      *                                                                  mis-configured consumer instance id within group metadata.\n725:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n726:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n727:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal or abortable error, or for any\n728:      *         other unexpected error\n729:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for sending the offsets has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n730:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n731:      */\n732:     public void sendOffsetsToTransaction(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets,\n733:                                          ConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata) throws ProducerFencedException {\n734:         throwIfInvalidGroupMetadata(groupMetadata);\n735:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n736:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n737: \n738:         if (!offsets.isEmpty()) {\n739:             long start = time.nanoseconds();\n740:             TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.sendOffsetsToTransaction(offsets, groupMetadata);\n741:             sender.wakeup();\n742:             result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n743:             producerMetrics.recordSendOffsets(time.nanoseconds() - start);\n744:         }\n745:     }\n746: \n747:     /**\n748:      * Commits the ongoing transaction. This method will flush any unsent records before actually committing the transaction.\n749:      * <p>\n750:      * Further, if any of the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls which were part of the transaction hit irrecoverable\n751:      * errors, this method will throw the last received exception immediately and the transaction will not be committed.\n752:      * So all {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls in a transaction must succeed in order for this method to succeed.\n753:      * <p>\n754:      * If the transaction is committed successfully and this method returns without throwing an exception, it is guaranteed\n755:      * that all {@link Callback callbacks} for records in the transaction will have been invoked and completed.\n756:      * Note that exceptions thrown by callbacks are ignored; the producer proceeds to commit the transaction in any case.\n757:      * <p>\n758:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transaction cannot be committed before expiration\n759:      * of {@code max.block.ms}, but this does not mean the request did not actually reach the broker. In fact, it only indicates\n760:      * that we cannot get the acknowledgement response in time, so it's up to the application's logic\n761:      * to decide how to handle timeouts.\n762:      * Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\n763:      * It is safe to retry in either case, but it is not possible to attempt a different operation (such as abortTransaction)\n764:      * since the commit may already be in the progress of completing. If not retrying, the only option is to close the producer.\n765:      *\n766:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started\n767:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n768:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n769:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n770:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n771:      *         transactional.id is not authorized. See the exception for more details\n772:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n773:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n774:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal or abortable error, or for any\n775:      *         other unexpected error\n776:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for committing the transaction has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n777:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n778:      */\n779:     public void commitTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\n780:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n781:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n782:         long commitStart = time.nanoseconds();\n783:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.beginCommit();\n784:         sender.wakeup();\n785:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n786:         producerMetrics.recordCommitTxn(time.nanoseconds() - commitStart);\n787:     }\n788: \n789:     /**\n790:      * Aborts the ongoing transaction. Any unflushed produce messages will be aborted when this call is made.\n791:      * This call will throw an exception immediately if any prior {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls failed with a\n792:      * {@link ProducerFencedException} or an instance of {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException}.\n793:      * <p>\n794:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transaction cannot be aborted before expiration\n795:      * of {@code max.block.ms}, but this does not mean the request did not actually reach the broker. In fact, it only indicates\n796:      * that we cannot get the acknowledgement response in time, so it's up to the application's logic\n797:      * to decide how to handle timeouts. Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\n798:      * It is safe to retry in either case, but it is not possible to attempt a different operation (such as {@link #commitTransaction})\n799:      * since the abort may already be in the progress of completing. If not retrying, the only option is to close the producer.\n800:      *\n801:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started\n802:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n803:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n804:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n805:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n806:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n807:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n808:      *         transactional.id is not authorized. See the exception for more details\n809:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal error or for any other unexpected error\n810:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for aborting the transaction has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n811:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n812:      */\n813:     public void abortTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\n814:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n815:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n816:         log.info(\"Aborting incomplete transaction\");\n817:         long abortStart = time.nanoseconds();\n818:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.beginAbort();\n819:         sender.wakeup();\n820:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n821:         producerMetrics.recordAbortTxn(time.nanoseconds() - abortStart);\n822:     }\n823: \n824:     /**\n825:      * Asynchronously send a record to a topic. Equivalent to <code>send(record, null)</code>.\n826:      * See {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)} for details.\n827:      */\n828:     @Override\n829:     public Future<RecordMetadata> send(ProducerRecord<K, V> record) {\n830:         return send(record, null);\n831:     }\n832: \n833:     /**\n834:      * Asynchronously send a record to a topic and invoke the provided callback when the send has been acknowledged.\n835:      * <p>\n836:      * The send is asynchronous and this method will return immediately (except for rare cases described below)\n837:      * once the record has been stored in the buffer of records waiting to be sent.\n838:      * This allows sending many records in parallel without blocking to wait for the response after each one.\n839:      * Can block for the following cases: 1) For the first record being sent to \n840:      * the cluster by this client for the given topic. In this case it will block for up to {@code max.block.ms} milliseconds if \n841:      * Kafka cluster is unreachable; 2) Allocating a buffer if buffer pool doesn't have any free buffers.\n842:      * <p>\n843:      * The result of the send is a {@link RecordMetadata} specifying the partition the record was sent to, the offset\n844:      * it was assigned and the timestamp of the record. If the producer is configured with acks = 0, the {@link RecordMetadata}\n845:      * will have offset = -1 because the producer does not wait for the acknowledgement from the broker.\n846:      * If {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#CREATE_TIME CreateTime} is used by the topic, the timestamp\n847:      * will be the user provided timestamp or the record send time if the user did not specify a timestamp for the\n848:      * record. If {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#LOG_APPEND_TIME LogAppendTime} is used for the\n849:      * topic, the timestamp will be the Kafka broker local time when the message is appended.\n850:      * <p>\n851:      * Since the send call is asynchronous it returns a {@link java.util.concurrent.Future Future} for the\n852:      * {@link RecordMetadata} that will be assigned to this record. Invoking {@link java.util.concurrent.Future#get()\n853:      * get()} on this future will block until the associated request completes and then return the metadata for the record\n854:      * or throw any exception that occurred while sending the record.\n855:      * <p>\n856:      * If you want to simulate a simple blocking call you can call the <code>get()</code> method immediately:\n857:      *\n858:      * <pre>\n859:      * {@code\n860:      * byte[] key = \"key\".getBytes();\n861:      * byte[] value = \"value\".getBytes();\n862:      * ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]> record = new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(\"my-topic\", key, value)\n863:      * producer.send(record).get();\n864:      * }</pre>\n865:      * <p>\n866:      * Fully non-blocking usage can make use of the {@link Callback} parameter to provide a callback that\n867:      * will be invoked when the request is complete.\n868:      *\n869:      * <pre>\n870:      * {@code\n871:      * ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]> record = new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(\"the-topic\", key, value);\n872:      * producer.send(myRecord,\n873:      *               new Callback() {\n874:      *                   public void onCompletion(RecordMetadata metadata, Exception e) {\n875:      *                       if(e != null) {\n876:      *                          e.printStackTrace();\n877:      *                       } else {\n878:      *                          System.out.println(\"The offset of the record we just sent is: \" + metadata.offset());\n879:      *                       }\n880:      *                   }\n881:      *               });\n882:      * }\n883:      * </pre>\n884:      *\n885:      * Callbacks for records being sent to the same partition are guaranteed to execute in order. That is, in the\n886:      * following example <code>callback1</code> is guaranteed to execute before <code>callback2</code>:\n887:      *\n888:      * <pre>\n889:      * {@code\n890:      * producer.send(new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(topic, partition, key1, value1), callback1);\n891:      * producer.send(new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(topic, partition, key2, value2), callback2);\n892:      * }\n893:      * </pre>\n894:      * <p>\n895:      * When used as part of a transaction, it is not necessary to define a callback or check the result of the future\n896:      * in order to detect errors from <code>send</code>. If any of the send calls failed with an irrecoverable error,\n897:      * the final {@link #commitTransaction()} call will fail and throw the exception from the last failed send. When\n898:      * this happens, your application should call {@link #abortTransaction()} to reset the state and continue to send\n899:      * data.\n900:      * </p>\n901:      * <p>\n902:      * Some transactional send errors cannot be resolved with a call to {@link #abortTransaction()}.  In particular,\n903:      * if a transactional send finishes with a {@link ProducerFencedException}, a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.OutOfOrderSequenceException},\n904:      * a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException}, or an\n905:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException}, then the only option left is to call {@link #close()}.\n906:      * Fatal errors cause the producer to enter a defunct state in which future API calls will continue to raise\n907:      * the same underlying error wrapped in a new {@link KafkaException}.\n908:      * </p>\n909:      * <p>\n910:      * It is a similar picture when idempotence is enabled, but no <code>transactional.id</code> has been configured.\n911:      * In this case, {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException} and\n912:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException} are considered fatal errors. However,\n913:      * {@link ProducerFencedException} does not need to be handled. Additionally, it is possible to continue\n914:      * sending after receiving an {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.OutOfOrderSequenceException}, but doing so\n915:      * can result in out of order delivery of pending messages. To ensure proper ordering, you should close the\n916:      * producer and create a new instance.\n917:      * </p>\n918:      * <p>\n919:      * If the message format of the destination topic is not upgraded to 0.11.0.0, idempotent and transactional\n920:      * produce requests will fail with an {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedForMessageFormatException}\n921:      * error. If this is encountered during a transaction, it is possible to abort and continue. But note that future\n922:      * sends to the same topic will continue receiving the same exception until the topic is upgraded.\n923:      * </p>\n924:      * <p>\n925:      * Note that callbacks will generally execute in the I/O thread of the producer and so should be reasonably fast or\n926:      * they will delay the sending of messages from other threads. If you want to execute blocking or computationally\n927:      * expensive callbacks it is recommended to use your own {@link java.util.concurrent.Executor} in the callback body\n928:      * to parallelize processing.\n929:      *\n930:      * @param record The record to send\n931:      * @param callback A user-supplied callback to execute when the record has been acknowledged by the server (null\n932:      *        indicates no callback)\n933:      *\n934:      * @throws IllegalStateException if a transactional.id has been configured and no transaction has been started, or\n935:      *                               when send is invoked after producer has been closed.\n936:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked\n937:      * @throws SerializationException If the key or value are not valid objects given the configured serializers\n938:      * @throws KafkaException If a Kafka related error occurs that does not belong to the public API exceptions.\n939:      */\n940:     @Override\n941:     public Future<RecordMetadata> send(ProducerRecord<K, V> record, Callback callback) {\n942:         // intercept the record, which can be potentially modified; this method does not throw exceptions\n943:         ProducerRecord<K, V> interceptedRecord = this.interceptors.onSend(record);\n944:         return doSend(interceptedRecord, callback);\n945:     }\n946: \n947:     // Verify that this producer instance has not been closed. This method throws IllegalStateException if the producer\n948:     // has already been closed.\n949:     private void throwIfProducerClosed() {\n950:         if (sender == null || !sender.isRunning())\n951:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Cannot perform operation after producer has been closed\");\n952:     }\n953: \n954:     /**\n955:      * Throws an exception if the transaction is in a prepared state.\n956:      * In a two-phase commit (2PC) flow, once a transaction enters the prepared state,\n957:      * only commit, abort, or complete operations are allowed.\n958:      *\n959:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any other operation is attempted in the prepared state.\n960:      */\n961:     private void throwIfInPreparedState() {\n962:         if (transactionManager != null &&\n963:             transactionManager.isTransactional() &&\n964:             transactionManager.isPrepared()\n965:         ) {\n966:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Cannot perform operation while the transaction is in a prepared state. \" +\n967:                 \"Only commitTransaction(), abortTransaction(), or completeTransaction() are permitted.\");\n968:         }\n969:     }\n970: \n971:     /**\n972:      * Implementation of asynchronously send a record to a topic.\n973:      */\n974:     private Future<RecordMetadata> doSend(ProducerRecord<K, V> record, Callback callback) {\n975:         // Append callback takes care of the following:\n976:         //  - call interceptors and user callback on completion\n977:         //  - remember partition that is calculated in RecordAccumulator.append\n978:         AppendCallbacks appendCallbacks = new AppendCallbacks(callback, this.interceptors, record);\n979: \n980:         try {\n981:             throwIfProducerClosed();\n982:             throwIfInPreparedState();\n983: \n984:             // first make sure the metadata for the topic is available\n985:             long nowMs = time.milliseconds();\n986:             ClusterAndWaitTime clusterAndWaitTime;\n987:             try {\n988:                 clusterAndWaitTime = waitOnMetadata(record.topic(), record.partition(), nowMs, maxBlockTimeMs);\n989:             } catch (KafkaException e) {\n990:                 if (metadata.isClosed())\n991:                     throw new KafkaException(\"Producer closed while send in progress\", e);\n992:                 throw e;\n993:             }\n994:             nowMs += clusterAndWaitTime.waitedOnMetadataMs;\n995:             long remainingWaitMs = Math.max(0, maxBlockTimeMs - clusterAndWaitTime.waitedOnMetadataMs);\n996:             Cluster cluster = clusterAndWaitTime.cluster;\n997:             byte[] serializedKey;\n998:             try {\n999:                 serializedKey = keySerializerPlugin.get().serialize(record.topic(), record.headers(), record.key());\n1000:             } catch (ClassCastException cce) {\n1001:                 throw new SerializationException(\"Can't convert key of class \" + record.key().getClass().getName() +\n1002:                         \" to class \" + producerConfig.getClass(ProducerConfig.KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG).getName() +\n1003:                         \" specified in key.serializer\", cce);\n1004:             }\n1005:             byte[] serializedValue;\n1006:             try {\n1007:                 serializedValue = valueSerializerPlugin.get().serialize(record.topic(), record.headers(), record.value());\n1008:             } catch (ClassCastException cce) {\n1009:                 throw new SerializationException(\"Can't convert value of class \" + record.value().getClass().getName() +\n1010:                         \" to class \" + producerConfig.getClass(ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG).getName() +\n1011:                         \" specified in value.serializer\", cce);\n1012:             }\n1013: \n1014:             // Try to calculate partition, but note that after this call it can be RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION,\n1015:             // which means that the RecordAccumulator would pick a partition using built-in logic (which may\n1016:             // take into account broker load, the amount of data produced to each partition, etc.).\n1017:             int partition = partition(record, serializedKey, serializedValue, cluster);\n1018: \n1019:             setReadOnly(record.headers());\n1020:             Header[] headers = record.headers().toArray();\n1021: \n1022:             int serializedSize = AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytesUpperBound(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n1023:                     compression.type(), serializedKey, serializedValue, headers);\n1024:             ensureValidRecordSize(serializedSize);\n1025:             long timestamp = record.timestamp() == null ? nowMs : record.timestamp();\n1026: \n1027:             // Append the record to the accumulator.  Note, that the actual partition may be\n1028:             // calculated there and can be accessed via appendCallbacks.topicPartition.\n1029:             RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult result = accumulator.append(record.topic(), partition, timestamp, serializedKey,\n1030:                     serializedValue, headers, appendCallbacks, remainingWaitMs, nowMs, cluster);\n1031:             assert appendCallbacks.getPartition() != RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION;\n1032: \n1033:             // Add the partition to the transaction (if in progress) after it has been successfully\n1034:             // appended to the accumulator. We cannot do it before because the partition may be\n1035:             // unknown. Note that the `Sender` will refuse to dequeue\n1036:             // batches from the accumulator until they have been added to the transaction.\n1037:             if (transactionManager != null) {\n1038:                 transactionManager.maybeAddPartition(appendCallbacks.topicPartition());\n1039:             }\n1040: \n1041:             if (result.batchIsFull || result.newBatchCreated) {\n1042:                 log.trace(\"Waking up the sender since topic {} partition {} is either full or getting a new batch\", record.topic(), appendCallbacks.getPartition());\n1043:                 this.sender.wakeup();\n1044:             }\n1045:             return result.future;\n1046:             // handling exceptions and record the errors;\n1047:             // for API exceptions return them in the future,\n1048:             // for other exceptions throw directly\n1049:         } catch (ApiException e) {\n1050:             log.debug(\"Exception occurred during message send:\", e);\n1051:             if (callback != null) {\n1052:                 TopicPartition tp = appendCallbacks.topicPartition();\n1053:                 RecordMetadata nullMetadata = new RecordMetadata(tp, -1, -1, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, -1, -1);\n1054:                 callback.onCompletion(nullMetadata, e);\n1055:             }\n1056:             this.errors.record();\n1057:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1058:             if (transactionManager != null) {\n1059:                 transactionManager.maybeTransitionToErrorState(e);\n1060:             }\n1061:             return new FutureFailure(e);\n1062:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n1063:             this.errors.record();\n1064:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1065:             throw new InterruptException(e);\n1066:         } catch (KafkaException e) {\n1067:             this.errors.record();\n1068:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1069:             throw e;\n1070:         } catch (Exception e) {\n1071:             // we notify interceptor about all exceptions, since onSend is called before anything else in this method\n1072:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1073:             throw e;\n1074:         }\n1075:     }\n1076: \n1077:     private void setReadOnly(Headers headers) {\n1078:         if (headers instanceof RecordHeaders) {\n1079:             ((RecordHeaders) headers).setReadOnly();\n1080:         }\n1081:     }\n1082: \n1083:     /**\n1084:      * Wait for cluster metadata including partitions for the given topic to be available.\n1085:      * @param topic The topic we want metadata for\n1086:      * @param partition A specific partition expected to exist in metadata, or null if there's no preference\n1087:      * @param nowMs The current time in ms\n1088:      * @param maxWaitMs The maximum time in ms for waiting on the metadata\n1089:      * @return The cluster containing topic metadata and the amount of time we waited in ms\n1090:      * @throws TimeoutException if metadata could not be refreshed within {@code max.block.ms}\n1091:      * @throws KafkaException for all Kafka-related exceptions, including the case where this method is called after producer close\n1092:      */\n1093:     private ClusterAndWaitTime waitOnMetadata(String topic, Integer partition, long nowMs, long maxWaitMs) throws InterruptedException {\n1094:         Cluster cluster = metadata.fetch();\n1095: \n1096:         if (cluster.invalidTopics().contains(topic))\n1097:             throw new InvalidTopicException(topic);\n1098: \n1099:         // add topic to metadata topic list if it is not there already and reset expiry\n1100:         metadata.add(topic, nowMs);\n1101: \n1102:         Integer partitionsCount = cluster.partitionCountForTopic(topic);\n1103:         // Return cached metadata if we have it, and if the record's partition is either undefined\n1104:         // or within the known partition range\n1105:         if (partitionsCount != null && (partition == null || partition < partitionsCount))\n1106:             return new ClusterAndWaitTime(cluster, 0);\n1107: \n1108:         long remainingWaitMs = maxWaitMs;\n1109:         long elapsed = 0;\n1110:         // Issue metadata requests until we have metadata for the topic and the requested partition,\n1111:         // or until maxWaitTimeMs is exceeded. This is necessary in case the metadata\n1112:         // is stale and the number of partitions for this topic has increased in the meantime.\n1113:         long nowNanos = time.nanoseconds();\n1114:         do {\n1115:             if (partition != null) {\n1116:                 log.trace(\"Requesting metadata update for partition {} of topic {}.\", partition, topic);\n1117:             } else {\n1118:                 log.trace(\"Requesting metadata update for topic {}.\", topic);\n1119:             }\n1120:             metadata.add(topic, nowMs + elapsed);\n1121:             int version = metadata.requestUpdateForTopic(topic);\n1122:             sender.wakeup();\n1123:             try {\n1124:                 metadata.awaitUpdate(version, remainingWaitMs);\n1125:             } catch (TimeoutException ex) {\n1126:                 // Rethrow with original maxWaitMs to prevent logging exception with remainingWaitMs\n1127:                 final String errorMessage = getErrorMessage(partitionsCount, topic, partition, maxWaitMs);\n1128:                 if (metadata.getError(topic) != null) {\n1129:                     throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage, metadata.getError(topic).exception());\n1130:                 }\n1131:                 throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage);\n1132:             }\n1133:             cluster = metadata.fetch();\n1134:             elapsed = time.milliseconds() - nowMs;\n1135:             if (elapsed >= maxWaitMs) {\n1136:                 final String errorMessage = getErrorMessage(partitionsCount, topic, partition, maxWaitMs);\n1137:                 if (metadata.getError(topic) != null && metadata.getError(topic).exception() instanceof RetriableException) {\n1138:                     throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage, metadata.getError(topic).exception());\n1139:                 }\n1140:                 throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage);\n1141:             }\n1142:             metadata.maybeThrowExceptionForTopic(topic);\n1143:             remainingWaitMs = maxWaitMs - elapsed;\n1144:             partitionsCount = cluster.partitionCountForTopic(topic);\n1145:         } while (partitionsCount == null || (partition != null && partition >= partitionsCount));\n1146: \n1147:         producerMetrics.recordMetadataWait(time.nanoseconds() - nowNanos);\n1148: \n1149:         return new ClusterAndWaitTime(cluster, elapsed);\n1150:     }\n1151: \n1152:     private String getErrorMessage(Integer partitionsCount, String topic, Integer partition, long maxWaitMs) {\n1153:         return partitionsCount == null ?\n1154:             String.format(\"Topic %s not present in metadata after %d ms.\",\n1155:                 topic, maxWaitMs) :\n1156:             String.format(\"Partition %d of topic %s with partition count %d is not present in metadata after %d ms.\",\n1157:                 partition, topic, partitionsCount, maxWaitMs);\n1158:     }\n1159:     /**\n1160:      * Validate that the record size isn't too large\n1161:      */\n1162:     private void ensureValidRecordSize(int size) {\n1163:         if (size > maxRequestSize)\n1164:             throw new RecordTooLargeException(\"The message is \" + size +\n1165:                     \" bytes when serialized which is larger than \" + maxRequestSize + \", which is the value of the \" +\n1166:                     ProducerConfig.MAX_REQUEST_SIZE_CONFIG + \" configuration.\");\n1167:         if (size > totalMemorySize)\n1168:             throw new RecordTooLargeException(\"The message is \" + size +\n1169:                     \" bytes when serialized which is larger than the total memory buffer you have configured with the \" +\n1170:                     ProducerConfig.BUFFER_MEMORY_CONFIG +\n1171:                     \" configuration.\");\n1172:     }\n1173: \n1174:     /**\n1175:      * Invoking this method makes all buffered records immediately available to send (even if <code>linger.ms</code> is\n1176:      * greater than 0) and blocks on the completion of the requests associated with these records. The post-condition\n1177:      * of <code>flush()</code> is that any previously sent record will have completed (e.g. <code>Future.isDone() == true</code>\n1178:      * and callbacks passed to {@link #send(ProducerRecord,Callback)} have been called).\n1179:      * A request is considered completed when it is successfully acknowledged\n1180:      * according to the <code>acks</code> configuration you have specified or else it results in an error.\n1181:      * <p>\n1182:      * Other threads can continue sending records while one thread is blocked waiting for a flush call to complete,\n1183:      * however no guarantee is made about the completion of records sent after the flush call begins.\n1184:      * <p>\n1185:      * This method can be useful when consuming from some input system and producing into Kafka. The <code>flush()</code> call\n1186:      * gives a convenient way to ensure all previously sent messages have actually completed.\n1187:      * <p>\n1188:      * This example shows how to consume from one Kafka topic and produce to another Kafka topic:\n1189:      * <pre>\n1190:      * {@code\n1191:      * for(ConsumerRecord<String, String> record: consumer.poll(100))\n1192:      *     producer.send(new ProducerRecord(\"my-topic\", record.key(), record.value());\n1193:      * producer.flush();\n1194:      * consumer.commitSync();\n1195:      * }\n1196:      * </pre>\n1197:      *\n1198:      * Note that the above example may drop records if the produce request fails. If we want to ensure that this does not occur\n1199:      * we need to set <code>retries=&lt;large_number&gt;</code> in our config.\n1200:      * </p>\n1201:      * <p>\n1202:      * Applications don't need to call this method for transactional producers, since the {@link #commitTransaction()} will\n1203:      * flush all buffered records before performing the commit. This ensures that all the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)}\n1204:      * calls made since the previous {@link #beginTransaction()} are completed before the commit.\n1205:      * </p>\n1206:      * <p>\n1207:      * <b>Important:</b> This method must not be called from within the callback provided to\n1208:      * {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)}. Invoking <code>flush()</code> in this context will result in a\n1209:      * {@link KafkaException} being thrown, as it will cause a deadlock.\n1210:      * </p>\n1211:      *\n1212:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked\n1213:      * @throws KafkaException If the method is invoked inside a {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)} callback\n1214:      */\n1215:     @Override\n1216:     public void flush() {\n1217:         if (Thread.currentThread() == this.ioThread) {\n1218:             log.error(\"KafkaProducer.flush() invocation inside a callback is not permitted because it may lead to deadlock.\");\n1219:             throw new KafkaException(\"KafkaProducer.flush() invocation inside a callback is not permitted because it may lead to deadlock.\");\n1220:         }\n1221: \n1222:         log.trace(\"Flushing accumulated records in producer.\");\n1223: \n1224:         long start = time.nanoseconds();\n1225:         this.accumulator.beginFlush();\n1226:         this.sender.wakeup();\n1227:         try {\n1228:             this.accumulator.awaitFlushCompletion();\n1229:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n1230:             throw new InterruptException(\"Flush interrupted.\", e);\n1231:         } finally {\n1232:             producerMetrics.recordFlush(time.nanoseconds() - start);\n1233:         }\n1234:     }\n1235: \n1236:     /**\n1237:      * Get the partition metadata for the given topic. This can be used for custom partitioning.\n1238:      * @throws AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1239:      * @throws AuthorizationException if not authorized to the specified topic. See the exception for more details\n1240:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n1241:      * @throws TimeoutException if metadata could not be refreshed within {@code max.block.ms}\n1242:      * @throws KafkaException for all Kafka-related exceptions, including the case where this method is called after producer close\n1243:      */\n1244:     @Override\n1245:     public List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic) {\n1246:         Objects.requireNonNull(topic, \"topic cannot be null\");\n1247:         try {\n1248:             return waitOnMetadata(topic, null, time.milliseconds(), maxBlockTimeMs).cluster.partitionsForTopic(topic);\n1249:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n1250:             throw new InterruptException(e);\n1251:         }\n1252:     }\n1253: \n1254:     /**\n1255:      * Get the full set of internal metrics maintained by the producer.\n1256:      */\n1257:     @Override\n1258:     public Map<MetricName, ? extends Metric> metrics() {\n1259:         return Collections.unmodifiableMap(this.metrics.metrics());\n1260:     }\n1261: \n1262: \n1263:     /**\n1264:      * Add the provided application metric for subscription.\n1265:      * This metric will be added to this client's metrics\n1266:      * that are available for subscription and sent as\n1267:      * telemetry data to the broker.\n1268:      * The provided metric must map to an OTLP metric data point\n1269:      * type in the OpenTelemetry v1 metrics protobuf message types.\n1270:      * Specifically, the metric should be one of the following:\n1271:      * <ul>\n1272:      *  <li>\n1273:      *     `Sum`: Monotonic total count meter (Counter). Suitable for metrics like total number of X, e.g., total bytes sent.\n1274:      *  </li>\n1275:      *  <li>\n1276:      *     `Gauge`: Non-monotonic current value meter (UpDownCounter). Suitable for metrics like current value of Y, e.g., current queue count.\n1277:      *  </li>\n1278:      * </ul>\n1279:      * Metrics not matching these types are silently ignored.\n1280:      * Executing this method for a previously registered metric is a benign operation and results in updating that metrics entry.\n1281:      *\n1282:      * @param metric The application metric to register\n1283:      */\n1284:     @Override\n1285:     public void registerMetricForSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\n1286:         if (!metrics().containsKey(metric.metricName())) {\n1287:             clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(reporter -> reporter.metricChange(metric));\n1288:         }  else {\n1289:             log.debug(\"Skipping registration for metric {}. Existing producer metrics cannot be overwritten.\", metric.metricName());\n1290:         }\n1291:     }\n1292: \n1293:     /**\n1294:      * Remove the provided application metric for subscription.\n1295:      * This metric is removed from this client's metrics\n1296:      * and will not be available for subscription any longer.\n1297:      * Executing this method with a metric that has not been registered is a\n1298:      * benign operation and does not result in any action taken (no-op).\n1299:      *\n1300:      * @param metric The application metric to remove\n1301:      */\n1302:     @Override\n1303:     public void unregisterMetricFromSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\n1304:         if (!metrics().containsKey(metric.metricName())) {\n1305:             clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(reporter -> reporter.metricRemoval(metric));\n1306:         } else {\n1307:             log.debug(\"Skipping unregistration for metric {}. Existing producer metrics cannot be removed.\", metric.metricName());\n1308:         }\n1309:     }\n1310: \n1311:     /**\n1312:      * Determines the client's unique client instance ID used for telemetry. This ID is unique to\n1313:      * this specific client instance and will not change after it is initially generated.\n1314:      * The ID is useful for correlating client operations with telemetry sent to the broker and\n1315:      * to its eventual monitoring destinations.\n1316:      * <p>\n1317:      * If telemetry is enabled, this will first require a connection to the cluster to generate\n1318:      * the unique client instance ID. This method waits up to {@code timeout} for the producer\n1319:      * client to complete the request.\n1320:      * <p>\n1321:      * Client telemetry is controlled by the {@link ProducerConfig#ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_CONFIG}\n1322:      * configuration option.\n1323:      *\n1324:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for producer client to determine its client instance ID.\n1325:      *                The value must be non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not\n1326:      *                wait for the initial request to complete if it hasn't already.\n1327:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\n1328:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to determine the client\n1329:      *                        instance ID, though this error does not necessarily imply the\n1330:      *                        producer client is otherwise unusable.\n1331:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the {@code timeout} is negative.\n1332:      * @throws IllegalStateException If telemetry is not enabled ie, config `{@code enable.metrics.push}`\n1333:      *                               is set to `{@code false}`.\n1334:      * @return The client's assigned instance id used for metrics collection.\n1335:      */\n1336:     @Override\n1337:     public Uuid clientInstanceId(Duration timeout) {\n1338:         if (clientTelemetryReporter.isEmpty()) {\n1339:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Telemetry is not enabled. Set config `\" + ProducerConfig.ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_CONFIG + \"` to `true`.\");\n1340:         }\n1341: \n1342:         return ClientTelemetryUtils.fetchClientInstanceId(clientTelemetryReporter.get(), timeout);\n1343:     }\n1344: \n1345:     /**\n1346:      * Close this producer. This method blocks until all previously sent requests complete.\n1347:      * This method is equivalent to <code>close(Long.MAX_VALUE, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)</code>.\n1348:      * <p>\n1349:      * <strong>If close() is called from {@link Callback}, a warning message will be logged and close(0, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\n1350:      * will be called instead. We do this because the sender thread would otherwise try to join itself and\n1351:      * block forever.</strong>\n1352:      * <p>\n1353:      *\n1354:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\n1355:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to close the client, this error should be treated\n1356:      *                        as fatal and indicate the client is no longer usable.\n1357:      */\n1358:     @Override\n1359:     public void close() {\n1360:         close(Duration.ofMillis(Long.MAX_VALUE));\n1361:     }\n1362: \n1363:     /**\n1364:      * This method waits up to <code>timeout</code> for the producer to complete the sending of all incomplete requests.\n1365:      * <p>\n1366:      * If the producer is unable to complete all requests before the timeout expires, this method will fail\n1367:      * any unsent and unacknowledged records immediately. It will also abort the ongoing transaction if it's not\n1368:      * already completing.\n1369:      * <p>\n1370:      * If invoked from within a {@link Callback} this method will not block and will be equivalent to\n1371:      * <code>close(Duration.ofMillis(0))</code>. This is done since no further sending will happen while\n1372:      * blocking the I/O thread of the producer.\n1373:      *\n1374:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for producer to complete any pending requests. The value should be\n1375:      *                non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not wait for pending send requests to complete.\n1376:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\n1377:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to close the client, this error should be treated\n1378:      *                        as fatal and indicate the client is no longer usable.\n1379:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the <code>timeout</code> is negative.\n1380:      *\n1381:      */\n1382:     @Override\n1383:     public void close(Duration timeout) {\n1384:         close(timeout, false);\n1385:     }\n1386: \n1387:     private void close(Duration timeout, boolean swallowException) {\n1388:         long timeoutMs = timeout.toMillis();\n1389:         if (timeoutMs < 0)\n1390:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The timeout cannot be negative.\");\n1391:         log.info(\"Closing the Kafka producer with timeoutMillis = {} ms.\", timeoutMs);\n1392: \n1393:         // this will keep track of the first encountered exception\n1394:         AtomicReference<Throwable> firstException = new AtomicReference<>();\n1395:         boolean invokedFromCallback = Thread.currentThread() == this.ioThread;\n1396:         if (timeoutMs > 0) {\n1397:             if (invokedFromCallback) {\n1398:                 log.warn(\"Overriding close timeout {} ms to 0 ms in order to prevent useless blocking due to self-join. \" +\n1399:                         \"This means you have incorrectly invoked close with a non-zero timeout from the producer call-back.\",\n1400:                         timeoutMs);\n1401:             } else {\n1402:                 // Try to close gracefully.\n1403:                 final Timer closeTimer = time.timer(timeout);\n1404:                 clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(ClientTelemetryReporter::initiateClose);\n1405:                 closeTimer.update();\n1406: \n1407:                 if (this.sender != null) {\n1408:                     this.sender.initiateClose();\n1409:                     closeTimer.update();\n1410:                 }\n1411:                 if (this.ioThread !=
