until the send is complete\n     */\n    public void awaitFlushCompletion() throws InterruptedException {\n        try {\n            // Obtain a copy of all of the incomplete ProduceRequestResult(s) at the time of the flush.\n            // We must be careful not to hold a reference to the ProduceBatch(s) so that garbage\n            // collection can occur on the contents.\n            // The sender will remove ProducerBatch(s) from the original incomplete collection.\n            //\n            // We use awaitAllDependents() here instead of await() to ensure that if any batch\n            // was split into multiple batches, we wait for all the split batches to complete.\n            // This is required to guarantee that all records sent before flush()\n            // must be fully complete, including records in split batches.\n            for (ProduceRequestResult result : this.incomplete.requestResults())\n                result.awaitAllDependents();\n        } finally {\n            this.flushesInProgress.decrementAndGet();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether there are any pending batches (whether sent or unsent).\n     */\n    public boolean hasIncomplete() {\n        return !this.incomplete.isEmpty();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * This function is only called when sender is closed forcefully. It will fail all the\n     * incomplete batches and return.\n     */\n    public void abortIncompleteBatches() {\n        // We need to keep aborting the incomplete batch until no thread is trying to append to\n        // 1. Avoid losing batches.\n        // 2. Free up memory in case appending threads are blocked on buffer full.\n        // This is a tight loop but should be able to get through very quickly.\n        do {\n            abortBatches();\n        } while (appendsInProgress());\n        // After this point, no thread will append any messages because they will see the close\n        // flag set. We need to do the last abort after no thread was appending in case there was a new\n        // batch appended by the last appending thread.\n        abortBatches();\n        this.topicInfoMap.clear();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Go through incomplete batches and abort them.\n     */\n    private void abortBatches() {\n        abortBatches(new KafkaException(\"Producer is closed forcefully.\"));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Abort all incomplete batches (whether they have been sent or not)\n     */\n    void abortBatches(final RuntimeException reason) {\n        for (ProducerBatch batch : incomplete.copyAll()) {\n            Deque<ProducerBatch> dq = getDeque(batch.topicPartition);\n            synchronized (dq) {\n                batch.abortRecordAppends();\n                dq.remove(batch);\n            }\n            batch.abort(reason);\n            deallocate(batch);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Abort any batches which have not been drained\n     */\n    void abortUndrainedBatches(RuntimeException reason) {\n        for (ProducerBatch batch : incomplete.copyAll()) {\n            Deque<ProducerBatch> dq = getDeque(batch.topicPartition);\n            boolean aborted = false;\n            synchronized (dq) {\n                if ((transactionManager != null && !batch.hasSequence()) || (transactionManager == null && !batch.isClosed())) {\n                    aborted = true;\n                    batch.abortRecordAppends();\n                    dq.remove(batch);\n                }\n            }\n            if (aborted) {\n                batch.abort(reason);\n                deallocate(batch);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void mutePartition(TopicPartition tp) {\n        muted.add(tp);\n    }\n\n    public void unmutePartition(TopicPartition tp) {\n        muted.remove(tp);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Close this accumulator and force all the record buffers to be drained\n     */\n    public void close() {\n        this.closed = true;\n        this.free.close();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Partitioner config for built-in partitioner\n     */\n    public static final class PartitionerConfig {\n        private final boolean enableAdaptivePartitioning;\n        private final long partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs;\n\n        /**\n         * Partitioner config\n         *\n         * @param enableAdaptivePartitioning If it's true, partition switching adapts to broker load, otherwise partition\n         *        switching is random.\n         * @param partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs If a broker cannot process produce requests from a partition\n         *        for the specified time, the partition is treated by the partitioner as not available.\n         *        If the timeout is 0, this logic is disabled.\n         */\n        public PartitionerConfig(boolean enableAdaptivePartitioning, long partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs) {\n            this.enableAdaptivePartitioning = enableAdaptivePartitioning;\n            this.partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs = partitionAvailabilityTimeoutMs;\n        }\n\n        public PartitionerConfig() {\n            this(false, 0);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /*\n     * Metadata about a record just appended to the record accumulator\n     */\n    public static final class RecordAppendResult {\n        public final FutureRecordMetadata future;\n        public final boolean batchIsFull;\n        public final boolean newBatchCreated;\n        public final int appendedBytes;\n\n        public RecordAppendResult(FutureRecordMetadata future,\n                                  boolean batchIsFull,\n                                  boolean newBatchCreated,\n                                  int appendedBytes) {\n            this.future = future;\n            this.batchIsFull = batchIsFull;\n            this.newBatchCreated = newBatchCreated;\n            this.appendedBytes = appendedBytes;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /*\n     * The callbacks passed into append\n     */\n    public interface AppendCallbacks extends Callback {\n        /**\n         * Called to set partition (when append is called, partition may not be calculated yet).\n         * @param partition The partition\n         */\n        void setPartition(int partition);\n    }\n\n    /*\n     * The set of nodes that have at least one complete record batch in the accumulator\n     */\n    public static final class ReadyCheckResult {\n        public final Set<Node> readyNodes;\n        public final long nextReadyCheckDelayMs;\n        public final Set<String> unknownLeaderTopics;\n\n        public ReadyCheckResult(Set<Node> readyNodes, long nextReadyCheckDelayMs, Set<String> unknownLeaderTopics) {\n            this.readyNodes = readyNodes;\n            this.nextReadyCheckDelayMs = nextReadyCheckDelayMs;\n            this.unknownLeaderTopics = unknownLeaderTopics;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Per topic info.\n     */\n    private static class TopicInfo {\n        public final ConcurrentMap<Integer /*partition*/, Deque<ProducerBatch>> batches = new CopyOnWriteMap<>();\n        public final BuiltInPartitioner builtInPartitioner;\n\n        public TopicInfo(BuiltInPartitioner builtInPartitioner) {\n            this.builtInPartitioner = builtInPartitioner;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Node latency stats for each node that are used for adaptive partition distribution\n     * Visible for testing\n     */\n    public static final class NodeLatencyStats {\n        public volatile long readyTimeMs;  // last time the node had batches ready to send\n        public volatile long drainTimeMs;  // last time the node was able to drain batches\n\n        NodeLatencyStats(long nowMs) {\n            readyTimeMs = nowMs;\n            drainTimeMs = nowMs;\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BatchAccumulator.java",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

Values can be\\n276:      * either strings or Objects of the appropriate type (for example a numeric configuration would accept either the\\n277:      * string \\\"42\\\" or the integer 42).\\n278:      * <p>\\n279:      * Note: after creating a {@code KafkaProducer} you must always {@link #close()} it to avoid resource leaks.\\n280:      * @param configs   The producer configs\\n281:      *\\n282:      */\\n283:     public KafkaProducer(final Map<String, Object> configs) {\\n284:         this(configs, null, null);\\n285:     }\\n286: \\n287:     /**\\n288:      * A producer is instantiated by providing a set of key-value pairs as configuration, a key and a value {@link Serializer}.\\n289:      * Valid configuration strings are documented <a href=\\\"http://kafka.apache.org/documentation.html#producerconfigs\\\">here</a>.\\n290:      * Values can be either strings or Objects of the appropriate type (for example a numeric configuration would accept\\n291:      * either the string \\\"42\\\" or the integer 42).\\n292:      * <p>\\n293:      * Note: after creating a {@code KafkaProducer} you must always {@link #close()} it to avoid resource leaks.\\n294:      * @param configs   The producer configs\\n295:      * @param keySerializer  The serializer for key that implements {@link Serializer}. size of 1.\n425:             int batchSize = Math.max(1, config.getInt(ProducerConfig.BATCH_SIZE_CONFIG));\n426:             this.accumulator = new RecordAccumulator(logContext,\n427:                     batchSize,\n428:                     compression,\n429:                     lingerMs(config),\n430:                     retryBackoffMs,\n431:                     retryBackoffMaxMs,\n432:                     deliveryTimeoutMs,\n433:                     partitionerConfig,\n434:                     metrics,\n435:                     PRODUCER_METRIC_GROUP_NAME,\n436:                     time,\n437:                     transactionManager,\n438:                     new BufferPool(this.totalMemorySize, batchSize, metrics, time, PRODUCER_METRIC_GROUP_NAME));\n439: \n440: {\n444:                 this.metadata = new ProducerMetadata(retryBackoffMs,\n445:                         retryBackoffMaxMs,\n446:                         config.getLong(ProducerConfig.METADATA_MAX_AGE_CONFIG),\n447:                         config.getLong(ProducerConfig.METADATA_MAX_IDLE_CONFIG),\n448:                         logContext,\n449: (config.getBoolean(ProducerConfig.ENABLE_IDEMPOTENCE_CONFIG)) {\n597:             final String transactionalId = config.getString(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTIONAL_ID_CONFIG);\n598:             final boolean enable2PC = config.getBoolean(ProducerConfig.TRANSACTION_TWO_PHASE_COMMIT_ENABLE_CONFIG);\n599:             final int transactionTimeoutMs = does the following:\n625:      * <ol>\n626:      * <li>Ensures any transactions initiated by previous instances of the producer with the same\n627:      *      {@code transactional.id} are completed. If the previous instance had failed with a transaction in\n628:      *      progress, it will be aborted. If the last transaction had begun completion,\n629:      *      but not yet finished, this method awaits its completion.</li>\n630:      * <li>Gets the internal producer id and epoch, used in all future transactional\n631:      *      messages issued by the producer.</li>\n632:      * </ol>\n633:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transactional state cannot\n634:      * be initialized before expiration of {@code max.block.ms}. {
  "endLine": 900,

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"650:         throwIfProducerClosed();\\n651:         long now = time.nanoseconds();\\n652:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.initializeTransactions(false);\\n653:         sender.wakeup();\\n654:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\\n655:         producerMetrics.recordInit(time.nanoseconds() - now);\\n656:         transactionManager.maybeUpdateTransactionV2Enabled(true);\\n657:     }\\n658: \\n659:     /**\\n660:      * Should be called before the start of each new transaction. Note that prior to the first invocation\\n661:      * of this method, you must invoke {@link #initTransactions()} exactly one time.\\n662:      *\\n663:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no {@code transactional.id} has been configured or if {@link #initTransactions()}\\n664:      *         has not yet been invoked\\n665:      * @throws ProducerFencedException if another producer with the same transactional.id is active\\n666:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\\n667:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\\n668:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\\n669:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\\n670:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\\n671:      *         {@code transactional.id} is not authorized. See the exception for more details\\n672:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal error or for any other unexpected error\\n673:      */\\n674:     public void beginTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\\n675:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\\n676:         throwIfProducerClosed();\\n677:         throwIfInPreparedState();\\n678:         long now = time.nanoseconds();\\n679:         transactionManager.beginTransaction();\\n680:         producerMetrics.recordBeginTxn(time.nanoseconds() - now);\\n681:     }\\n682: \\n683:     /**\\n684:      * Sends a list of specified offsets to the consumer group coordinator, and also marks\\n685:      * those offsets as part of the current transaction. These offsets will be considered\\n686:      * committed only if the transaction is committed successfully. The committed offset should\\n687:      * be the next message your application will consume, i.e. {@code nextRecordToBeProcessed.offset()}\\n688:      * (or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}). You should also add the leader epoch as commit metadata,\\n689:      * which can be obtained from {@link ConsumerRecord#leaderEpoch()} or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}.\\n690:      * <p>\\n691:      * This method should be used when you need to batch consumed and produced messages\\n692:      * together, typically in a consume-transform-produce pattern. Thus, the specified\\n693:      * {@code groupMetadata} should be extracted from the used {@link KafkaConsumer consumer} via\\n694:      * {@link KafkaConsumer#groupMetadata()} to leverage consumer group metadata. This will provide\\n695:      * stronger fencing than just supplying the {@code consumerGroupId} and passing in {@code new ConsumerGroupMetadata(consumerGroupId)},\\n696:      * however note that the full set of consumer group metadata returned by {@link KafkaConsumer#groupMetadata()}\\n697:      * requires the brokers to be on version 2.5 or newer to understand.\\n698:      *\\n699:      * <p>\\n700:      * This method is a blocking call that waits until the request has been received and acknowledged by the consumer group\\n701:      * coordinator; but the offsets are not considered as committed until the transaction itself is successfully committed later (via\\n702:      * the {@link #commitTransaction()} call).\\n703:      *\\n704:      * <p>\\n705:      * Note, that the consumer should have {@code enable.auto.commit=false} and should\\n706:      * also not commit offsets manually (via {@link KafkaConsumer#commitSync(Map) sync} or\\n707:      * {@link KafkaConsumer#commitAsync(Map, OffsetCommitCallback) async} commits).\\n708:      * This method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the producer cannot send offsets before expiration of {@code max.block.ms}.\\n709:      * Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\\n710:      *\\n711:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started.\\n712:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\\n713:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\\n714:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0) or\\n715:      *         the broker doesn't support the latest version of transactional API with all consumer group metadata\\n716:      *         (i.e. if its version is lower than 2.5.0).\\n717:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedForMessageFormatException fatal error indicating the message\\n718:      *         format used for the offsets topic on the broker does not support transactions\\n719:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\\n720:      *         transactional.id is not authorized, or the consumer group id is not authorized.\\n721:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException if the commit failed and cannot be retried\\n722:      *         (e.g. if the consumer has been kicked out of the group). Users should handle this by aborting the transaction.\\n723:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this producer instance gets fenced by broker due to a\\n724:      *                                                                  mis-configured consumer instance id within group metadata.\\n725:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\\n726:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\\n727:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal or abortable error, or for any\\n728:      *         other unexpected error\\n729:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for sending the offsets has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\\n730:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\\n731:      */\\n732:     public void sendOffsetsToTransaction(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets,\\n733:                                          ConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata) throws ProducerFencedException {\\n734:         throwIfInvalidGroupMetadata(groupMetadata);\\n735:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\\n736:         throwIfProducerClosed();\\n737: \\n738:         if (!offsets.isEmpty()) {\\n739:             long start = time.nanoseconds();\\n740:             TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.sendOffsetsToTransaction(offsets, groupMetadata);\\n741:             sender.wakeup();\\n742:             result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\\n743:             producerMetrics.recordSendOffsets(time.nanoseconds() - start);\\n744:         }\\n745:     }\\n746: \\n747:     /**\\n748:      * Commits the ongoing transaction. This method will flush any unsent records before actually committing the transaction.\\n749:      * <p>\\n750:      * Further, if any of the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls which were part of the transaction hit irrecoverable\\n751:      * errors, this method will throw the last received exception immediately and the transaction will not be committed.\\n752:      * So all {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls in a transaction must succeed in order for this method to succeed.\\n753:      * <p>\\n754:      * If the transaction is committed successfully and this method returns without throwing an exception, it is guaranteed\\n755:      * that all {@link Callback callbacks} for records in the transaction will have been invoked and completed.\\n756:      * Note that exceptions thrown by callbacks are ignored; the producer proceeds to commit the transaction in any case.\\n757:      * <p>\\n758:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transaction cannot be committed before expiration\\n759:      * of {@code max.block.ms}, but this does not mean the request did not actually reach the broker. In fact, it only indicates\\n760:      * that we cannot get the acknowledgement response in time, so it's up to the application's logic\\n761:      * to decide how to handle timeouts.\\n762:      * Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\\n763:      * It is safe to retry in either case, but it is not possible to attempt a different operation (such as abortTransaction)\\n764:      * since the commit may already be in the progress of completing. If not retrying, the only option is to close the producer.\\n765:      *\\n766:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started\\n767:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\\n768:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\\n769:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\\n770:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\\n771:      *         transactional.id is not authorized. See the exception for more details\\n772:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\\n773:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\\n774:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal or abortable error, or for any\\n775:      *         other unexpected error\\n776:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for committing the transaction has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\\n777:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\\n778:      */\\n779:     public void commitTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\\n780:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\\n781:         throwIfProducerClosed();\\n782:         long commitStart = time.nanoseconds();\\n783:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.beginCommit();\\n784:         sender.wakeup();\\n785:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\\n786:         producerMetrics.recordCommitTxn(time.nanoseconds() - commitStart);\\n787:     }\\n788: \\n789:     /**\\n790:      * Aborts the ongoing transaction. Any unflushed produce messages will be aborted when this call is made.\\n791:      * This call will throw an exception immediately if any prior {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls failed with a\\n792:      * {@link ProducerFencedException} or an instance of {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException}.\\n793:      * <p>\\n794:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transaction cannot be aborted before expiration\\n795:      * of {@code max.block.ms}, but this does not mean the request did not actually reach the broker. In fact, it only indicates\\n796:      * that we cannot get the acknowledgement response in time, so it's up to the application's logic\\n797:      * to decide how to handle timeouts. Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\\n798:      * It is safe to retry in either case, but it is not possible to attempt a different operation (such as {@link #commitTransaction})\\n799:      * since the abort may already be in the progress of completing. If not retrying, the only option is to close the producer.\\n800:      *\\n801:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started\\n802:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\\n803:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\\n804:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\\n805:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\\n806:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\\n807:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\\n808:      *         transactional.id is not authorized. See the exception for more details\\n809:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal error or for any other unexpected error\\n810:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for aborting the transaction has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\\n811:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\\n812:      */\\n813:     public void abortTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\\n814:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\\n815:         throwIfProducerClosed();\\n816:         log.info(\\\"Aborting incomplete transaction\\\");\\n817:         long abortStart = time.nanoseconds();\\n818:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.beginAbort();\\n819:         sender.wakeup();\\n820:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\\n821:         producerMetrics.recordAbortTxn(time.nanoseconds() - abortStart);\\n822:     }\\n823: \\n824:     /**\\n825:      * Asynchronously send a record to a topic. Equivalent to <code>send(record, null)</code>.\\n826:      * See {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)} for details.\\n827:      */\\n828:     @Override\\n829:     public Future<RecordMetadata> send(ProducerRecord<K, V> record) {\\n830:         return send(record, null);\\n831:     }\\n832: \\n833:     /**\\n834:      * Asynchronously send a record to a topic and invoke the provided callback when the send has been acknowledged.\\n835:      * <p>\\n836:      * The send is asynchronous and this method will return immediately (except for rare cases described below)\\n837:      * once the record has been stored in the buffer of records waiting to be sent.\\n838:      * This allows sending many records in parallel without blocking to wait for the response after each one.\\n839:      * Can block for the following cases: 1) For the first record being sent to \\n840:      * the cluster by this client for the given topic. In this case it will block for up to {@code max.block.ms} milliseconds if \\n841:      * Kafka cluster is unreachable; 2) Allocating a buffer if buffer pool doesn't have any free buffers.\\n842:      * <p>\\n843:      * The result of the send is a {@link RecordMetadata} specifying the partition the record was sent to, the offset\\n844:      * it was assigned and the timestamp of the record. If the producer is configured with acks = 0, the {@link RecordMetadata}\\n845:      * will have offset = -1 because the producer does not wait for the acknowledgement from the broker.\\n846:      * If {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#CREATE_TIME CreateTime} is used by the topic, the timestamp\\n847:      * will be the user provided timestamp or the record send time if the user did not specify a timestamp for the\\n848:      * record. If {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#LOG_APPEND_TIME LogAppendTime} is used for the\\n849:      * topic, the timestamp will be the Kafka broker local time when the message is appended.\\n850:      * <p>\\n851:      * Since the send call is asynchronous it returns a {@link java.util.concurrent.Future Future} for the\\n852:      * {@link RecordMetadata} that will be assigned to this record. Invoking {@link java.util.concurrent.Future#get()\\n853:      * get()} on this future will block until the associated request completes and then return the metadata for the record\\n854:      * or throw any exception that occurred while sending the record.\\n855:      * <p>\\n856:      * If you want to simulate a simple blocking call you can call the <code>get()</code> method immediately:\\n857:      *\\n858:      * <pre>\\n859:      * {@code\\n860:      * byte[] key = \\\"key\\\".getBytes();\\n861:      * byte[] value = \\\"value\\\".getBytes();\\n862:      * ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]> record = new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(\\\"my-topic\\\", key, value)\\n863:      * producer.send(record).get();\\n864:      * }</pre>\\n865:      * <p>\\n866:      * Fully non-blocking usage can make use of the {@link Callback} parameter to provide a callback that\\n867:      * will be invoked when the request is complete.\\n868:      *\\n869:      * <pre>\\n870:      * {@code\\n871:      * ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]> record = new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(\\\"the-topic\\\", key, value);\\n872:      * producer.send(myRecord,\\n873:      *               new Callback() {\\n874:      *                   public void onCompletion(RecordMetadata metadata, Exception e) {\\n875:      *                       if(e != null) {\\n876:      *                          e.printStackTrace();\\n877:      *                       } else {\\n878:      *                          System.out.println(\\\"The offset of the record we just sent is: \\\" + metadata.offset());\\n879:      *                       }\\n880:      *                   }\\n881:      *               });\\n882:      * }\\n883:      * </pre>\\n884:      *\\n885:      * Callbacks for records being sent to the same partition are guaranteed to execute in order. That is, in the\\n886:      * following example <code>callback1</code> is guaranteed to execute before <code>callback2</code>:\\n887:      *\\n888:      * <pre>\\n889:      * {@code\\n890:      * producer.send(new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(topic, partition, key1, value1), callback1);\\n891:      * producer.send(new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(topic, partition, key2, value2), callback2);\\n892:      * }\\n893:      * </pre>\\n894:      * <p>\\n895:      * When used as part of a transaction, it is not necessary to define a callback or check the result of the future\\n896:      * in order to detect errors from <code>send</code>. If any of the send calls failed with an irrecoverable error,\\n897:      * the final {@link #commitTransaction()} call will fail and throw the exception from the last failed send. When\\n898:      * this happens, your application should call {@link #abortTransaction()} to reset the state and continue to send\\n899:      * data.\\n900:      * </p>\\n901: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java%3FL650-900\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "650:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n651:         long now = time.nanoseconds();\n652:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.initializeTransactions(false);\n653:         sender.wakeup();\n654:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n655:         producerMetrics.recordInit(time.nanoseconds() - now);\n656:         transactionManager.maybeUpdateTransactionV2Enabled(true);\n657:     }\n658: \n659:     /**\n660:      * Should be called before the start of each new transaction. Note that prior to the first invocation\n661:      * of this method, you must invoke {@link #initTransactions()} exactly one time.\n662:      *\n663:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no {@code transactional.id} has been configured or if {@link #initTransactions()}\n664:      *         has not yet been invoked\n665:      * @throws ProducerFencedException if another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n666:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n667:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n668:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n669:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n670:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n671:      *         {@code transactional.id} is not authorized. See the exception for more details\n672:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal error or for any other unexpected error\n673:      */\n674:     public void beginTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\n675:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n676:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n677:         throwIfInPreparedState();\n678:         long now = time.nanoseconds();\n679:         transactionManager.beginTransaction();\n680:         producerMetrics.recordBeginTxn(time.nanoseconds() - now);\n681:     }\n682: \n683:     /**\n684:      * Sends a list of specified offsets to the consumer group coordinator, and also marks\n685:      * those offsets as part of the current transaction. These offsets will be considered\n686:      * committed only if the transaction is committed successfully. The committed offset should\n687:      * be the next message your application will consume, i.e. {@code nextRecordToBeProcessed.offset()}\n688:      * (or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}). You should also add the leader epoch as commit metadata,\n689:      * which can be obtained from {@link ConsumerRecord#leaderEpoch()} or {@link ConsumerRecords#nextOffsets()}.\n690:      * <p>\n691:      * This method should be used when you need to batch consumed and produced messages\n692:      * together, typically in a consume-transform-produce pattern. Thus, the specified\n693:      * {@code groupMetadata} should be extracted from the used {@link KafkaConsumer consumer} via\n694:      * {@link KafkaConsumer#groupMetadata()} to leverage consumer group metadata. This will provide\n695:      * stronger fencing than just supplying the {@code consumerGroupId} and passing in {@code new ConsumerGroupMetadata(consumerGroupId)},\n696:      * however note that the full set of consumer group metadata returned by {@link KafkaConsumer#groupMetadata()}\n697:      * requires the brokers to be on version 2.5 or newer to understand.\n698:      *\n699:      * <p>\n700:      * This method is a blocking call that waits until the request has been received and acknowledged by the consumer group\n701:      * coordinator; but the offsets are not considered as committed until the transaction itself is successfully committed later (via\n702:      * the {@link #commitTransaction()} call).\n703:      *\n704:      * <p>\n705:      * Note, that the consumer should have {@code enable.auto.commit=false} and should\n706:      * also not commit offsets manually (via {@link KafkaConsumer#commitSync(Map) sync} or\n707:      * {@link KafkaConsumer#commitAsync(Map, OffsetCommitCallback) async} commits).\n708:      * This method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the producer cannot send offsets before expiration of {@code max.block.ms}.\n709:      * Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\n710:      *\n711:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started.\n712:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n713:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n714:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0) or\n715:      *         the broker doesn't support the latest version of transactional API with all consumer group metadata\n716:      *         (i.e. if its version is lower than 2.5.0).\n717:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedForMessageFormatException fatal error indicating the message\n718:      *         format used for the offsets topic on the broker does not support transactions\n719:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n720:      *         transactional.id is not authorized, or the consumer group id is not authorized.\n721:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException if the commit failed and cannot be retried\n722:      *         (e.g. if the consumer has been kicked out of the group). Users should handle this by aborting the transaction.\n723:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.FencedInstanceIdException if this producer instance gets fenced by broker due to a\n724:      *                                                                  mis-configured consumer instance id within group metadata.\n725:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n726:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n727:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal or abortable error, or for any\n728:      *         other unexpected error\n729:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for sending the offsets has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n730:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n731:      */\n732:     public void sendOffsetsToTransaction(Map<TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata> offsets,\n733:                                          ConsumerGroupMetadata groupMetadata) throws ProducerFencedException {\n734:         throwIfInvalidGroupMetadata(groupMetadata);\n735:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n736:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n737: \n738:         if (!offsets.isEmpty()) {\n739:             long start = time.nanoseconds();\n740:             TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.sendOffsetsToTransaction(offsets, groupMetadata);\n741:             sender.wakeup();\n742:             result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n743:             producerMetrics.recordSendOffsets(time.nanoseconds() - start);\n744:         }\n745:     }\n746: \n747:     /**\n748:      * Commits the ongoing transaction. This method will flush any unsent records before actually committing the transaction.\n749:      * <p>\n750:      * Further, if any of the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls which were part of the transaction hit irrecoverable\n751:      * errors, this method will throw the last received exception immediately and the transaction will not be committed.\n752:      * So all {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls in a transaction must succeed in order for this method to succeed.\n753:      * <p>\n754:      * If the transaction is committed successfully and this method returns without throwing an exception, it is guaranteed\n755:      * that all {@link Callback callbacks} for records in the transaction will have been invoked and completed.\n756:      * Note that exceptions thrown by callbacks are ignored; the producer proceeds to commit the transaction in any case.\n757:      * <p>\n758:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transaction cannot be committed before expiration\n759:      * of {@code max.block.ms}, but this does not mean the request did not actually reach the broker. In fact, it only indicates\n760:      * that we cannot get the acknowledgement response in time, so it's up to the application's logic\n761:      * to decide how to handle timeouts.\n762:      * Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\n763:      * It is safe to retry in either case, but it is not possible to attempt a different operation (such as abortTransaction)\n764:      * since the commit may already be in the progress of completing. If not retrying, the only option is to close the producer.\n765:      *\n766:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started\n767:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n768:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n769:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n770:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n771:      *         transactional.id is not authorized. See the exception for more details\n772:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n773:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n774:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal or abortable error, or for any\n775:      *         other unexpected error\n776:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for committing the transaction has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n777:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n778:      */\n779:     public void commitTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\n780:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n781:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n782:         long commitStart = time.nanoseconds();\n783:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.beginCommit();\n784:         sender.wakeup();\n785:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n786:         producerMetrics.recordCommitTxn(time.nanoseconds() - commitStart);\n787:     }\n788: \n789:     /**\n790:      * Aborts the ongoing transaction. Any unflushed produce messages will be aborted when this call is made.\n791:      * This call will throw an exception immediately if any prior {@link #send(ProducerRecord)} calls failed with a\n792:      * {@link ProducerFencedException} or an instance of {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException}.\n793:      * <p>\n794:      * Note that this method will raise {@link TimeoutException} if the transaction cannot be aborted before expiration\n795:      * of {@code max.block.ms}, but this does not mean the request did not actually reach the broker. In fact, it only indicates\n796:      * that we cannot get the acknowledgement response in time, so it's up to the application's logic\n797:      * to decide how to handle timeouts. Additionally, it will raise {@link InterruptException} if interrupted.\n798:      * It is safe to retry in either case, but it is not possible to attempt a different operation (such as {@link #commitTransaction})\n799:      * since the abort may already be in the progress of completing. If not retrying, the only option is to close the producer.\n800:      *\n801:      * @throws IllegalStateException if no transactional.id has been configured or no transaction has been started\n802:      * @throws ProducerFencedException fatal error indicating another producer with the same transactional.id is active\n803:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidProducerEpochException if the producer has attempted to produce with an old epoch\n804:      *         to the partition leader. See the exception for more details\n805:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException fatal error indicating the broker\n806:      *         does not support transactions (i.e. if its version is lower than 0.11.0.0)\n807:      * @throws org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException fatal error indicating that the configured\n808:      *         transactional.id is not authorized. See the exception for more details\n809:      * @throws KafkaException if the producer has encountered a previous fatal error or for any other unexpected error\n810:      * @throws TimeoutException if the time taken for aborting the transaction has surpassed <code>max.block.ms</code>.\n811:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n812:      */\n813:     public void abortTransaction() throws ProducerFencedException {\n814:         throwIfNoTransactionManager();\n815:         throwIfProducerClosed();\n816:         log.info(\"Aborting incomplete transaction\");\n817:         long abortStart = time.nanoseconds();\n818:         TransactionalRequestResult result = transactionManager.beginAbort();\n819:         sender.wakeup();\n820:         result.await(maxBlockTimeMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n821:         producerMetrics.recordAbortTxn(time.nanoseconds() - abortStart);\n822:     }\n823: \n824:     /**\n825:      * Asynchronously send a record to a topic. Equivalent to <code>send(record, null)</code>.\n826:      * See {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)} for details.\n827:      */\n828:     @Override\n829:     public Future<RecordMetadata> send(ProducerRecord<K, V> record) {\n830:         return send(record, null);\n831:     }\n832: \n833:     /**\n834:      * Asynchronously send a record to a topic and invoke the provided callback when the send has been acknowledged.\n835:      * <p>\n836:      * The send is asynchronous and this method will return immediately (except for rare cases described below)\n837:      * once the record has been stored in the buffer of records waiting to be sent.\n838:      * This allows sending many records in parallel without blocking to wait for the response after each one.\n839:      * Can block for the following cases: 1) For the first record being sent to \n840:      * the cluster by this client for the given topic. In this case it will block for up to {@code max.block.ms} milliseconds if \n841:      * Kafka cluster is unreachable; 2) Allocating a buffer if buffer pool doesn't have any free buffers.\n842:      * <p>\n843:      * The result of the send is a {@link RecordMetadata} specifying the partition the record was sent to, the offset\n844:      * it was assigned and the timestamp of the record. If the producer is configured with acks = 0, the {@link RecordMetadata}\n845:      * will have offset = -1 because the producer does not wait for the acknowledgement from the broker.\n846:      * If {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#CREATE_TIME CreateTime} is used by the topic, the timestamp\n847:      * will be the user provided timestamp or the record send time if the user did not specify a timestamp for the\n848:      * record. If {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#LOG_APPEND_TIME LogAppendTime} is used for the\n849:      * topic, the timestamp will be the Kafka broker local time when the message is appended.\n850:      * <p>\n851:      * Since the send call is asynchronous it returns a {@link java.util.concurrent.Future Future} for the\n852:      * {@link RecordMetadata} that will be assigned to this record. Invoking {@link java.util.concurrent.Future#get()\n853:      * get()} on this future will block until the associated request completes and then return the metadata for the record\n854:      * or throw any exception that occurred while sending the record.\n855:      * <p>\n856:      * If you want to simulate a simple blocking call you can call the <code>get()</code> method immediately:\n857:      *\n858:      * <pre>\n859:      * {@code\n860:      * byte[] key = \"key\".getBytes();\n861:      * byte[] value = \"value\".getBytes();\n862:      * ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]> record = new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(\"my-topic\", key, value)\n863:      * producer.send(record).get();\n864:      * }</pre>\n865:      * <p>\n866:      * Fully non-blocking usage can make use of the {@link Callback} parameter to provide a callback that\n867:      * will be invoked when the request is complete.\n868:      *\n869:      * <pre>\n870:      * {@code\n871:      * ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]> record = new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(\"the-topic\", key, value);\n872:      * producer.send(myRecord,\n873:      *               new Callback() {\n874:      *                   public void onCompletion(RecordMetadata metadata, Exception e) {\n875:      *                       if(e != null) {\n876:      *                          e.printStackTrace();\n877:      *                       } else {\n878:      *                          System.out.println(\"The offset of the record we just sent is: \" + metadata.offset());\n879:      *                       }\n880:      *                   }\n881:      *               });\n882:      * }\n883:      * </pre>\n884:      *\n885:      * Callbacks for records being sent to the same partition are guaranteed to execute in order. That is, in the\n886:      * following example <code>callback1</code> is guaranteed to execute before <code>callback2</code>:\n887:      *\n888:      * <pre>\n889:      * {@code\n890:      * producer.send(new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(topic, partition, key1, value1), callback1);\n891:      * producer.send(new ProducerRecord<byte[],byte[]>(topic, partition, key2, value2), callback2);\n892:      * }\n893:      * </pre>\n894:      * <p>\n895:      * When used as part of a transaction, it is not necessary to define a callback or check the result of the future\n896:      * in order to detect errors from <code>send</code>. If any of the send calls failed with an irrecoverable error,\n897:      * the final {@link #commitTransaction()} call will fail and throw the exception from the last failed send. When\n898:      * this happens, your application should call {@link #abortTransaction()} to reset the state and continue to send\n899:      * data.\n900:      * </p>\n901: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java%3FL650-900"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"900:      * </p>\\n901:      * <p>\\n902:      * Some transactional send errors cannot be resolved with a call to {@link #abortTransaction()}.  In particular,\\n903:      * if a transactional send finishes with a {@link ProducerFencedException}, a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.OutOfOrderSequenceException},\\n904:      * a {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException}, or an\\n905:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException}, then the only option left is to call {@link #close()}.\\n906:      * Fatal errors cause the producer to enter a defunct state in which future API calls will continue to raise\\n907:      * the same underlying error wrapped in a new {@link KafkaException}.\\n908:      * </p>\\n909:      * <p>\\n910:      * It is a similar picture when idempotence is enabled, but no <code>transactional.id</code> has been configured.\\n911:      * In this case, {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedVersionException} and\\n912:      * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException} are considered fatal errors. However,\\n913:      * {@link ProducerFencedException} does not need to be handled. Additionally, it is possible to continue\\n914:      * sending after receiving an {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.OutOfOrderSequenceException}, but doing so\\n915:      * can result in out of order delivery of pending messages. To ensure proper ordering, you should close the\\n916:      * producer and create a new instance.\\n917:      * </p>\\n918:      * <p>\\n919:      * If the message format of the destination topic is not upgraded to 0.11.0.0, idempotent and transactional\\n920:      * produce requests will fail with an {@link org.apache.kafka.common.errors.UnsupportedForMessageFormatException}\\n921:      * error. If this is encountered during a transaction, it is possible to abort and continue. But note that future\\n922:      * sends to the same topic will continue receiving the same exception until the topic is upgraded.\\n923:      * </p>\\n924:      * <p>\\n925:      * Note that callbacks will generally execute in the I/O thread of the producer and so should be reasonably fast or\\n926:      * they will delay the sending of messages from other threads. If you want to execute blocking or computationally\\n927:      * expensive callbacks it is recommended to use your own {@link java.util.concurrent.Executor} in the callback body\\n928:      * to parallelize processing.\\n929:      *\\n930:      * @param record The record to send\\n931:      * @param callback A user-supplied callback to execute when the record has been acknowledged by the server (null\\n932:      *        indicates no callback)\\n933:      *\\n934:      * @throws IllegalStateException if a transactional.id has been configured and no transaction has been started, or\\n935:      *                               when send is invoked after producer has been closed.\\n936:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked\\n937:      * @throws SerializationException If the key or value are not valid objects given the configured serializers\\n938:      * @throws KafkaException If a Kafka related error occurs that does not belong to the public API exceptions.\\n939:      */\\n940:     @Override\\n941:     public Future<RecordMetadata> send(ProducerRecord<K, V> record, Callback callback) {\\n942:         // intercept the record, which can be potentially modified; this method does not throw exceptions\\n943:         ProducerRecord<K, V> interceptedRecord = this.interceptors.onSend(record);\\n944:         return doSend(interceptedRecord, callback);\\n945:     }\\n946: \\n947:     // Verify that this producer instance has not been closed. This method throws IllegalStateException if the producer\\n948:     // has already been closed.\\n949:     private void throwIfProducerClosed() {\\n950:         if (sender == null || !sender.isRunning())\\n951:             throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"Cannot perform operation after producer has been closed\\\");\\n952:     }\\n953: \\n954:     /**\\n955:      * Throws an exception if the transaction is in a prepared state.\\n956:      * In a two-phase commit (2PC) flow, once a transaction enters the prepared state,\\n957:      * only commit, abort, or complete operations are allowed.\\n958:      *\\n959:      * @throws IllegalStateException if any other operation is attempted in the prepared state.\\n960:      */\\n961:     private void throwIfInPreparedState() {\\n962:         if (transactionManager != null &&\\n963:             transactionManager.isTransactional() &&\\n964:             transactionManager.isPrepared()\\n965:         ) {\\n966:             throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"Cannot perform operation while the transaction is in a prepared state. \\\" +\\n967:                 \\\"Only commitTransaction(), abortTransaction(), or completeTransaction() are permitted.\\\");\\n968:         }\\n969:     }\\n970: \\n971:     /**\\n972:      * Implementation of asynchronously send a record to a topic.\\n973:      */\\n974:     private Future<RecordMetadata> doSend(ProducerRecord<K, V> record, Callback callback) {\\n975:         // Append callback takes care of the following:\\n976:         //  - call interceptors and user callback on completion\\n977:         //  - remember partition that is calculated in RecordAccumulator.append\\n978:         AppendCallbacks appendCallbacks = new AppendCallbacks(callback, this.interceptors, record);\\n979: \\n980:         try {\\n981:             throwIfProducerClosed();\\n982:             throwIfInPreparedState();\\n983: \\n984:             // first make sure the metadata for the topic is available\\n985:             long nowMs = time.milliseconds();\\n986:             ClusterAndWaitTime clusterAndWaitTime;\\n987:             try {\\n988:                 clusterAndWaitTime = waitOnMetadata(record.topic(), record.partition(), nowMs, maxBlockTimeMs);\\n989:             } catch (KafkaException e) {\\n990:                 if (metadata.isClosed())\\n991:                     throw new KafkaException(\\\"Producer closed while send in progress\\\", e);\\n992:                 throw e;\\n993:             }\\n994:             nowMs += clusterAndWaitTime.waitedOnMetadataMs;\\n995:             long remainingWaitMs = Math.max(0, maxBlockTimeMs - clusterAndWaitTime.waitedOnMetadataMs);\\n996:             Cluster cluster = clusterAndWaitTime.cluster;\\n997:             byte[] serializedKey;\\n998:             try {\\n999:                 serializedKey = keySerializerPlugin.get().serialize(record.topic(), record.headers(), record.key());\\n1000:             } catch (ClassCastException cce) {\\n1001:                 throw new SerializationException(\\\"Can't convert key of class \\\" + record.key().getClass().getName() +\\n1002:                         \\\" to class \\\" + producerConfig.getClass(ProducerConfig.KEY_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG).getName() +\\n1003:                         \\\" specified in key.serializer\\\", cce);\\n1004:             }\\n1005:             byte[] serializedValue;\\n1006:             try {\\n1007:                 serializedValue = valueSerializerPlugin.get().serialize(record.topic(), record.headers(), record.value());\\n1008:             } catch (ClassCastException cce) {\\n1009:                 throw new SerializationException(\\\"Can't convert value of class \\\" + record.value().getClass().getName() +\\n1010:                         \\\" to class \\\" + producerConfig.getClass(ProducerConfig.VALUE_SERIALIZER_CLASS_CONFIG).getName() +\\n1011:                         \\\" specified in value.serializer\\\", cce);\\n1012:             }\\n1013: \\n1014:             // Try to calculate partition, but note that after this call it can be RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION,\\n1015:             // which means that the RecordAccumulator would pick a partition using built-in logic (which may\\n1016:             // take into account broker load, the amount of data produced to each partition, etc.).\\n1017:             int partition = partition(record, serializedKey, serializedValue, cluster);\\n1018: \\n1019:             setReadOnly(record.headers());\\n1020:             Header[] headers = record.headers().toArray();\\n1021: \\n1022:             int serializedSize = AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytesUpperBound(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\\n1023:                     compression.type(), serializedKey, serializedValue, headers);\\n1024:             ensureValidRecordSize(serializedSize);\\n1025:             long timestamp = record.timestamp() == null ? nowMs : record.timestamp();\\n1026: \\n1027:             // Append the record to the accumulator.  Note, that the actual partition may be\\n1028:             // calculated there and can be accessed via appendCallbacks.topicPartition.\\n1029:             RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult result = accumulator.append(record.topic(), partition, timestamp, serializedKey,\\n1030:                     serializedValue, headers, appendCallbacks, remainingWaitMs, nowMs, cluster);\\n1031:             assert appendCallbacks.getPartition() != RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION;\\n1032: \\n1033:             // Add the partition to the transaction (if in progress) after it has been successfully\\n1034:             // appended to the accumulator. We cannot do it before because the partition may be\\n1035:             // unknown. Note that the `Sender` will refuse to dequeue\\n1036:             // batches from the accumulator until they have been added to the transaction.\\n1037:             if (transactionManager != null) {\\n1038:                 transactionManager.maybeAddPartition(appendCallbacks.topicPartition());\\n1039:             }\\n1040: \\n1041:             if (result.batchIsFull || result.newBatchCreated) {\\n1042:                 log.trace(\\\"Waking up the sender since topic {} partition {} is either full or getting a new batch\\\", record.topic(), appendCallbacks.getPartition());\\n1043:                 this.sender.wakeup();\\n1044:             }\\n1045:             return result.future;\\n1046:             // handling exceptions and record the errors;\\n1047:             // for API exceptions return them in the future,\\n1048:             // for other exceptions throw directly\\n1049:         } catch (ApiException e) {\\n1050:             log.debug(\\\"Exception occurred during message send:\\\", e);\\n1051:             if (callback != null) {\\n1052:                 TopicPartition tp = appendCallbacks.topicPartition();\\n1053:                 RecordMetadata nullMetadata = new RecordMetadata(tp, -1, -1, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, -1, -1);\\n1054:                 callback.onCompletion(nullMetadata, e);\\n1055:             }\\n1056:             this.errors.record();\\n1057:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\\n1058:             if (transactionManager != null) {\\n1059:                 transactionManager.maybeTransitionToErrorState(e);\\n1060:             }\\n1061:             return new FutureFailure(e);\\n1062:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\\n1063:             this.errors.record();\\n1064:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\\n1065:             throw new InterruptException(e);\\n1066:         } catch (KafkaException e) {\\n1067:             this.errors.record();\\n1068:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\\n1069:             throw e;\\n1070:         } catch (Exception e) {\\n1071:             // we notify interceptor about all exceptions, since onSend is called before anything else in this method\\n1072:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\\n1073:             throw e;\\n1074:         }\\n1075:     }\\n1076: \\n1077:     private void setReadOnly(Headers headers) {\\n1078:         if (headers instanceof RecordHeaders) {\\n1079:             ((RecordHeaders) headers).setReadOnly();\\n1080:         }\\n1081:     }\\n1082: \\n1083:     /**\\n1084:      * Wait for cluster metadata including partitions for the given topic to be available.\\n1085:      * @param topic The topic we want metadata for\\n1086:      * @param partition A specific partition expected to exist in metadata, or null if there's no preference\\n1087:      * @param nowMs The current time in ms\\n1088:      * @param maxWaitMs The maximum time in ms for waiting on the metadata\\n1089:      * @return The cluster containing topic metadata and the amount of time we waited in ms\\n1090:      * @throws TimeoutException if metadata could not be refreshed within {@code max.block.ms}\\n1091:      * @throws KafkaException for all Kafka-related exceptions, including the case where this method is called after producer close\\n1092:      */\\n1093:     private ClusterAndWaitTime waitOnMetadata(String topic, Integer partition, long nowMs, long maxWaitMs) throws InterruptedException {\\n1094:         Cluster cluster = metadata.fetch();\\n1095: \\n1096:         if (cluster.invalidTopics().contains(topic))\\n1097:             throw new InvalidTopicException(topic);\\n1098: \\n1099:         // add topic to metadata topic list if it is not there already and reset expiry\\n1100:         metadata.add(topic, nowMs);\\n1101: accumulator.  Note, that the actual partition may be\n1028:             // calculated there and can be accessed via appendCallbacks.topicPartition.\n1029:             RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult result = accumulator.append(record.topic(), partition, timestamp, serializedKey,\n1030:                     serializedValue, headers, appendCallbacks, remainingWaitMs, nowMs, cluster);\n1031:             assert appendCallbacks.getPartition() != RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION;\n1032: \n1033:             // Add the partition to the transaction (if in progress) after it has been successfully\n1034:             // appended to the accumulator. We cannot do it before because the partition may be\n1035:             // unknown. Note that the `Sender` will refuse to dequeue\n1036:             // batches from the accumulator until they have been added to the transaction.\n1037:             if (transactionManager != null) {\n1038:                 transactionManager.maybeAddPartition(appendCallbacks.topicPartition());\n1039:             }\n1040: \n1041:             if (result.batchIsFull || result.newBatchCreated) {\n1042:                 log.trace(\"Waking up the sender since topic {} partition {} is either full or getting a new batch\", record.topic(), appendCallbacks.getPartition());\n1043:                 this.sender.wakeup();\n1044:             }\n1045:             return result.future;\n1046:             // handling exceptions and record the errors;\n1047:             // for API exceptions return them in the future,\n1048:             // for other exceptions throw directly\n1049:         } catch (ApiException e) {\n1050:             log.debug(\"Exception occurred during message send:\", e);\n1051:             if (callback != null) {\n1052:                 TopicPartition tp = appendCallbacks.topicPartition();\n1053:                 RecordMetadata nullMetadata = new RecordMetadata(tp, -1, -1, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, -1, -1);\n1054:                 callback.onCompletion(nullMetadata, e);\n1055:             }\n1056:             this.errors.record();\n1057:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1058:             if (transactionManager != null) {\n1059:                 transactionManager.maybeTransitionToErrorState(e);\n1060:             }\n1061:             return new FutureFailure(e);\n1062:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n1063:             this.errors.record();\n1064:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1065:             throw new InterruptException(e);\n1066:         } catch (KafkaException e) {\n1067:             this.errors.record();\n1068:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1069:             throw e;\n1070:         } catch (Exception e) {\n1071:             // we notify interceptor about all exceptions, since onSend is called before anything else in this method\n1072:             this.interceptors.onSendError(record, appendCallbacks.topicPartition(), e);\n1073:             throw e;\n1074:         }\n1075:     }\n1076: \n1077:     private void setReadOnly(Headers headers) {\n1078:         if (headers instanceof RecordHeaders) {\n1079:             ((RecordHeaders) headers).setReadOnly();\n1080:         }\n1081:     }\n1082: \n1083:     /**\n1084:      * Wait for cluster metadata including partitions for the given topic to be available.\n1085:      * @param topic The topic we want metadata for\n1086:      * @param partition A specific partition expected to exist in metadata, or null if there's no preference\n1087:      * @param nowMs The current time in ms\n1088:      * @param maxWaitMs The maximum time in ms for waiting on the metadata\n1089:      * @return The cluster containing topic metadata and the amount of time we waited in ms\n1090:      * @throws TimeoutException if metadata could not be refreshed within {@code max.block.ms}\n1091:      * @throws KafkaException for all Kafka-related exceptions, including the case where this method is called after producer close\n1092:      */\n1093:     private ClusterAndWaitTime waitOnMetadata(String topic, Integer partition, long nowMs, long maxWaitMs) throws InterruptedException {\n1094:         Cluster cluster = metadata.fetch();\n1095: \n1096:         if (cluster.invalidTopics().contains(topic))\n1097:             throw new InvalidTopicException(topic);\n1098: \n1099:         // add topic to metadata topic list if it is not there already and reset expiry\n1100:         metadata.add(topic, nowMs);\n1101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java%3FL900-1100"
  }
}
