  "content": "{\"content\":\"1100:         metadata.add(topic, nowMs);\\n1101: \\n1102:         Integer partitionsCount = cluster.partitionCountForTopic(topic);\\n1103:         // Return cached metadata if we have it, and if the record's partition is either undefined\\n1104:         // or within the known partition range\\n1105:         if (partitionsCount != null && (partition == null || partition < partitionsCount))\\n1106:             return new ClusterAndWaitTime(cluster, 0);\\n1107: \\n1108:         long remainingWaitMs = maxWaitMs;\\n1109:         long elapsed = 0;\\n1110:         // Issue metadata requests until we have metadata for the topic and the requested partition,\\n1111:         // or until maxWaitTimeMs is exceeded. This is necessary in case the metadata\\n1112:         // is stale and the number of partitions for this topic has increased in the meantime.\\n1113:         long nowNanos = time.nanoseconds();\\n1114:         do {\\n1115:             if (partition != null) {\\n1116:                 log.trace(\\\"Requesting metadata update for partition {} of topic {}.\\\", partition, topic);\\n1117:             } else {\\n1118:                 log.trace(\\\"Requesting metadata update for topic {}.\\\", topic);\\n1119:             }\\n1120:             metadata.add(topic, nowMs + elapsed);\\n1121:             int version = metadata.requestUpdateForTopic(topic);\\n1122:             sender.wakeup();\\n1123:             try {\\n1124:                 metadata.awaitUpdate(version, remainingWaitMs);\\n1125:             } catch (TimeoutException ex) {\\n1126:                 // Rethrow with original maxWaitMs to prevent logging exception with remainingWaitMs\\n1127:                 final String errorMessage = getErrorMessage(partitionsCount, topic, partition, maxWaitMs);\\n1128:                 if (metadata.getError(topic) != null) {\\n1129:                     throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage, metadata.getError(topic).exception());\\n1130:                 }\\n1131:                 throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage);\\n1132:             }\\n1133:             cluster = metadata.fetch();\\n1134:             elapsed = time.milliseconds() - nowMs;\\n1135:             if (elapsed >= maxWaitMs) {\\n1136:                 final String errorMessage = getErrorMessage(partitionsCount, topic, partition, maxWaitMs);\\n1137:                 if (metadata.getError(topic) != null && metadata.getError(topic).exception() instanceof RetriableException) {\\n1138:                     throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage, metadata.getError(topic).exception());\\n1139:                 }\\n1140:                 throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage);\\n1141:             }\\n1142:             metadata.maybeThrowExceptionForTopic(topic);\\n1143:             remainingWaitMs = maxWaitMs - elapsed;\\n1144:             partitionsCount = cluster.partitionCountForTopic(topic);\\n1145:         } while (partitionsCount == null || (partition != null && partition >= partitionsCount));\\n1146: \\n1147:         producerMetrics.recordMetadataWait(time.nanoseconds() - nowNanos);\\n1148: \\n1149:         return new ClusterAndWaitTime(cluster, elapsed);\\n1150:     }\\n1151: \\n1152:     private String getErrorMessage(Integer partitionsCount, String topic, Integer partition, long maxWaitMs) {\\n1153:         return partitionsCount == null ?\\n1154:             String.format(\\\"Topic %s not present in metadata after %d ms.\\\",\\n1155:                 topic, maxWaitMs) :\\n1156:             String.format(\\\"Partition %d of topic %s with partition count %d is not present in metadata after %d ms.\\\",\\n1157:                 partition, topic, partitionsCount, maxWaitMs);\\n1158:     }\\n1159:     /**\\n1160:      * Validate that the record size isn't too large\\n1161:      */\\n1162:     private void ensureValidRecordSize(int size) {\\n1163:         if (size > maxRequestSize)\\n1164:             throw new RecordTooLargeException(\\\"The message is \\\" + size +\\n1165:                     \\\" bytes when serialized which is larger than \\\" + maxRequestSize + \\\", which is the value of the \\\" +\\n1166:                     ProducerConfig.MAX_REQUEST_SIZE_CONFIG + \\\" configuration.\\\");\\n1167:         if (size > totalMemorySize)\\n1168:             throw new RecordTooLargeException(\\\"The message is \\\" + size +\\n1169:                     \\\" bytes when serialized which is larger than the total memory buffer you have configured with the \\\" +\\n1170:                     ProducerConfig.BUFFER_MEMORY_CONFIG +\\n1171:                     \\\" configuration.\\\");\\n1172:     }\\n1173: \\n1174:     /**\\n1175:      * Invoking this method makes all buffered records immediately available to send (even if <code>linger.ms</code> is\\n1176:      * greater than 0) and blocks on the completion of the requests associated with these records. The post-condition\\n1177:      * of <code>flush()</code> is that any previously sent record will have completed (e.g. <code>Future.isDone() == true</code>\\n1178:      * and callbacks passed to {@link #send(ProducerRecord,Callback)} have been called).\\n1179:      * A request is considered completed when it is successfully acknowledged\\n1180:      * according to the <code>acks</code> configuration you have specified or else it results in an error.\\n1181:      * <p>\\n1182:      * Other threads can continue sending records while one thread is blocked waiting for a flush call to complete,\\n1183:      * however no guarantee is made about the completion of records sent after the flush call begins.\\n1184:      * <p>\\n1185:      * This method can be useful when consuming from some input system and producing into Kafka. The <code>flush()</code> call\\n1186:      * gives a convenient way to ensure all previously sent messages have actually completed.\\n1187:      * <p>\\n1188:      * This example shows how to consume from one Kafka topic and produce to another Kafka topic:\\n1189:      * <pre>\\n1190:      * {@code\\n1191:      * for(ConsumerRecord<String, String> record: consumer.poll(100))\\n1192:      *     producer.send(new ProducerRecord(\\\"my-topic\\\", record.key(), record.value());\\n1193:      * producer.flush();\\n1194:      * consumer.commitSync();\\n1195:      * }\\n1196:      * </pre>\\n1197:      *\\n1198:      * Note that the above example may drop records if the produce request fails. If we want to ensure that this does not occur\\n1199:      * we need to set <code>retries=&lt;large_number&gt;</code> in our config.\\n1200:      * </p>\\n1201:      * <p>\\n1202:      * Applications don't need to call this method for transactional producers, since the {@link #commitTransaction()} will\\n1203:      * flush all buffered records before performing the commit. This ensures that all the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)}\\n1204:      * calls made since the previous {@link #beginTransaction()} are completed before the commit.\\n1205:      * </p>\\n1206:      * <p>\\n1207:      * <b>Important:</b> This method must not be called from within the callback provided to\\n1208:      * {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)}. Invoking <code>flush()</code> in this context will result in a\\n1209:      * {@link KafkaException} being thrown, as it will cause a deadlock.\\n1210:      * </p>\\n1211:      *\\n1212:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked\\n1213:      * @throws KafkaException If the method is invoked inside a {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)} callback\\n1214:      */\\n1215:     @Override\\n1216:     public void flush() {\\n1217:         if (Thread.currentThread() == this.ioThread) {\\n1218:             log.error(\\\"KafkaProducer.flush() invocation inside a callback is not permitted because it may lead to deadlock.\\\");\\n1219:             throw new KafkaException(\\\"KafkaProducer.flush() invocation inside a callback is not permitted because it may lead to deadlock.\\\");\\n1220:         }\\n1221: \\n1222:         log.trace(\\\"Flushing accumulated records in producer.\\\");\\n1223: \\n1224:         long start = time.nanoseconds();\\n1225:         this.accumulator.beginFlush();\\n1226:         this.sender.wakeup();\\n1227:         try {\\n1228:             this.accumulator.awaitFlushCompletion();\\n1229:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\\n1230:             throw new InterruptException(\\\"Flush interrupted.\\\", e);\\n1231:         } finally {\\n1232:             producerMetrics.recordFlush(time.nanoseconds() - start);\\n1233:         }\\n1234:     }\\n1235: \\n1236:     /**\\n1237:      * Get the partition metadata for the given topic. This can be used for custom partitioning.\\n1238:      * @throws AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\\n1239:      * @throws AuthorizationException if not authorized to the specified topic. See the exception for more details\\n1240:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\\n1241:      * @throws TimeoutException if metadata could not be refreshed within {@code max.block.ms}\\n1242:      * @throws KafkaException for all Kafka-related exceptions, including the case where this method is called after producer close\\n1243:      */\\n1244:     @Override\\n1245:     public List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic) {\\n1246:         Objects.requireNonNull(topic, \\\"topic cannot be null\\\");\\n1247:         try {\\n1248:             return waitOnMetadata(topic, null, time.milliseconds(), maxBlockTimeMs).cluster.partitionsForTopic(topic);\\n1249:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\\n1250:             throw new InterruptException(e);\\n1251:         }\\n1252:     }\\n1253: \\n1254:     /**\\n1255:      * Get the full set of internal metrics maintained by the producer.\\n1256:      */\\n1257:     @Override\\n1258:     public Map<MetricName, ? extends Metric> metrics() {\\n1259:         return Collections.unmodifiableMap(this.metrics.metrics());\\n1260:     }\\n1261: \\n1262: \\n1263:     /**\\n1264:      * Add the provided application metric for subscription.\\n1265:      * This metric will be added to this client's metrics\\n1266:      * that are available for subscription and sent as\\n1267:      * telemetry data to the broker.\\n1268:      * The provided metric must map to an OTLP metric data point\\n1269:      * type in the OpenTelemetry v1 metrics protobuf message types.\\n1270:      * Specifically, the metric should be one of the following:\\n1271:      * <ul>\\n1272:      *  <li>\\n1273:      *     `Sum`: Monotonic total count meter (Counter). Suitable for metrics like total number of X, e.g., total bytes sent.\\n1274:      *  </li>\\n1275:      *  <li>\\n1276:      *     `Gauge`: Non-monotonic current value meter (UpDownCounter). Suitable for metrics like current value of Y, e.g., current queue count.\\n1277:      *  </li>\\n1278:      * </ul>\\n1279:      * Metrics not matching these types are silently ignored.\\n1280:      * Executing this method for a previously registered metric is a benign operation and results in updating that metrics entry.\\n1281:      *\\n1282:      * @param metric The application metric to register\\n1283:      */\\n1284:     @Override\\n1285:     public void registerMetricForSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\\n1286:         if (!metrics().containsKey(metric.metricName())) {\\n1287:             clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(reporter -> reporter.metricChange(metric));\\n1288:         }  else {\\n1289:             log.debug(\\\"Skipping registration for metric {}. Existing producer metrics cannot be overwritten.\\\", metric.metricName());\\n1290:         }\\n1291:     }\\n1292: \\n1293:     /**\\n1294:      * Remove the provided application metric for subscription.\\n1295:      * This metric is removed from this client's metrics\\n1296:      * and will not be available for subscription any longer.\\n1297:      * Executing this method with a metric that has not been registered is a\\n1298:      * benign operation and does not result in any action taken (no-op).\\n1299:      *\\n1300:      * @param metric The application metric to remove\\n1301:      */\\n1302:     @Override\\n1303:     public void unregisterMetricFromSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\\n1304:         if (!metrics().containsKey(metric.metricName())) {\\n1305:             clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(reporter -> reporter.metricRemoval(metric));\\n1306:         } else {\\n1307:             log.debug(\\\"Skipping unregistration for metric {}. Existing producer metrics cannot be removed.\\\", metric.metricName());\\n1308:         }\\n1309:     }\\n1310: \\n1311:     /**\\n1312:      * Determines the client's unique client instance ID used for telemetry. This ID is unique to\\n1313:      * this specific client instance and will not change after it is initially generated.\\n1314:      * The ID is useful for correlating client operations with telemetry sent to the broker and\\n1315:      * to its eventual monitoring destinations.\\n1316:      * <p>\\n1317:      * If telemetry is enabled, this will first require a connection to the cluster to generate\\n1318:      * the unique client instance ID. This method waits up to {@code timeout} for the producer\\n1319:      * client to complete the request.\\n1320:      * <p>\\n1321:      * Client telemetry is controlled by the {@link ProducerConfig#ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_CONFIG}\\n1322:      * configuration option.\\n1323:      *\\n1324:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for producer client to determine its client instance ID.\\n1325:      *                The value must be non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not\\n1326:      *                wait for the initial request to complete if it hasn't already.\\n1327:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\\n1328:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to determine the client\\n1329:      *                        instance ID, though this error does not necessarily imply the\\n1330:      *                        producer client is otherwise unusable.\\n1331:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the {@code timeout} is negative.\\n1332:      * @throws IllegalStateException If telemetry is not enabled ie, config `{@code enable.metrics.push}`\\n1333:      *                               is set to `{@code false}`.\\n1334:      * @return The client's assigned instance id used for metrics collection.\\n1335:      */\\n1336:     @Override\\n1337:     public Uuid clientInstanceId(Duration timeout) {\\n1338:         if (clientTelemetryReporter.isEmpty()) {\\n1339:             throw new IllegalStateException(\\\"Telemetry is not enabled. Set config `\\\" + ProducerConfig.ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_CONFIG + \\\"` to `true`.\\\");\\n1340:         }\\n1341: \\n1342:         return ClientTelemetryUtils.fetchClientInstanceId(clientTelemetryReporter.get(), timeout);\\n1343:     }\\n1344: \\n1345:     /**\\n1346:      * Close this producer. This method blocks until all previously sent requests complete.\\n1347:      * This method is equivalent to <code>close(Long.MAX_VALUE, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)</code>.\\n1348:      * <p>\\n1349:      * <strong>If close() is called from {@link Callback}, a warning message will be logged and close(0, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\\n1350:      * will be called instead. We do this because the sender thread would otherwise try to join itself and\\n1351:      * block forever.</strong>\\n1352:      * <p>\\n1353:      *\\n1354:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\\n1355:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to close the client, this error should be treated\\n1356:      *                        as fatal and indicate the client is no longer usable.\\n1357:      */\\n1358:     @Override\\n1359:     public void close() {\\n1360:         close(Duration.ofMillis(Long.MAX_VALUE));\\n1361:     }\\n1362: \\n1363:     /**\\n1364:      * This method waits up to <code>timeout</code> for the producer to complete the sending of all incomplete requests.\\n1365:      * <p>\\n1366:      * If the producer is unable to complete all requests before the timeout expires, this method will fail\\n1367:      * any unsent and unacknowledged records immediately. It will also abort the ongoing transaction if it's not\\n1368:      * already completing.\\n1369:      * <p>\\n1370:      * If invoked from within a {@link Callback} this method will not block and will be equivalent to\\n1371:      * <code>close(Duration.ofMillis(0))</code>. This is done since no further sending will happen while\\n1372:      * blocking the I/O thread of the producer.\\n1373:      *\\n1374:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for producer to complete any pending requests. The value should be\\n1375:      *                non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not wait for pending send requests to complete.\\n1376:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\\n1377:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to close the client, this error should be treated\\n1378:      *                        as fatal and indicate the client is no longer usable.\\n1379:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the <code>timeout</code> is negative.\\n1380:      *\\n1381: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java%3FL1100-1380\"}",

nowNanos);\n1148: \n1149:         return new ClusterAndWaitTime(cluster, elapsed);\n1150:     }\n1151: \n1152:     private String getErrorMessage(Integer partitionsCount, String topic, Integer partition, long maxWaitMs) {\n1153:         return partitionsCount == null ?\n1154:             String.format(\"Topic %s not present in metadata after %d ms.\",\n1155:                 topic, maxWaitMs) :\n1156:             String.format(\"Partition %d of topic %s with partition count %d is not present in metadata after %d ms.\",\n1157:                 partition, topic, partitionsCount, maxWaitMs);\n1158:     }\n1159:     /**\n1160:      * Validate that the record size isn't too large\n1161:      */\n1162:     private void ensureValidRecordSize(int size) {\n1163:         if (size > maxRequestSize)\n1164:             throw new RecordTooLargeException(\"The message is \" + size +\n1165:                     \" bytes when serialized which is larger than \" + maxRequestSize + \", which is the value of the \" +\n1166:                     ProducerConfig.MAX_REQUEST_SIZE_CONFIG + \" configuration.\");\n1167:         if (size > totalMemorySize)\n1168:             throw new RecordTooLargeException(\"The message is \" + size +\n1169:                     \" bytes when serialized which is larger than the total memory buffer you have configured with the \" +\n1170:                     ProducerConfig.BUFFER_MEMORY_CONFIG +\n1171:                     \" configuration.\");\n1172:     }\n1173: \n1174:     /**\n1175:      * Invoking this method makes all buffered records immediately available to send (even if <code>linger.ms</code> is\n1176:      * greater than 0) and blocks on the completion of the requests associated with these records. The post-condition\n1177:      * of <code>flush()</code> is that any previously sent record will have completed (e.g. <code>Future.isDone() == true</code>\n1178:      * and callbacks passed to {@link #send(ProducerRecord,Callback)} have been called).\n1179:      * A request is considered completed when it is successfully acknowledged\n1180:      * according to the <code>acks</code> configuration you have specified or else it results in an error.\n1181:      * <p>\n1182:      * Other threads can continue sending records while one thread is blocked waiting for a flush call to complete,\n1183:      * however no guarantee is made about the completion of records sent after the flush call begins.\n1184:      * <p>\n1185:      * This method can be useful when consuming from some input system and producing into Kafka. The <code>flush()</code> call\n1186:      * gives a convenient way to ensure all previously sent messages have actually completed.\n1187:      * <p>\n1188:      * This example shows how to consume from one Kafka topic and produce to another Kafka topic:\n1189:      * <pre>\n1190:      * {@code\n1191:      * for(ConsumerRecord<String, String> record: consumer.poll(100))\n1192:      *     producer.send(new ProducerRecord(\"my-topic\", record.key(), record.value());\n1193:      * producer.flush();\n1194:      * consumer.commitSync();\n1195:      * }\n1196:      * </pre>\n1197:      *\n1198:      * Note that the above example may drop records if the produce request fails. If we want to ensure that this does not occur\n1199:      * we need to set <code>retries=&lt;large_number&gt;</code> in our config.\n1200:      * </p>\n1201:      * <p>\n1202:      * Applications don't need to call this method for transactional producers, since the {@link #commitTransaction()} will\n1203:      * flush all buffered records before performing the commit. This ensures that all the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)}\n1204:      * calls made since the previous {@link #beginTransaction()} are completed before the commit.\n1205:      * </p>\n1206:      * <p>\n1207:      * <b>Important:</b> This method must not be called from within the callback provided to\n1208:      * {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)}. Invoking <code>flush()</code> in this context will result in a\n1209:      * {@link KafkaException} being thrown, as it will cause a deadlock.\n1210:      * </p>\n1211:      *\n1212:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked\n1213:      * @throws KafkaException If the method is invoked inside a {@link #send(ProducerRecord, Callback)} callback\n1214:      */\n1215:     @Override\n1216:     public void flush() {\n1217:         if (Thread.currentThread() == this.ioThread) {\n1218:             log.error(\"KafkaProducer.flush() invocation inside a callback is not permitted because it may lead to deadlock.\");\n1219:             throw new KafkaException(\"KafkaProducer.flush() invocation inside a callback is not permitted because it may lead to deadlock.\");\n1220:         }\n1221: \n1222:         log.trace(\"Flushing accumulated records in producer.\");\n1223: \n1224:         long start = time.nanoseconds();\n1225:         this.accumulator.beginFlush();\n1226:         this.sender.wakeup();\n1227:         try {\n1228:             this.accumulator.awaitFlushCompletion();\n1229:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n1230:             throw new InterruptException(\"Flush interrupted.\", e);\n1231:         } finally {\n1232:             producerMetrics.recordFlush(time.nanoseconds() - start);\n1233:         }\n1234:     }\n1235: \n1236:     /**\n1237:      * Get the partition metadata for the given topic. This can be used for custom partitioning.\n1238:      * @throws AuthenticationException if authentication fails. See the exception for more details\n1239:      * @throws AuthorizationException if not authorized to the specified topic. See the exception for more details\n1240:      * @throws InterruptException if the thread is interrupted while blocked\n1241:      * @throws TimeoutException if metadata could not be refreshed within {@code max.block.ms}\n1242:      * @throws KafkaException for all Kafka-related exceptions, including the case where this method is called after producer close\n1243:      */\n1244:     @Override\n1245:     public List<PartitionInfo> partitionsFor(String topic) {\n1246:         Objects.requireNonNull(topic, \"topic cannot be null\");\n1247:         try {\n1248:             return waitOnMetadata(topic, null, time.milliseconds(), maxBlockTimeMs).cluster.partitionsForTopic(topic);\n1249:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n1250:             throw new InterruptException(e);\n1251:         }\n1252:     }\n1253: \n1254:     /**\n1255:      * Get the full set of internal metrics maintained by the producer.\n1256:      */\n1257:     @Override\n1258:     public Map<MetricName, ? extends Metric> metrics() {\n1259:         return Collections.unmodifiableMap(this.metrics.metrics());\n1260:     }\n1261: \n1262: \n1263:     /**\n1264:      * Add the provided application metric for subscription.\n1265:      * This metric will be added to this client's metrics\n1266:      * that are available for subscription and sent as\n1267:      * telemetry data to the broker.\n1268:      * The provided metric must map to an OTLP metric data point\n1269:      * type in the OpenTelemetry v1 metrics protobuf message types.\n1270:      * Specifically, the metric should be one of the following:\n1271:      * <ul>\n1272:      *  <li>\n1273:      *     `Sum`: Monotonic total count meter (Counter). Suitable for metrics like total number of X, e.g., total bytes sent.\n1274:      *  </li>\n1275:      *  <li>\n1276:      *     `Gauge`: Non-monotonic current value meter (UpDownCounter). Suitable for metrics like current value of Y, e.g., current queue count.\n1277:      *  </li>\n1278:      * </ul>\n1279:      * Metrics not matching these types are silently ignored.\n1280:      * Executing this method for a previously registered metric is a benign operation and results in updating that metrics entry.\n1281:      *\n1282:      * @param metric The application metric to register\n1283:      */\n1284:     @Override\n1285:     public void registerMetricForSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\n1286:         if (!metrics().containsKey(metric.metricName())) {\n1287:             clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(reporter -> reporter.metricChange(metric));\n1288:         }  else {\n1289:             log.debug(\"Skipping registration for metric {}. Existing producer metrics cannot be overwritten.\", metric.metricName());\n1290:         }\n1291:     }\n1292: \n1293:     /**\n1294:      * Remove the provided application metric for subscription.\n1295:      * This metric is removed from this client's metrics\n1296:      * and will not be available for subscription any longer.\n1297:      * Executing this method with a metric that has not been registered is a\n1298:      * benign operation and does not result in any action taken (no-op).\n1299:      *\n1300:      * @param metric The application metric to remove\n1301:      */\n1302:     @Override\n1303:     public void unregisterMetricFromSubscription(KafkaMetric metric) {\n1304:         if (!metrics().containsKey(metric.metricName())) {\n1305:             clientTelemetryReporter.ifPresent(reporter -> reporter.metricRemoval(metric));\n1306:         } else {\n1307:             log.debug(\"Skipping unregistration for metric {}. Existing producer metrics cannot be removed.\", metric.metricName());\n1308:         }\n1309:     }\n1310: \n1311:     /**\n1312:      * Determines the client's unique client instance ID used for telemetry. This ID is unique to\n1313:      * this specific client instance and will not change after it is initially generated.\n1314:      * The ID is useful for correlating client operations with telemetry sent to the broker and\n1315:      * to its eventual monitoring destinations.\n1316:      * <p>\n1317:      * If telemetry is enabled, this will first require a connection to the cluster to generate\n1318:      * the unique client instance ID. This method waits up to {@code timeout} for the producer\n1319:      * client to complete the request.\n1320:      * <p>\n1321:      * Client telemetry is controlled by the {@link ProducerConfig#ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_CONFIG}\n1322:      * configuration option.\n1323:      *\n1324:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for producer client to determine its client instance ID.\n1325:      *                The value must be non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not\n1326:      *                wait for the initial request to complete if it hasn't already.\n1327:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\n1328:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to determine the client\n1329:      *                        instance ID, though this error does not necessarily imply the\n1330:      *                        producer client is otherwise unusable.\n1331:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the {@code timeout} is negative.\n1332:      * @throws IllegalStateException If telemetry is not enabled ie, config `{@code enable.metrics.push}`\n1333:      *                               is set to `{@code false}`.\n1334:      * @return The client's assigned instance id used for metrics collection.\n1335:      */\n1336:     @Override\n1337:     public Uuid clientInstanceId(Duration timeout) {\n1338:         if (clientTelemetryReporter.isEmpty()) {\n1339:             throw new IllegalStateException(\"Telemetry is not enabled. Set config `\" + ProducerConfig.ENABLE_METRICS_PUSH_CONFIG + \"` to `true`.\");\n1340:         }\n1341: \n1342:         return ClientTelemetryUtils.fetchClientInstanceId(clientTelemetryReporter.get(), timeout);\n1343:     }\n1344: \n1345:     /**\n1346:      * Close this producer. This method blocks until all previously sent requests complete.\n1347:      * This method is equivalent to <code>close(Long.MAX_VALUE, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)</code>.\n1348:      * <p>\n1349:      * <strong>If close() is called from {@link Callback}, a warning message will be logged and close(0, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\n1350:      * will be called instead. We do this because the sender thread would otherwise try to join itself and\n1351:      * block forever.</strong>\n1352:      * <p>\n1353:      *\n1354:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\n1355:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to close the client, this error should be treated\n1356:      *                        as fatal and indicate the client is no longer usable.\n1357:      */\n1358:     @Override\n1359:     public void close() {\n1360:         close(Duration.ofMillis(Long.MAX_VALUE));\n1361:     }\n1362: \n1363:     /**\n1364:      * This method waits up to <code>timeout</code> for the producer to complete the sending of all incomplete requests.\n1365:      * <p>\n1366:      * If the producer is unable to complete all requests before the timeout expires, this method will fail\n1367:      * any unsent and unacknowledged records immediately. It will also abort the ongoing transaction if it's not\n1368:      * already completing.\n1369:      * <p>\n1370:      * If invoked from within a {@link Callback} this method will not block and will be equivalent to\n1371:      * <code>close(Duration.ofMillis(0))</code>. This is done since no further sending will happen while\n1372:      * blocking the I/O thread of the producer.\n1373:      *\n1374:      * @param timeout The maximum time to wait for producer to complete any pending requests. The value should be\n1375:      *                non-negative. Specifying a timeout of zero means do not wait for pending send requests to complete.\n1376:      * @throws InterruptException If the thread is interrupted while blocked.\n1377:      * @throws KafkaException If an unexpected error occurs while trying to close the client, this error should be treated\n1378:      *                        as fatal and indicate the client is no longer usable.\n1379:      * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the <code>timeout</code> is negative.\n1380:      *\n1381: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java%3FL1100-1380"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"350:             maybeAbortBatches(exception);\\n351:             transactionManager.transitionToUninitialized(exception);\\n352:             return true;\\n353:         }\\n354:         return false;\\n355:     }\\n356: \\n357:     private long sendProducerData(long now) {\\n358:         MetadataSnapshot metadataSnapshot = metadata.fetchMetadataSnapshot();\\n359:         // get the list of partitions with data ready to send\\n360:         RecordAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult result = this.accumulator.ready(metadataSnapshot, now);\\n361: \\n362:         // if there are any partitions whose leaders are not known yet, force metadata update\\n363:         if (!result.unknownLeaderTopics.isEmpty()) {\\n364:             // The set of topics with unknown leader contains topics with leader election pending as well as\\n365:             // topics which may have expired. Add the topic again to metadata to ensure it is included\\n366:             // and request metadata update, since there are messages to send to the topic.\\n367:             for (String topic : result.unknownLeaderTopics)\\n368:                 this.metadata.add(topic, now);\\n369: \\n370:             log.debug(\\\"Requesting metadata update due to unknown leader topics from the batched records: {}\\\",\\n371:                 result.unknownLeaderTopics);\\n372:             this.metadata.requestUpdate(false);\\n373:         }\\n374: \\n375:         // remove any nodes we aren't ready to send to\\n376:         Iterator<Node> iter = result.readyNodes.iterator();\\n377:         long notReadyTimeout = Long.MAX_VALUE;\\n378:         while (iter.hasNext()) {\\n379:             Node node = iter.next();\\n380:             if (!this.client.ready(node, now)) {\\n381:                 // Update just the readyTimeMs of the latency stats, so that it moves forward\\n382:                 // every time the batch is ready (then the difference between readyTimeMs and\\n383:                 // drainTimeMs would represent how long data is waiting for the node).\\n384:                 this.accumulator.updateNodeLatencyStats(node.id(), now, false);\\n385:                 iter.remove();\\n386:                 notReadyTimeout = Math.min(notReadyTimeout, this.client.pollDelayMs(node, now));\\n387:             } else {\\n388:                 // Update both readyTimeMs and drainTimeMs, this would \\\"reset\\\" the node\\n389:                 // latency.\\n390:                 this.accumulator.updateNodeLatencyStats(node.id(), now, true);\\n391:             }\\n392:         }\\n393: \\n394:         // create produce requests\\n395:         Map<Integer, List<ProducerBatch>> batches = this.accumulator.drain(metadataSnapshot, result.readyNodes, this.maxRequestSize, now);\\n396:         addToInflightBatches(batches);\\n397:         if (guaranteeMessageOrder) {\\n398:             // Mute all the partitions drained\\n399:             for (List<ProducerBatch> batchList : batches.values()) {\\n400:                 for (ProducerBatch batch : batchList)\\n401:                     this.accumulator.mutePartition(batch.topicPartition);\\n402:             }\\n403:         }\\n404: \\n405:         accumulator.resetNextBatchExpiryTime();\\n406:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredInflightBatches = getExpiredInflightBatches(now);\\n407:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches = this.accumulator.expiredBatches(now);\\n408:         expiredBatches.addAll(expiredInflightBatches);\\n409: \\n410:         // Reset the producer id if an expired batch has previously been sent to the broker. Also update the metrics\\n411:         // for expired batches. see the documentation of @TransactionState.resetIdempotentProducerId to understand why\\n412:         // we need to reset the producer id here.\\n413:         if (!expiredBatches.isEmpty())\\n414:             log.trace(\\\"Expired {} batches in accumulator\\\", expiredBatches.size());\\n415:         for (ProducerBatch expiredBatch : expiredBatches) {\\n416:             String errorMessage = \\\"Expiring \\\" + expiredBatch.recordCount + \\\" record(s) for \\\" + expiredBatch.topicPartition\\n417:                 + \\\":\\\" + (now - expiredBatch.createdMs) + \\\" ms has passed since batch creation\\\";\\n418:             failBatch(expiredBatch, new TimeoutException(errorMessage), false);\\n419:             if (transactionManager != null && expiredBatch.inRetry()) {\\n420:                 // This ensures that no new batches are drained until the current in flight batches are fully resolved.\\n421: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java%3FL350-420\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "350:             maybeAbortBatches(exception);\n351:             transactionManager.transitionToUninitialized(exception);\n352:             return true;\n353:         }\n354:         return false;\n355:     }\n356: \n357:     private long sendProducerData(long now) {\n358:         MetadataSnapshot metadataSnapshot = metadata.fetchMetadataSnapshot();\n359:         // get the list of partitions with data ready to send\n360:         RecordAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult result = this.accumulator.ready(metadataSnapshot, now);\n361: \n362:         // if there are any partitions whose leaders are not known yet, force metadata update\n363:         if (!result.unknownLeaderTopics.isEmpty()) {\n364:             // The set of topics with unknown leader contains topics with leader election pending as well as\n365:             // topics which may have expired. Add the topic again to metadata to ensure it is included\n366:             // and request metadata update, since there are messages to send to the topic.\n367:             for (String topic : result.unknownLeaderTopics)\n368:                 this.metadata.add(topic, now);\n369: \n370:             log.debug(\"Requesting metadata update due to unknown leader topics from the batched records: {}\",\n371:                 result.unknownLeaderTopics);\n372:             this.metadata.requestUpdate(false);\n373:         }\n374: \n375:         // remove any nodes we aren't ready to send to\n376:         Iterator<Node> iter = result.readyNodes.iterator();\n377:         long notReadyTimeout = Long.MAX_VALUE;\n378:         while (iter.hasNext()) {\n379:             Node node = iter.next();\n380:             if (!this.client.ready(node, now)) {\n381:                 // Update just the readyTimeMs of the latency stats, so that it moves forward\n382:                 // every time the batch is ready (then the difference between readyTimeMs and\n383:                 // drainTimeMs would represent how long data is waiting for the node).\n384:                 this.accumulator.updateNodeLatencyStats(node.id(), now, false);\n385:                 iter.remove();\n386:                 notReadyTimeout = Math.min(notReadyTimeout, this.client.pollDelayMs(node, now));\n387:             } else {\n388:                 // Update both readyTimeMs and drainTimeMs, this would \"reset\" the node\n389:                 // latency.\n390:                 this.accumulator.updateNodeLatencyStats(node.id(), now, true);\n391:             }\n392:         }\n393: \n394:         // create produce requests\n395:         Map<Integer, List<ProducerBatch>> batches = this.accumulator.drain(metadataSnapshot, result.readyNodes, this.maxRequestSize, now);\n396:         addToInflightBatches(batches);\n397:         if (guaranteeMessageOrder) {\n398:             // Mute all the partitions drained\n399:             for (List<ProducerBatch> batchList : batches.values()) {\n400:                 for (ProducerBatch batch : batchList)\n401:                     this.accumulator.mutePartition(batch.topicPartition);\n402:             }\n403:         }\n404: \n405:         accumulator.resetNextBatchExpiryTime();\n406:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredInflightBatches = getExpiredInflightBatches(now);\n407:         List<ProducerBatch> expiredBatches = this.accumulator.expiredBatches(now);\n408:         expiredBatches.addAll(expiredInflightBatches);\n409: \n410:         // Reset the producer id if an expired batch has previously been sent to the broker. Also update the metrics\n411:         // for expired batches. see the documentation of @TransactionState.resetIdempotentProducerId to understand why\n412:         // we need to reset the producer id here.\n413:         if (!expiredBatches.isEmpty())\n414:             log.trace(\"Expired {} batches in accumulator\", expiredBatches.size());\n415:         for (ProducerBatch expiredBatch : expiredBatches) {\n416:             String errorMessage = \"Expiring \" + expiredBatch.recordCount + \" record(s) for \" + expiredBatch.topicPartition\n417:                 + \":\" + (now - expiredBatch.createdMs) + \" ms has passed since batch creation\";\n418:             failBatch(expiredBatch, new TimeoutException(errorMessage), false);\n419:             if (transactionManager != null && expiredBatch.inRetry()) {\n420:                 // This ensures that no new batches are drained until the current in flight batches are fully resolved.\n421: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java%3FL350-420"
  }
}

Input
{
  "endLine": 270,
  "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489",
  "startLine": 250
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"250:         // The queueSizes.length represents the number of all partitions in the topic and if we have\\n251:         // less than 2 partitions, there is no need to do adaptive logic.\\n252:         // If partitioner.availability.timeout.ms != 0, then partitions that experience high latencies\\n253:         // (greater than partitioner.availability.timeout.ms) may be excluded, the length represents\\n254:         // partitions that are not excluded.  If some partitions were excluded, we'd still want to\\n255:         // go through adaptive logic, even if we have one partition.\\n256:         // See also RecordAccumulator#partitionReady where the queueSizes are built.\\n257:         if (length < 1 || queueSizes.length < 2) {\\n258:             log.trace(\\\"The number of partitions is too small: available={}, all={}, not using adaptive for topic {}\\\",\\n259:                     length, queueSizes.length, topic);\\n260:             partitionLoadStats = null;\\n261:             return;\\n262:         }\\n263: \\n264:         // We build cumulative frequency table from the queue sizes in place.  At the beginning\\n265:         // each entry contains queue size, then we invert it (so it represents the frequency)\\n266:         // and convert to a running sum.  Then a uniformly distributed random variable\\n267:         // in the range [0..last) would map to a partition with weighted probability.\\n268:         // Example: suppose we have 3 partitions with the corresponding queue sizes:\\n269:         //  0 3 1\\n270:         // Then we can invert them by subtracting the queue size from the max queue size + 1 = 4:\\n271: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java%3FL250-270\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "250:         // The queueSizes.length represents the number of all partitions in the topic and if we have\n251:         // less than 2 partitions, there is no need to do adaptive logic.\n252:         // If partitioner.availability.timeout.ms != 0, then partitions that experience high latencies\n253:         // (greater than partitioner.availability.timeout.ms) may be excluded, the length represents\n254:         // partitions that are not excluded.  If some partitions were excluded, we'd still want to\n255:         // go through adaptive logic, even if we have one partition.\n256:         // See also RecordAccumulator#partitionReady where the queueSizes are built.\n257:         if (length < 1 || queueSizes.length < 2) {\n258:             log.trace(\"The number of partitions is too small: available={}, all={}, not using adaptive for topic {}\",\n259:                     length, queueSizes.length, topic);\n260:             partitionLoadStats = null;\n261:             return;\n262:         }\n263: \n264:         // We build cumulative frequency table from the queue sizes in place.  At the beginning\n265:         // each entry contains queue size, then we invert it (so it represents the frequency)\n266:         // and convert to a running sum.  Then a uniformly distributed random variable\n267:         // in the range [0..last) would map to a partition with weighted probability.\n268:         // Example: suppose we have 3 partitions with the corresponding queue sizes:\n269:         //  0 3 1\n270:         // Then we can invert them by subtracting the queue size from the max queue size + 1 = 4:\n271: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java%3FL250-270"
  }
}

org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\\n36: \\n37: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Benchmark;\\n38: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.BenchmarkMode;\\n39: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Fork;\\n40: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Level;\\n41: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Measurement;\\n42: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Mode;\\n43: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.State;\\n48: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.TearDown;\\n49: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Warmup;\\n50: \\n51: import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;\\n52: import java.util.ArrayList;\\n53: import java.util.Collections;\\n54: import java.util.List;\\n55: import java.util.Map;\\n56: import java.util.Optional;\\n57: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;\\n58: import java.util.function.Function;\\n59: import java.util.stream.Collectors;\\n60: import java.util.stream.Stream;\\n61: \\n62: @State(Scope.Benchmark)\\n63: @Fork(value = 1)\\n64: @Warmup(iterations = 5)\\n65: @Measurement(iterations = 15)\\n66: @BenchmarkMode(Mode.AverageTime)\\n67: @OutputTimeUnit(TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\\n68: public class RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\\n69: \\n70:     private static final String TOPIC = \\\"test\\\";\\n71:     private static final int PARTITION = 0;\\n72:     private static final int BATCH_SIZE = 1024;\\n73:     private static final long TOTAL_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024;\\n74: \\n75:     @Param({\\\"5000\\\", \\\"10000\\\"})\\n76:     private int numRecords;\\n77: \\n78:     private RecordAccumulator accum;\\n79:     private Metrics metrics;\\n80:     private TopicPartition tp;\\n81:     private Time time;\\n82: \\n83:     @Setup(Level.Invocation)\\n84:     public void setup() throws InterruptedException {\\n85:         tp = new TopicPartition(TOPIC, PARTITION);\\n86:         time = new MockTime();\\n87:         metrics = new Metrics(time);\\n88: \\n89:         Cluster cluster = createTestCluster();\\n90:         accum = createRecordAccumulator();\\n91: \\n92:         appendRecords(cluster);\\n93:         prepareFlush();\\n94:     }\\n95: \\n96:     @TearDown(Level.Invocation)\\n97:     public void tearDown() {\\n98:         deallocateBatches();\\n99:         if (metrics != null) {\\n100:             metrics.close();\\n101:         }\\n102:     }\\n103: \\n104:     @Benchmark\\n105:     public void measureFlushCompletion() throws InterruptedException {\\n106:         accum.awaitFlushCompletion();\\n107:     }\\n108: \\n109:     private Cluster createTestCluster() {\\n110:         Node node = new Node(0, \\\"localhost\\\", 1111);\\n111:         MetadataResponse.PartitionMetadata partMetadata = new MetadataResponse.PartitionMetadata(\\n112:             Errors.NONE,\\n113:             tp,\\n114:             Optional.of(node.id()),\\n115:             Optional.empty(),\\n116:             null,\\n117:             null,\\n118:             null\\n119:         );\\n120: \\n121:         Map<Integer, Node> nodes = Stream.of(node).collect(Collectors.toMap(Node::id, Function.identity()));\\n122:         MetadataSnapshot metadataCache = new MetadataSnapshot(\\n123:             null,\\n124:             nodes,\\n125:             Collections.singletonList(partMetadata),\\n126:             Collections.emptySet(),\\n127:             Collections.emptySet(),\\n128:             Collections.emptySet(),\\n129:             null,\\n130:             Collections.emptyMap()\\n131:         );\\n132:         return metadataCache.cluster();\\n133:     }\\n134: \\n135:     private RecordAccumulator createRecordAccumulator() org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.Errors;\n30: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.DefaultRecordBatch;\n31: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.Record;\n32: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.MetadataResponse;\n33: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\n34: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.MockTime;\n35: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\n36: \n37: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Benchmark;\n38: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.BenchmarkMode;\n39: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Fork;\n40: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Level;\n41: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Measurement;\n42: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Mode;\n43: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.OutputTimeUnit;\n44: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Param;\n45: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Scope;\n46: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Setup;\n47: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.State;\n48: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.TearDown;\n49: import org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Warmup;\n50: \n51: import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;\n52: import java.util.ArrayList;\n53: import java.util.Collections;\n54: import java.util.List;\n55: import java.util.Map;\n56: import java.util.Optional;\n57: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;\n58: import java.util.function.Function;\n59: import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n60: import java.util.stream.Stream;\n61: \n62: @State(Scope.Benchmark)\n63: @Fork(value = 1)\n64: @Warmup(iterations = 5)\n65: @Measurement(iterations = 15)\n66: @BenchmarkMode(Mode.AverageTime)\n67: @OutputTimeUnit(TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\n68: public class RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n69: \n70:     private static final String TOPIC = \"test\";\n71:     private static final int PARTITION = 0;\n72:     private static final int BATCH_SIZE = 1024;\n73:     private static final long TOTAL_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024;\n74: \n75:     @Param({\"5000\", \"10000\"})\n76:     private int numRecords;\n77: \n78:     private RecordAccumulator accum;\n79:     private Metrics metrics;\n80:     private TopicPartition tp;\n81:     private Time time;\n82: \n83:     @Setup(Level.Invocation)\n84:     public void setup() throws InterruptedException {\n85:         tp = new TopicPartition(TOPIC, PARTITION);\n86:         time = new MockTime();\n87:         metrics = new Metrics(time);\n88: \n89:         Cluster cluster = createTestCluster();\n90:         accum = createRecordAccumulator();\n91: \n92:         appendRecords(cluster);\n93:         prepareFlush();\n94:     }\n95: \n96:     @TearDown(Level.Invocation)\n97:     public void tearDown() {\n98:         deallocateBatches();\n99:         if (metrics != null) {\n100:             metrics.close();\n101:         }\n102:     }\n103: \n104:     @Benchmark\n105:     public void measureFlushCompletion() throws InterruptedException {\n106:         accum.awaitFlushCompletion();\n107:     }\n108: \n109:     private Cluster createTestCluster() {\n110:         Node node = new Node(0, \"localhost\", 1111);\n111:         MetadataResponse.PartitionMetadata partMetadata = new MetadataResponse.PartitionMetadata(\n112:             Errors.NONE,\n113:             tp,\n114:             Optional.of(node.id()),\n115:             Optional.empty(),\n116:             null,\n117:             null,\n118:             null\n119:         );\n120: \n121:         Map<Integer, Node> nodes = Stream.of(node).collect(Collectors.toMap(Node::id, Function.identity()));\n122:         MetadataSnapshot metadataCache = new MetadataSnapshot(\n123:             null,\n124:             nodes,\n125:             Collections.singletonList(partMetadata),\n126:             Collections.emptySet(),\n127:             Collections.emptySet(),\n128:             Collections.emptySet(),\n129:             null,\n130:             Collections.emptyMap()\n131:         );\n132:         return metadataCache.cluster();\n133:     }\n134: \n135:     private RecordAccumulator createRecordAccumulator() {\n136:         return new RecordAccumulator(\n137:             new LogContext(),\n138:             BATCH_SIZE + DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD,\n139:             Compression.NONE,\n140:             Integer.MAX_VALUE,  // lingerMs\n141:             100L,               // retryBackoffMs\n142:             1000L,              // retryBackoffMaxMs\n143:             3200,               // deliveryTimeoutMs\n144:             metrics,\n145:             \"producer-metrics\",\n146:             time,\n147:             null,\n148:             new BufferPool(TOTAL_SIZE, BATCH_SIZE, metrics, time, \"producer-metrics\")\n149:         );\n150:     }\n151: \n152:     private void appendRecords(Cluster cluster) throws InterruptedException {\n153:         byte[] key = \"key\".getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);\n154:         byte[] value = \"value\".getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);\n155: \n156:         for (int i = 0; i < numRecords; i++) {\n157:             accum.append(\n158:                 TOPIC,\n159:                 PARTITION,\n160:                 0L,\n161:                 key,\n162:                 value,\n163:                 Record.EMPTY_HEADERS,\n164:                 null,\n165:                 1000L,\n166:                 time.milliseconds(),\n167:                 cluster\n168:             );\n169:         }\n170:     }\n171: \n172:     private void prepareFlush() {\n173:         accum.beginFlush();\n174: \n175:         // Complete all batches to mimic successful sends\n176:         List<ProducerBatch> batches = new ArrayList<>(accum.getDeque(tp));\n177:         for (ProducerBatch batch : batches) {\n178:             batch.complete(0L, time.milliseconds());\n179:         }\n180:     }\n181: \n182:     private void deallocateBatches() {\n183:         if (accum != null && tp != null) {\n184:             List<ProducerBatch> batches = new ArrayList<>(accum.getDeque(tp));\n185:             for (ProducerBatch batch : batches) {\n186:                 accum.deallocate(batch);\n187:             }\n188:         }\n189:     }\n190: }\n191: ",

46. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\n\npackage org.apache.kafka.jmh.producer;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.clients.MetadataSnapshot;\nimport org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BufferPool;\nimport org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.ProducerBatch;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.Cluster;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.Node;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.Metrics;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.Errors;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.DefaultRecordBatch;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.Record;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.MetadataResponse;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.MockTime;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\n\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Benchmark;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.BenchmarkMode;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Fork;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Level;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Measurement;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Mode;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.OutputTimeUnit;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Param;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Scope;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Setup;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.State;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.TearDown;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Warmup;\n\nimport java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Collections;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;\nimport java.util.function.Function;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\nimport java.util.stream.Stream;\n\n@State(Scope.Benchmark)\n@Fork(value = 1)\n@Warmup(iterations = 5)\n@Measurement(iterations = 15)\n@BenchmarkMode(Mode.AverageTime)\n@OutputTimeUnit(TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\npublic class BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n\n    private static final String TOPIC = \"test\";\n    private static final int PARTITION = 0;\n    private static final int BATCH_SIZE = 1024;\n    private static final long TOTAL_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024;\n\n    @Param({\"5000\", \"10000\"})\n    private int numRecords;\n\n    private BatchAccumulator accum;\n    private Metrics metrics;\n    private TopicPartition tp;\n    private Time time;\n\n    @Setup(Level.Invocation)\n    public void setup() throws InterruptedException {\n        tp = new TopicPartition(TOPIC, PARTITION);\n        time = new MockTime();\n        metrics = new Metrics(time);\n\n        Cluster cluster = createTestCluster();\n        accum = createBatchAccumulator();\n\n        appendRecords(cluster);\n        prepareFlush();\n    }\n\n    @TearDown(Level.Invocation)\n    public void tearDown() {\n        deallocateBatches();\n        if (metrics != null) {\n            metrics.close();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Benchmark\n    public void measureFlushCompletion() throws InterruptedException {\n        accum.awaitFlushCompletion();\n    }\n\n    private Cluster createTestCluster() {\n        Node node = new Node(0, \"localhost\", 1111);\n        MetadataResponse.PartitionMetadata partMetadata = new MetadataResponse.PartitionMetadata(\n            Errors.NONE,\n            tp,\n            Optional.of(node.id()),\n            Optional.empty(),\n            null,\n            null,\n            null\n        );\n\n        Map<Integer, Node> nodes = Stream.of(node).collect(Collectors.toMap(Node::id, Function.identity()));\n        MetadataSnapshot metadataCache = new MetadataSnapshot(\n            null,\n            nodes,\n            Collections.singletonList(partMetadata),\n            Collections.emptySet(),\n            Collections.emptySet(),\n            Collections.emptySet(),\n            null,\n            Collections.emptyMap()\n        );\n        return metadataCache.cluster();\n    }\n\n    private BatchAccumulator createBatchAccumulator() {\n        return new BatchAccumulator(\n            new LogContext(),\n            BATCH_SIZE + DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD,\n            Compression.NONE,\n            Integer.MAX_VALUE,  // lingerMs\n            100L,               // retryBackoffMs\n            1000L,              // retryBackoffMaxMs\n            3200,               // deliveryTimeoutMs\n            metrics,\n            \"producer-metrics\",\n            time,\n            null,\n            new BufferPool(TOTAL_SIZE, BATCH_SIZE, metrics, time, \"producer-metrics\")\n        );\n    }\n\n    private void appendRecords(Cluster cluster) throws InterruptedException {\n        byte[] key = \"key\".getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);\n        byte[] value = \"value\".getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);\n\n        for (int i = 0; i < numRecords; i++) {\n            accum.append(\n                TOPIC,\n                PARTITION,\n                0L,\n                key,\n                value,\n                Record.EMPTY_HEADERS,\n                null,\n                1000L,\n                time.milliseconds(),\n                cluster\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void prepareFlush() {\n        accum.beginFlush();\n\n        // Complete all batches to mimic successful sends\n        List<ProducerBatch> batches = new ArrayList<>(accum.getDeque(tp));\n        for (ProducerBatch batch : batches) {\n            batch.complete(0L, time.milliseconds());\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void deallocateBatches() {\n        if (accum != null && tp != null) {\n            List<ProducerBatch> batches = new ArrayList<>(accum.getDeque(tp));\n            for (ProducerBatch batch : batches) {\n                accum.deallocate(batch);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java"
}
Output
{
  "content": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\n\npackage org.apache.kafka.jmh.producer;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.clients.MetadataSnapshot;\nimport org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BufferPool;\nimport org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.ProducerBatch;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.Cluster;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.Node;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.TopicPartition;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.Metrics;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.protocol.Errors;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.DefaultRecordBatch;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.Record;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.requests.MetadataResponse;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.MockTime;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\n\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Benchmark;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.BenchmarkMode;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Fork;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Level;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Measurement;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Mode;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.OutputTimeUnit;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Param;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Scope;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Setup;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.State;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.TearDown;\nimport org.openjdk.jmh.annotations.Warmup;\n\nimport java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Collections;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;\nimport java.util.function.Function;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\nimport java.util.stream.Stream;\n\n@State(Scope.Benchmark)\n@Fork(value = 1)\n@Warmup(iterations = 5)\n@Measurement(iterations = 15)\n@BenchmarkMode(Mode.AverageTime)\n@OutputTimeUnit(TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)\npublic class BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n\n    private static final String TOPIC = \"test\";\n    private static final int PARTITION = 0;\n    private static final int BATCH_SIZE = 1024;\n    private static final long TOTAL_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024;\n\n    @Param({\"5000\", \"10000\"})\n    private int numRecords;\n\n    private BatchAccumulator accum;\n    private Metrics metrics;\n    private TopicPartition tp;\n    private Time time;\n\n    @Setup(Level.Invocation)\n    public void setup() throws InterruptedException {\n        tp = new TopicPartition(TOPIC, PARTITION);\n        time = new MockTime();\n        metrics = new Metrics(time);\n\n        Cluster cluster = createTestCluster();\n        accum = createBatchAccumulator();\n\n        appendRecords(cluster);\n        prepareFlush();\n    }\n\n    @TearDown(Level.Invocation)\n    public void tearDown() {\n        deallocateBatches();\n        if (metrics != null) {\n            metrics.close();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Benchmark\n    public void measureFlushCompletion() throws InterruptedException {\n        accum.awaitFlushCompletion();\n    }\n\n    private Cluster createTestCluster() {\n        Node node = new Node(0, \"localhost\", 1111);\n        MetadataResponse.PartitionMetadata partMetadata = new MetadataResponse.PartitionMetadata(\n            Errors.NONE,\n            tp,\n            Optional.of(node.id()),\n            Optional.empty(),\n            null,\n            null,\n            null\n        );\n\n        Map<Integer, Node> nodes = Stream.of(node).collect(Collectors.toMap(Node::id, Function.identity()));\n        MetadataSnapshot metadataCache = new MetadataSnapshot(\n            null,\n            nodes,\n            Collections.singletonList(partMetadata),\n            Collections.emptySet(),\n            Collections.emptySet(),\n            Collections.emptySet(),\n            null,\n            Collections.emptyMap()\n        );\n        return metadataCache.cluster();\n    }\n\n    private BatchAccumulator createBatchAccumulator() {\n        return new BatchAccumulator(\n            new LogContext(),\n            BATCH_SIZE + DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD,\n            Compression.NONE,\n            Integer.MAX_VALUE,  // lingerMs\n            100L,               // retryBackoffMs\n            1000L,              // retryBackoffMaxMs\n            3200,               // deliveryTimeoutMs\n            metrics,\n            \"producer-metrics\",\n            time,\n            null,\n            new BufferPool(TOTAL_SIZE, BATCH_SIZE, metrics, time, \"producer-metrics\")\n        );\n    }\n\n    private void appendRecords(Cluster cluster) throws InterruptedException {\n        byte[] key = \"key\".getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);\n        byte[] value = \"value\".getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);\n\n        for (int i = 0; i < numRecords; i++) {\n            accum.append(\n                TOPIC,\n                PARTITION,\n                0L,\n                key,\n                value,\n                Record.EMPTY_HEADERS,\n                null,\n                1000L,\n                time.milliseconds(),\n                cluster\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void prepareFlush() {\n        accum.beginFlush();\n\n        // Complete all batches to mimic successful sends\n        List<ProducerBatch> batches = new ArrayList<>(accum.getDeque(tp));\n        for (ProducerBatch batch : batches) {\n            batch.complete(0L, time.milliseconds());\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void deallocateBatches() {\n        if (accum != null && tp != null) {\n            List<ProducerBatch> batches = new ArrayList<>(accum.getDeque(tp));\n            for (ProducerBatch batch : batches) {\n                accum.deallocate(batch);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}
