  "content": "{\"content\":\"50: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.AuthorizationException;\\n51: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InterruptException;\\n52: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.InvalidTopicException;\\n53: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.ProducerFencedException;\\n54: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.RecordTooLargeException;\\n55: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.RetriableException;\\n56: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.SerializationException;\\n57: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException;\\n58: import org.apache.kafka.common.header.Header;\\n59: import org.apache.kafka.common.header.Headers;\\n60: import org.apache.kafka.common.header.internals.RecordHeaders;\\n61: import org.apache.kafka.common.internals.ClusterResourceListeners;\\n62: import org.apache.kafka.common.internals.Plugin;\\n63: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.KafkaMetric;\\n64: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.KafkaMetricsContext;\\n65: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.MetricConfig;\\n66: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.Metrics;\\n67: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.MetricsContext;\\n68: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.MetricsReporter;\\n69: import org.apache.kafka.common.metrics.Sensor;\\n70: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.AbstractRecords;\\n71: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.CompressionType;\\n72: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.RecordBatch;\\n73: import org.apache.kafka.common.requests.JoinGroupRequest;\\n74: import org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.Serializer;\\n75: import org.apache.kafka.common.telemetry.internals.ClientTelemetryReporter;\\n76: import org.apache.kafka.common.telemetry.internals.ClientTelemetryUtils;\\n77: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AppInfoParser;\\n78: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\\n79: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\\n80: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Timer;\\n81: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\\n82: \\n83: import org.slf4j.Logger;\\n84: \\n85: import java.net.InetSocketAddress;\\n86: import java.time.Duration;\\n87: import java.util.Arrays;\\n88: import java.util.Collections;\\n89: import java.util.List;\\n90: import java.util.Map;\\n91: import java.util.Objects;\\n92: import java.util.Optional;\\n93: import java.util.Properties;\\n94: import java.util.concurrent.ExecutionException;\\n95: import java.util.concurrent.Future;\\n96: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;\\n97: import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReference;\\n98: \\n99: \\n100: /**\\n101:  * A Kafka client that publishes records to the Kafka cluster.\\n102:  * <P>\\n103:  * The producer is <i>thread safe</i> and sharing a single producer instance across threads will generally be faster than\\n104:  * having multiple instances.\\n105:  * <p>\\n106:  * Here is a simple example of using the producer to send records with strings containing sequential numbers as the key/value\\n107:  * pairs.\\n108:  * <pre>\\n109:  * {@code\\n110:  * Properties props = new Properties();\\n111:  * props.put(\\\"bootstrap.servers\\\", \\\"localhost:9092\\\");\\n112:  * props.put(\\\"linger.ms\\\", 1);\\n113:  * props.put(\\\"key.serializer\\\", \\\"org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer\\\");\\n114:  * props.put(\\\"value.serializer\\\", \\\"org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer\\\");\\n115:  *\\n116:  * Producer<String, String> producer = new KafkaProducer<>(props);\\n117:  * for (int i = 0; i < 100; i++)\\n118:  *     producer.send(new ProducerRecord<String, String>(\\\"my-topic\\\", Integer.toString(i), Integer.toString(i)));\\n119:  *\\n120:  * producer.close();\\n121:  * }</pre>\\n122:  * <p>\\n123:  * The producer consists of a pool of buffer space that holds records that haven't yet been transmitted to the server\\n124:  * as well as a background I/O thread that is responsible for turning these records into requests and transmitting them\\n125:  * to the cluster. Failure to close the producer after use will leak these resources.\\n126:  * <p>\\n127:  * The {@link #send(ProducerRecord) send()} method is asynchronous. When called, it adds the record to a buffer of pending record sends\\n128:  * and immediately returns. This allows the producer to batch together individual records for efficiency.\\n129:  * <p>\\n130:  * The <code>acks</code> config controls the criteria under which requests are considered complete. The default setting \\\"all\\\"\\n131:  * will result in blocking on the full commit of the record, the slowest but most durable setting.\\n132:  * <p>\\n133:  * If the request fails, the producer can automatically retry. The <code>retries</code> setting defaults to <code>Integer.MAX_VALUE</code>, and\\n134:  * it's recommended to use <code>delivery.timeout.ms</code> to control retry behavior, instead of <code>retries</code>.\\n135:  * <p>\\n136:  * The producer maintains buffers of unsent records for each partition. These buffers are of a size specified by\\n137:  * the <code>batch.size</code> config. Making this larger can result in more batching, but requires more memory (since we will\\n138:  * generally have one of these buffers for each active partition).\\n139:  * <p>\\n140:  * By default a buffer is available to send immediately even if there is additional unused space in the buffer. However if you\\n141:  * want to reduce the number of requests you can set <code>linger.ms</code> to something greater than 0. This will\\n142:  * instruct the producer to wait up to that number of milliseconds before sending a request in hope that more records will\\n143:  * arrive to fill up the same batch. This is analogous to Nagle's algorithm in TCP. For example, in the code snippet above,\\n144:  * likely all 100 records would be sent in a single request since we set our linger time to 1 millisecond. However this setting\\n145:  * would add 1 millisecond of latency to our request waiting for more records to arrive if we didn't fill up the buffer. Note that\\n146:  * records that arrive close together in time will generally batch together even with <code>linger.ms=0</code>. So, under heavy load,\\n147:  * batching will occur regardless of the linger configuration; however setting this to something larger than 0 can lead to fewer, more\\n148:  * efficient requests when not under maximal load at the cost of a small amount of latency.\\n149:  * <p>\\n150:  * The <code>buffer.memory</code> controls the total amount of memory available to the producer for buffering. If records\\n151:  * are sent faster than they can be transmitted to the server then this buffer space will be exhausted. When the buffer space is\\n152:  * exhausted additional send calls will block. The threshold for time to block is determined by <code>max.block.ms</code> after which it returns\\n153:  * a failed future with BufferExhaustedException.\\n154:  * <p>\\n155:  * The <code>key.serializer</code> and <code>value.serializer</code> instruct how to turn the key and value objects the user provides with\\n156:  * their <code>ProducerRecord</code> into bytes. You can use the included {@link org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.ByteArraySerializer} or\\n157:  * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer} for simple byte or string types.\\n158:  * <p>\\n159:  * From Kafka 0.11, the KafkaProducer supports two additional modes: the idempotent producer and the transactional producer.\\n160:  * The idempotent producer strengthens Kafka's delivery semantics from at least once to exactly once delivery. In particular\\n161:  * producer retries will no longer introduce duplicates. The transactional producer allows an application to send messages\\n162:  * to multiple partitions (and topics!) atomically.\\n163:  * </p>\\n164:  * <p>\\n165:  * From Kafka 3.0, the <code>enable.idempotence</code> configuration defaults to true. When enabling idempotence,\\n166:  * <code>retries</code> config will default to <code>Integer.MAX_VALUE</code> and the <code>acks</code> config will\\n167:  * default to <code>all</code>. There are no API changes for the idempotent producer, so existing applications will\\n168:  * not need to be modified to take advantage of this feature.\\n169:  * </p>\\n170:  * <p>\\n171:  * To take advantage of the idempotent producer, it is imperative to avoid application level re-sends since these cannot\\n172:  * be de-duplicated. As such, if an application enables idempotence, it is recommended to leave the <code>retries</code>\\n173:  * config unset, as it will be defaulted to <code>Integer.MAX_VALUE</code>. Additionally, if a {@link #send(ProducerRecord)}\\n174:  * returns an error even with infinite retries (for instance if the message expires in the buffer before being sent),\\n175:  * then it is recommended to shut down the producer and check the contents of the last produced message to ensure that\\n176:  * it is not duplicated. Finally, the producer can only guarantee idempotence for messages sent within a single session.\\n177:  * </p>\\n178:  * <p>To use the transactional producer and the attendant APIs, you must set the <code>transactional.id</code>\\n179:  * configuration property. If the <code>transactional.id</code> is set, idempotence is automatically enabled along with\\n180:  * the producer configs which idempotence depends on. Further, topics which are included in transactions should be configured\\n181:  * for durability. In particular, the <code>replication.factor</code> should be at least <code>3</code>, and the\\n182:  * <code>min.insync.replicas</code> for these topics should be set to 2. Finally, in order for transactional guarantees\\n183:  * to be realized from end-to-end, the consumers must be configured to read only committed messages as well.\\n184:  * </p>\\n185:  * <p>\\n186:  * The purpose of the <code>transactional.id</code> is to enable transaction recovery across multiple sessions of a\\n187:  * single producer instance. It would typically be derived from the shard identifier in a partitioned, stateful, application.\\n188:  * As such, it should be unique to each producer instance running within a partitioned application.\\n189:  * </p>\\n190:  * <p>All the new transactional APIs are blocking and will throw exceptions on failure. The example\\n191:  * below illustrates how the new APIs are meant to be used. It is similar to the example above, except that all\\n192:  * 100 messages are part of a single transaction.\\n193:  * </p>\\n194:  * <p>\\n195:  * <pre>\\n196:  * {@code\\n197:  * Properties props = new Properties();\\n198:  * props.put(\\\"bootstrap.servers\\\", \\\"localhost:9092\\\");\\n199:  * props.put(\\\"transactional.id\\\", \\\"my-transactional-id\\\");\\n200:  * Producer<String, String> producer = new KafkaProducer<>(props, new StringSerializer(), new StringSerializer());\\n201:  *\\n202:  * producer.initTransactions();\\n203:  *\\n204:  * try {\\n205:  *     producer.beginTransaction();\\n206:  *     for (int i = 0; i < 100; i++)\\n207:  *         producer.send(new ProducerRecord<>(\\\"my-topic\\\", Integer.toString(i), Integer.toString(i)));\\n208:  *     producer.commitTransaction();\\n209:  * } catch (ProducerFencedException | OutOfOrderSequenceException | AuthorizationException e) {\\n210:  *     // We can't recover from these exceptions, so our only option is to close the producer and exit.\\n211:  *     producer.close();\\n212:  * } catch (KafkaException e) {\\n213:  *     // For all other exceptions, just abort the transaction and try again.\\n214:  *     producer.abortTransaction();\\n215:  * }\\n216:  * producer.close();\\n217:  * } </pre>\\n218:  * </p>\\n219:  * <p>\\n220:  * As is hinted at in the example, there can be only one open transaction per producer. All messages sent between the\\n221:  * {@link #beginTransaction()} and {@link #commitTransaction()} calls will be part of a single transaction. When the\\n222:  * <code>transactional.id</code> is specified, all messages sent by the producer must be part of a transaction.\\n223:  * </p>\\n224:  * <p>\\n225:  * The transactional producer uses exceptions to communicate error states. In particular, it is not required\\n226:  * to specify callbacks for <code>producer.send()</code> or to call <code>.get()</code> on the returned Future: a\\n227:  * <code>KafkaException</code> would be thrown if any of the\\n228:  * <code>producer.send()</code> or transactional calls hit an irrecoverable error during a transaction. See the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)}\\n229:  * documentation for more details about detecting errors from a transactional send.\\n230:  * </p>\\n231:  * </p>By calling\\n232:  * <code>producer.abortTransaction()</code> upon receiving a <code>KafkaException</code> we can ensure that any\\n233:  * successful writes are marked as aborted, hence keeping the transactional guarantees.\\n234:  * </p>\\n235:  * <p>\\n236:  * This client can communicate with brokers that are version 0.10.0 or newer. Older or newer brokers may not support\\n237:  * certain client features.  For instance, the transactional APIs need broker versions 0.11.0 or later. You will receive an\\n238:  * <code>UnsupportedVersionException</code> when invoking an API that is not available in the running broker version.\\n239:  * </p>\\n240:  */\\n241: public class KafkaProducer<K, V> implements Producer<K, V> {\\n242: \\n243:     private final Logger log;\\n244:     private static final String JMX_PREFIX = \\\"kafka.producer\\\";\\n245:     public static final String NETWORK_THREAD_PREFIX = \\\"kafka-producer-network-thread\\\";\\n246:     public static final String PRODUCER_METRIC_GROUP_NAME = \\\"producer-metrics\\\";\\n247: \\n248:     private final String clientId;\\n249:     // Visible for testing\\n250:     final Metrics metrics;\\n251: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java%3FL50-250\"}",

to the server\n124:  * as well as a background I/O thread that is responsible for turning these records into requests and transmitting them\n125:  * to the cluster. Failure to close the producer after use will leak these resources.\n126:  * <p>\n127:  * The {@link #send(ProducerRecord) send()} method is asynchronous. When called, it adds the record to a buffer of pending record sends\n128:  * and immediately returns. This allows the producer to batch together individual records for efficiency.\n129:  * <p>\n130:  * The <code>acks</code> config controls the criteria under which requests are considered complete. The default setting \"all\"\n131:  * will result in blocking on the full commit of the record, the slowest but most durable setting.\n132:  * <p>\n133:  * If the request fails, the producer can automatically retry. The <code>retries</code> setting defaults to <code>Integer.MAX_VALUE</code>, and\n134:  * it's recommended to use <code>delivery.timeout.ms</code> to control retry behavior, instead of <code>retries</code>.\n135:  * <p>\n136:  * The producer maintains buffers of unsent records for each partition. Making this larger can result in more batching, but requires more memory (since we will\n138:  * generally have one of these buffers for each active partition).\n139:  * <p>\n140:  * By default a buffer is available to send immediately even if there is additional unused space in the buffer. However if you\n141:  * want to reduce the number of requests you can set <code>linger.ms</code> to something greater than 0. This will\n142:  * instruct the producer to wait up to that number of milliseconds before sending a request in hope that more records will\n143:  * arrive to fill up the same batch. This is analogous to Nagle's algorithm in TCP. For example, in the code snippet above,\n144:  * likely all 100 records would be sent in a single request since we set our linger time to 1 millisecond. However this setting\n145:  * would add 1 millisecond of latency to our request waiting for more records to arrive if we didn't fill up the buffer. Note that\n146:  * records that arrive close together in time will generally batch together even with <code>linger.ms=0</code>. So, under heavy load,\n147:  * batching will occur regardless of the linger configuration; however setting this to something larger than 0 can lead to fewer, more\n148:  * efficient requests when not under maximal load at the cost of a small amount of latency.\n149:  * <p>\n150:  * The <code>buffer.memory</code> controls the total amount of memory available to the producer for buffering. If records\n151:  * are sent faster than they can be transmitted to the server then this buffer space will be exhausted. When the buffer space is\n152:  * exhausted additional send calls will block. The threshold for time to block is determined by <code>max.block.ms</code> after which it returns\n153:  * a failed future with BufferExhaustedException.\n154:  * <p>\n155:  * The <code>key.serializer</code> and <code>value.serializer</code> instruct how to turn the key and value objects the user provides with\n156:  * their <code>ProducerRecord</code> into bytes. You can use the included {@link org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.ByteArraySerializer} or\n157:  * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer} for simple byte or string types.\n158:  * <p>\n159:  * From Kafka 0.11, the KafkaProducer supports two additional modes: the idempotent producer and the transactional producer.\n160:  * The idempotent producer strengthens Kafka's delivery semantics from at least once to exactly once delivery. In particular\n161:  * producer retries will no longer introduce duplicates. The transactional producer allows an application to send messages\n162:  * to multiple partitions (and topics!) atomically.\n163:  * </p>\n164:  * <p>\n165:  * From Kafka 3.0, the <code>enable.idempotence</code> configuration defaults to true. When enabling idempotence,\n166:  * <code>retries</code> config will default to <code>Integer.MAX_VALUE</code> and the <code>acks</code> config will\n167:  * default to <code>all</code>. There are no API changes for the idempotent producer, so existing applications will\n168:  * not need to be modified to take advantage of this feature.\n169:  * </p>\n170:  * <p>\n171:  * To take advantage of the idempotent producer, it is imperative to avoid application level re-sends since these cannot\n172:  * be de-duplicated. As such, if an application enables idempotence, it is recommended to leave the <code>retries</code>\n173:  * config unset, as it will be defaulted to <code>Integer.MAX_VALUE</code>. Additionally, if a {@link #send(ProducerRecord)}\n174:  * returns an error even with infinite retries (for instance if the message expires in the buffer before being sent),\n175:  * then it is recommended to shut down the producer and check the contents of the last produced message to ensure that\n176:  * it is not duplicated. Finally, the producer can only guarantee idempotence for messages sent within a single session.\n177:  * </p>\n178:  * <p>To use the transactional producer and the attendant APIs, you must set the <code>transactional.id</code>\n179:  * configuration property. If the <code>transactional.id</code> is set, idempotence is automatically enabled along with\n180:  * the producer configs which idempotence depends on. Further, topics which are included in transactions should be configured\n181:  * for durability. In particular, the <code>replication.factor</code> should be at least <code>3</code>, and the\n182:  * <code>min.insync.replicas</code> for these topics should be set to 2. Finally, in order for transactional guarantees\n183:  * to be realized from end-to-end, the consumers must be configured to read only committed messages as well.\n184:  * </p>\n185:  * <p>\n186:  * The purpose of the <code>transactional.id</code> is to enable transaction recovery across multiple sessions of a\n187:  * single producer instance. It would typically be derived from the shard identifier in a partitioned, stateful, application.\n188:  * As such, it should be unique to each producer instance running within a partitioned application.\n189:  * </p>\n190:  * <p>All the new transactional APIs are blocking and will throw exceptions on failure. The example\n191:  * below illustrates how the new APIs are meant to be used. It is similar to the example above, except that all\n192:  * 100 messages are part of a single transaction.\n193:  * </p>\n194:  * <p>\n195:  * <pre>\n196:  * {@code\n197:  * Properties props = new Properties();\n198:  * props.put(\"bootstrap.servers\", \"localhost:9092\");\n199:  * props.put(\"transactional.id\", \"my-transactional-id\");\n200:  * Producer<String, String> producer = new KafkaProducer<>(props, new StringSerializer(), new StringSerializer());\n201:  *\n202:  * producer.initTransactions();\n203:  *\n204:  * try {\n205:  *     producer.beginTransaction();\n206:  *     for (int i = 0; i < 100; i++)\n207:  *         producer.send(new ProducerRecord<>(\"my-topic\", Integer.toString(i), Integer.toString(i)));\n208:  *     producer.commitTransaction();\n209:  * } catch (ProducerFencedException | OutOfOrderSequenceException | AuthorizationException e) {\n210:  *     // We can't recover from these exceptions, so our only option is to close the producer and exit.\n211:  *     producer.close();\n212:  * } catch (KafkaException e) {\n213:  *     // For all other exceptions, just abort the transaction and try again.\n214:  *     producer.abortTransaction();\n215:  * }\n216:  * producer.close();\n217:  * } </pre>\n218:  * </p>\n219:  * <p>\n220:  * As is hinted at in the example, there can be only one open transaction per producer. All messages sent between the\n221:  * {@link #beginTransaction()} and {@link #commitTransaction()} calls will be part of a single transaction. When the\n222:  * <code>transactional.id</code> is specified, all messages sent by the producer must be part of a transaction.\n223:  * </p>\n224:  * <p>\n225:  * The transactional producer uses exceptions to communicate error states. In particular, it is not required\n226:  * to specify callbacks for <code>producer.send()</code> or to call <code>.get()</code> on the returned Future: a\n227:  * <code>KafkaException</code> would be thrown if any of the\n228:  * <code>producer.send()</code> or transactional calls hit an irrecoverable error during a transaction. See the {@link #send(ProducerRecord)}\n229:  * documentation for more details about detecting errors from a transactional send.\n230:  * </p>\n231:  * </p>By calling\n232:  * <code>producer.abortTransaction()</code> upon receiving a <code>KafkaException</code> we can ensure that any\n233:  * successful writes are marked as aborted, hence keeping the transactional guarantees.\n234:  * </p>\n235:  * <p>\n236:  * This client can communicate with brokers that are version 0.10.0 or newer. Older or newer brokers may not support\n237:  * certain client features.  For instance, the transactional APIs need broker versions 0.11.0 or later. You will receive an\n238:  * <code>UnsupportedVersionException</code> when invoking an API that is not available in the running broker version.\n239:  * </p>\n240:  */\n241: public class KafkaProducer<K, V> implements Producer<K, V> {\n242: \n243:     private final Logger log;\n244:     private static final String JMX_PREFIX = \"kafka.producer\";\n245:     public static final String NETWORK_THREAD_PREFIX = \"kafka-producer-network-thread\";\n246:     public static final String PRODUCER_METRIC_GROUP_NAME = \"producer-metrics\";\n247: \n248:     private final String clientId;\n249:     // Visible for testing\n250:     final Metrics metrics;\n251: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java%3FL50-250"
  }
}

54. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "## Files Examined\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java` \u2014 **primary definition** of the class being renamed; contains the class declaration, two constructors, inner classes `RecordAppendResult`, `ReadyCheckResult`, `PartitionerConfig`, `AppendCallbacks`, and `NodeLatencyStats`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java` \u2014 imports and uses `RecordAccumulator` as a field type, in two constructor overloads, and references `RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig` and `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java` \u2014 imports and uses `RecordAccumulator` as a field type, in its constructor, and references `RecordAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java` \u2014 comment on line 34 names `RecordAccumulator` as the caller; comment on line 256 references `RecordAccumulator#partitionReady`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/Node.java` \u2014 comment on line 35 references `RecordAccumulator.ready` as a perf-sensitive caller\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulatorTest.java` \u2014 test class for `RecordAccumulator`; needs renaming to `BatchAccumulatorTest` with all usages updated\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/SenderTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator`, uses it as a field, and references inner types `AppendCallbacks` and `PartitionerConfig`\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/TransactionManagerTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator` and instantiates it directly\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducerTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator` and references inner types `AppendCallbacks` and `RecordAppendResult`\n- `jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2014 benchmark class that imports and directly uses `RecordAccumulator`; needs renaming to `BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark`\n- `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppression entries reference the filename `RecordAccumulator.java` in three regex patterns for `ParameterNumber`, `CyclomaticComplexity`, and `NPathComplexity` checks\n\n## Dependency Chain\n\n1. **Definition**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java` \u2014 original class definition\n2. **Direct usage (production)**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator`, holds it as a field, constructs it, and uses inner types\n3. **Direct usage (production)**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator`, holds it as a constructor parameter and field, uses `ReadyCheckResult`\n4. **Comment reference**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java` \u2014 Javadoc comments name `RecordAccumulator` as the context class\n5. **Comment reference**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/Node.java` \u2014 inline comment references `RecordAccumulator.ready`\n6. **Test**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulatorTest.java` \u2014 tests the class directly\n7. **Transitive (via Sender)**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/SenderTest.java` \u2014 creates `RecordAccumulator` instances to test Sender\n8. **Transitive (via TransactionManager + Sender)**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/TransactionManagerTest.java` \u2014 creates `RecordAccumulator` instances to test transaction manager via Sender\n9. **Transitive (via KafkaProducer)**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducerTest.java` \u2014 mocks and references `RecordAccumulator` inner types\n10. **Benchmark**: `jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2014 directly benchmarks `RecordAccumulator`\n11. **Style config**: `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppresses checkstyle warnings for `RecordAccumulator.java` filename\n\n## Code Changes\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java \u2192 BatchAccumulator.java\n\nThe entire file is renamed. All internal uses of the class name change:\n\n```diff\n-public class RecordAccumulator {\n+public class BatchAccumulator {\n\n-    public RecordAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n+    public BatchAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n                              ...\n-        this.log = logContext.logger(RecordAccumulator.class);\n+        this.log = logContext.logger(BatchAccumulator.class);\n\n-    public RecordAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n+    public BatchAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n                              ...\n```\n\nInner class names (`RecordAppendResult`, `ReadyCheckResult`, `PartitionerConfig`, `AppendCallbacks`, `NodeLatencyStats`) are unchanged \u2014 they are inner members of the renamed outer class.\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-    private final RecordAccumulator accumulator;\n+    private final BatchAccumulator accumulator;\n\n     ...\n-            RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig partitionerConfig = new RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig(\n+            BatchAccumulator.PartitionerConfig partitionerConfig = new BatchAccumulator.PartitionerConfig(\n                 enableAdaptivePartitioning,\n                 config.getLong(ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_AVAILABILITY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n             );\n-            this.accumulator = new RecordAccumulator(logContext,\n+            this.accumulator = new BatchAccumulator(logContext,\n\n     ...\n     // visible for testing\n     KafkaProducer(ProducerConfig config,\n                   LogContext logContext,\n                   Metrics metrics,\n                   Serializer<K> keySerializer,\n                   Serializer<V> valueSerializer,\n                   ProducerMetadata metadata,\n-                  RecordAccumulator accumulator,\n+                  BatchAccumulator accumulator,\n                   TransactionManager transactionManager,\n\n     ...\n         // Append callback takes care of the following:\n         //  - call interceptors and user callback on completion\n-        //  - remember partition that is calculated in RecordAccumulator.append\n+        //  - remember partition that is calculated in BatchAccumulator.append\n\n     ...\n             // Try to calculate partition, but note that after this call it can be RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION,\n-            // which means that the RecordAccumulator would pick a partition using built-in logic (which may\n+            // which means that the BatchAccumulator would pick a partition using built-in logic (which may\n             // take into account broker load, the amount of data produced to each partition, etc.).\n\n     ...\n-            RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult result = accumulator.append(record.topic(), partition, timestamp, serializedKey,\n+            BatchAccumulator.RecordAppendResult result = accumulator.append(record.topic(), partition, timestamp, serializedKey,\n```\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-    /* the record accumulator that batches records */\n-    private final RecordAccumulator accumulator;\n+    /* the record accumulator that batches records */\n+    private final BatchAccumulator accumulator;\n\n     ...\n     public Sender(LogContext logContext,\n                   KafkaClient client,\n                   ProducerMetadata metadata,\n-                  RecordAccumulator accumulator,\n+                  BatchAccumulator accumulator,\n                   boolean guaranteeMessageOrder,\n\n     ...\n-        RecordAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult result = this.accumulator.ready(metadataSnapshot, now);\n+        BatchAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult result = this.accumulator.ready(metadataSnapshot, now);\n```\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java\n\n```diff\n  /**\n   * Built-in default partitioner.  Note, that this is just a utility class that is used directly from\n-  * RecordAccumulator, it does not implement the Partitioner interface.\n+  * BatchAccumulator, it does not implement the Partitioner interface.\n\n     ...\n-        // See also RecordAccumulator#partitionReady where the queueSizes are built.\n+        // See also BatchAccumulator#partitionReady where the queueSizes are built.\n```\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/Node.java\n\n```diff\n-    // Cache hashCode as it is called in performance sensitive parts of the code (e.g. RecordAccumulator.ready)\n+    // Cache hashCode as it is called in performance sensitive parts of the code (e.g. BatchAccumulator.ready)\n```\n\n### checkstyle/suppressions.xml\n\n```diff\n     <suppress checks=\"ParameterNumber\"\n-              files=\"(RecordAccumulator|Sender).java\"/>\n+              files=\"(BatchAccumulator|Sender).java\"/>\n\n     ...\n     <suppress checks=\"CyclomaticComplexity\"\n-              files=\"(...|RecordAccumulator|...).java\"/>\n+              files=\"(...|BatchAccumulator|...).java\"/>\n\n     ...\n     <suppress checks=\"NPathComplexity\"\n-              files=\"(...|RecordAccumulator|...).java\"/>\n+              files=\"(...|BatchAccumulator|...).java\"/>\n```\n\n### clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulatorTest.java \u2192 BatchAccumulatorTest.java\n\nThe file is renamed. All internal substitutions:\n\n```diff\n-public class RecordAccumulatorTest {\n+public class BatchAccumulatorTest {\n\n-    private RecordAccumulator createTestRecordAccumulator(int batchSize, long totalSize, Compression compression, int lingerMs) {\n+    private BatchAccumulator createTestBatchAccumulator(int batchSize, long totalSize, Compression compression, int lingerMs) {\n\n-    private RecordAccumulator createTestRecordAccumulator(int deliveryTimeoutMs, int batchSize, long totalSize, Compression compression, int lingerMs) {\n+    private BatchAccumulator createTestBatchAccumulator(int deliveryTimeoutMs, int batchSize, long totalSize, Compression compression, int lingerMs) {\n\n-    private RecordAccumulator createTestRecordAccumulator(TransactionManager txnManager, ...) {\n+    private BatchAccumulator createTestBatchAccumulator(TransactionManager txnManager, ...) {\n         ...\n-        return new RecordAccumulator(...) {\n+        return new BatchAccumulator(...) {\n```\n\nAll call sites of `createTestRecordAccumulator(...)` in the test class are also updated to `createTestBatchAccumulator(...)`.\n\nAll occurrences of `RecordAccumulator.` used to qualify inner types (e.g. `RecordAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult`) change to `BatchAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult`.\n\n### clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/SenderTest.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-    private RecordAccumulator accumulator = null;\n+ BatchAccumulator(logContext, batchSize, Compression.NONE, 0, 0L, 0L,\n                 deliveryTimeoutMs, metrics, metricGrpName, time, transactionManager,\n\n     ...\n- accumulator = new BatchAccumulator(logContext, 16 * 1024, Compression.NONE, 0, 0L, 0L,\n```\n\n### transactionManager,\n```\n\n### clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducerTest.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n- invocation.getArguments()[6];\n             callbacks.setPartition(initialSelectedPartition.partition());\n-            return new RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult(\n+            return new BatchAccumulator.RecordAppendResult(\n                 futureRecordMetadata,\n```\n\n### jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java \u2192 BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-public class RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n+public class BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n\n     ...\n-    private RecordAccumulator accum;\n+    private BatchAccumulator accum;\n\n     ...\n-        accum = createRecordAccumulator();\n+        accum = createBatchAccumulator();\n\n     ...\n-    private RecordAccumulator createRecordAccumulator() {\n-        return new RecordAccumulator(\n+    private BatchAccumulator createBatchAccumulator() {\n+        return new BatchAccumulator(\n```\n\n## Analysis\n\n### Refactoring Strategy\n\nThis is a pure rename refactoring: the class `RecordAccumulator` is renamed to `BatchAccumulator` while all observable behaviour, method signatures, and inner-class interfaces remain identical. The rename is justified because the class accumulates `ProducerBatch` objects (not individual records) in per-partition deques, and its primary methods (`append`, `drain`, `ready`) all operate at batch granularity.\n\n### Affected Areas\n\n**Production code (4 files):**\n- `RecordAccumulator.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulator.java`: The source of truth. Two constructor signatures and the logger `logger(Class)` call must reference the new name.\n- `KafkaProducer.java`: One import, one field declaration, one constructor that creates a `BatchAccumulator`, one overloaded test-visible constructor that accepts a `BatchAccumulator`, and two inner-type references (`PartitionerConfig`, `RecordAppendResult`).\n- `Sender.java`: One import, one field declaration, one constructor parameter, one inner-type reference (`ReadyCheckResult`).\n- `BuiltInPartitioner.java` & `Node.java`: Comment-only changes; no compilation impact.\n\n**Test code (4 files):**\n- `RecordAccumulatorTest.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulatorTest.java`: File and class renamed; factory methods renamed; `new RecordAccumulator(...)` constructor calls updated.\n- `SenderTest.java`: Import, field type, all `new RecordAccumulator(...)` calls, and `RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks` / `RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig` usages.\n- `TransactionManagerTest.java`: Import, field type, all `new RecordAccumulator(...)` calls.\n- `KafkaProducerTest.java`: Import and `RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks` / `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` inner-type references.\n\n**Benchmark (1 file):**\n- `RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java`: File and class renamed; field type and factory method renamed.\n\n**Checkstyle (1 file):**\n- `suppressions.xml`: Three regex patterns that include `RecordAccumulator` must be updated to `BatchAccumulator` to keep suppressions effective.\n\n### Inner Class Names\n\nThe inner classes `RecordAppendResult`, `ReadyCheckResult`, `PartitionerConfig`, `AppendCallbacks`, and `NodeLatencyStats` are **not renamed** \u2014 the task specification only asks to rename the outer class. Their qualified names change from `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` etc. to `BatchAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` etc., but the simple names are preserved.\n\n### Verification Approach\n\nAfter applying all changes:\n1. `grep -r \"RecordAccumulator\" clients/ jmh-benchmarks/ checkstyle/` should return only `ProducerBatch.java` (which has a Javadoc comment referencing `RecordAccumulator` in the context of the old name \u2014 this is a comment-only reference that can optionally also be updated) and no compilation-relevant references.\n2. The full producer test suite (`./gradlew :clients:test --tests \"*BatchAccumulatorTest\"`, `\"*SenderTest\"`, `\"*TransactionManagerTest\"`, `\"*KafkaProducerTest\"`) should compile and pass without modification to the test logic, since only names changed.\n3. The JMH benchmark module should compile with the renamed class.\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/solution.md"
}

{
  "content": "## Files Examined\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java` \u2014 **primary definition** of the class being renamed; contains the class declaration, two constructors, inner classes `RecordAppendResult`, `ReadyCheckResult`, `PartitionerConfig`, `AppendCallbacks`, and `NodeLatencyStats`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java` \u2014 imports and uses `RecordAccumulator` as a field type, in two constructor overloads, and references `RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig` and `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java` \u2014 imports and uses `RecordAccumulator` as a field type, in its constructor, and references `RecordAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java` \u2014 comment on line 34 names `RecordAccumulator` as the caller; comment on line 256 references `RecordAccumulator#partitionReady`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/Node.java` \u2014 comment on line 35 references `RecordAccumulator.ready` as a perf-sensitive caller\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulatorTest.java` \u2014 test class for `RecordAccumulator`; needs renaming to `BatchAccumulatorTest` with all usages updated\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/SenderTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator`, uses it as a field, and references inner types `AppendCallbacks` and `PartitionerConfig`\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/TransactionManagerTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator` and instantiates it directly\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducerTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator` and references inner types `AppendCallbacks` and `RecordAppendResult`\n- `jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2014 benchmark class that imports and directly uses `RecordAccumulator`; needs renaming to `BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark`\n- `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppression entries reference the filename `RecordAccumulator.java` in three regex patterns for `ParameterNumber`, `CyclomaticComplexity`, and `NPathComplexity` checks\n\n## Dependency Chain\n\n1. **Definition**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java` \u2014 original class definition\n2. **Direct usage (production)**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator`, holds it as a field, constructs it, and uses inner types\n3. **Direct usage (production)**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator`, holds it as a constructor parameter and field, uses `ReadyCheckResult`\n4. **Comment reference**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java` \u2014 Javadoc comments name `RecordAccumulator` as the context class\n5. **Comment reference**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/Node.java` \u2014 inline comment references `RecordAccumulator.ready`\n6. **Test**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulatorTest.java` \u2014 tests the class directly\n7. **Transitive (via Sender)**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/SenderTest.java` \u2014 creates `RecordAccumulator` instances to test Sender\n8. **Transitive (via TransactionManager + Sender)**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/TransactionManagerTest.java` \u2014 creates `RecordAccumulator` instances to test transaction manager via Sender\n9. **Transitive (via KafkaProducer)**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducerTest.java` \u2014 mocks and references `RecordAccumulator` inner types\n10. **Benchmark**: `jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2014 directly benchmarks `RecordAccumulator`\n11. **Style config**: `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppresses checkstyle warnings for `RecordAccumulator.java` filename\n\n## Code Changes\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java \u2192 BatchAccumulator.java\n\nThe entire file is renamed. All internal uses of the class name change:\n\n```diff\n-public class RecordAccumulator {\n+public class BatchAccumulator {\n\n-    public RecordAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n+    public BatchAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n                              ...\n-        this.log = logContext.logger(RecordAccumulator.class);\n+        this.log = logContext.logger(BatchAccumulator.class);\n\n-    public RecordAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n+    public BatchAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n                              ...\n```\n\nInner class names (`RecordAppendResult`, `ReadyCheckResult`, `PartitionerConfig`, `AppendCallbacks`, `NodeLatencyStats`) are unchanged \u2014 they are inner members of the renamed outer class.\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import BatchAccumulator.PartitionerConfig(\n                 enableAdaptivePartitioning,\n                 config.getLong(ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_AVAILABILITY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n             );\n- config,\n                   LogContext logContext,\n                   Metrics metrics,\n                   Serializer<K> keySerializer,\n                   Serializer<V> valueSerializer,\n RecordAccumulator.append\n+        //  - remember partition that is calculated in BatchAccumulator.append\n\n     ...\n             // Try to calculate partition, but note that after this call it can be RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION,\n-            // which means that the RecordAccumulator would pick a partition using built-in logic (which may\n+            // which means that the BatchAccumulator would pick a partition using built-in logic (which may\n             // take into account broker load, the amount of data produced to each partition, etc.).\n\n     ...\n- 57. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 540,
  "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/ProducerBatch.java",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489",
  "startLine": 525
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"525:     /**\\n526:      * Abort the record builder and reset the state of the underlying buffer. This is used prior to aborting\\n527:      * the batch with {@link #abort(RuntimeException)} and ensures that no record previously appended can be\\n528:      * read. This is used in scenarios where we want to ensure a batch ultimately gets aborted, but in which\\n529:      * it is not safe to invoke the completion callbacks (e.g. because we are holding a lock, such as\\n530:      * when aborting batches in {@link RecordAccumulator}).\\n531:      */\\n532:     public void abortRecordAppends() {\\n533:         recordsBuilder.abort();\\n534:     }\\n535: \\n536:     public boolean isClosed() {\\n537:         return recordsBuilder.isClosed();\\n538:     }\\n539: \\n540:     public ByteBuffer buffer() {\\n541: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/ProducerBatch.java%3FL525-540\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "525:     /**\n526:      * Abort the record builder and reset the state of the underlying buffer. This is used prior to aborting\n527:      * the batch with {@link #abort(RuntimeException)} and ensures that no record previously appended can be\n528:      * read. This is used in scenarios where we want to ensure a batch ultimately gets aborted, but in which\n529:      * it is not safe to invoke the completion callbacks (e.g. because we are holding a lock, such as\n530:      * when aborting batches in {@link RecordAccumulator}).\n531:      */\n532:     public void abortRecordAppends() {\n533:         recordsBuilder.abort();\n534:     }\n535: \n536:     public boolean isClosed() {\n537:         return recordsBuilder.isClosed();\n538:     }\n539: \n540:     public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n541: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--0753c489@31d9200189f81283bf60e929ddc38fd68ac034e2/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/ProducerBatch.java%3FL525-540"

59. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/solution.md",
  "new_string": "- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/ProducerBatch.java` \u2014 Javadoc comment on line 530 uses `{@link RecordAccumulator}` to reference the class by name\n- `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppression entries reference the filename `RecordAccumulator.java` in three regex patterns for `ParameterNumber`, `CyclomaticComplexity`, and `NPathComplexity` checks",
  "old_string": "- `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppression entries reference the filename `RecordAccumulator.java` in three regex patterns for `ParameterNumber`, `CyclomaticComplexity`, and `NPathComplexity` checks",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/logs/agent/solution.md",
  "newString": "- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/ProducerBatch.java` \u2014 Javadoc comment on line 530 uses `{@link RecordAccumulator}` to reference the class by name\n- `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppression entries reference the filename `RecordAccumulator.java` in three regex patterns for `ParameterNumber`, `CyclomaticComplexity`, and `NPathComplexity` checks",
  "oldString": "- `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppression entries reference the filename `RecordAccumulator.java` in three regex patterns for `ParameterNumber`, `CyclomaticComplexity`, and `NPathComplexity` checks",
  "originalFile": "## Files Examined\n\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java` \u2014 **primary definition** of the class being renamed; contains the class declaration, two constructors, inner classes `RecordAppendResult`, `ReadyCheckResult`, `PartitionerConfig`, `AppendCallbacks`, and `NodeLatencyStats`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java` \u2014 imports and uses `RecordAccumulator` as a field type, in two constructor overloads, and references `RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig` and `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java` \u2014 imports and uses `RecordAccumulator` as a field type, in its constructor, and references `RecordAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java` \u2014 comment on line 34 names `RecordAccumulator` as the caller; comment on line 256 references `RecordAccumulator#partitionReady`\n- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/Node.java` \u2014 comment on line 35 references `RecordAccumulator.ready` as a perf-sensitive caller\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulatorTest.java` \u2014 test class for `RecordAccumulator`; needs renaming to `BatchAccumulatorTest` with all usages updated\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/SenderTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator`, uses it as a field, and references inner types `AppendCallbacks` and `PartitionerConfig`\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/TransactionManagerTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator` and instantiates it directly\n- `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducerTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator` and references inner types `AppendCallbacks` and `RecordAppendResult`\n- `jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2014 benchmark class that imports and directly uses `RecordAccumulator`; needs renaming to `BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark`\n- `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppression entries reference the filename `RecordAccumulator.java` in three regex patterns for `ParameterNumber`, `CyclomaticComplexity`, and `NPathComplexity` checks\n\n## Dependency Chain\n\n1. **Definition**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java` \u2014 original class definition\n2. **Direct usage (production)**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator`, holds it as a field, constructs it, and uses inner types\n3. **Direct usage (production)**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator`, holds it as a constructor parameter and field, uses `ReadyCheckResult`\n4. **Comment reference**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java` \u2014 Javadoc comments name `RecordAccumulator` as the context class\n5. **Comment reference**: `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/Node.java` \u2014 inline comment references `RecordAccumulator.ready`\n6. **Test**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulatorTest.java` \u2014 tests the class directly\n7. **Transitive (via Sender)**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/SenderTest.java` \u2014 creates `RecordAccumulator` instances to test Sender\n8. **Transitive (via TransactionManager + Sender)**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/TransactionManagerTest.java` \u2014 creates `RecordAccumulator` instances to test transaction manager via Sender\n9. **Transitive (via KafkaProducer)**: `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducerTest.java` \u2014 mocks and references `RecordAccumulator` inner types\n10. **Benchmark**: `jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2014 directly benchmarks `RecordAccumulator`\n11. **Style config**: `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppresses checkstyle warnings for `RecordAccumulator.java` filename\n\n## Code Changes\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulator.java \u2192 BatchAccumulator.java\n\nThe entire file is renamed. All internal uses of the class name change:\n\n```diff\n-public class RecordAccumulator {\n+public class BatchAccumulator {\n\n-    public RecordAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n+    public BatchAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n                              ...\n-        this.log = logContext.logger(RecordAccumulator.class);\n+        this.log = logContext.logger(BatchAccumulator.class);\n\n-    public RecordAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n+    public BatchAccumulator(LogContext logContext,\n                              ...\n```\n\nInner class names (`RecordAppendResult`, `ReadyCheckResult`, `PartitionerConfig`, `AppendCallbacks`, `NodeLatencyStats`) are unchanged \u2014 they are inner members of the renamed outer class.\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-    private final RecordAccumulator accumulator;\n+    private final BatchAccumulator accumulator;\n\n     ...\n-            RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig partitionerConfig = new RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig(\n+            BatchAccumulator.PartitionerConfig partitionerConfig = new BatchAccumulator.PartitionerConfig(\n                 enableAdaptivePartitioning,\n                 config.getLong(ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_AVAILABILITY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n             );\n-            this.accumulator = new RecordAccumulator(logContext,\n+            this.accumulator = new BatchAccumulator(logContext,\n\n     ...\n     // visible for testing\n     KafkaProducer(ProducerConfig config,\n                   LogContext logContext,\n                   Metrics metrics,\n                   Serializer<K> keySerializer,\n                   Serializer<V> valueSerializer,\n                   ProducerMetadata metadata,\n-                  RecordAccumulator accumulator,\n+                  BatchAccumulator accumulator,\n                   TransactionManager transactionManager,\n\n     ...\n         // Append callback takes care of the following:\n         //  - call interceptors and user callback on completion\n-        //  - remember partition that is calculated in RecordAccumulator.append\n+        //  - remember partition that is calculated in BatchAccumulator.append\n\n     ...\n             // Try to calculate partition, but note that after this call it can be RecordMetadata.UNKNOWN_PARTITION,\n-            // which means that the RecordAccumulator would pick a partition using built-in logic (which may\n+            // which means that the BatchAccumulator would pick a partition using built-in logic (which may\n             // take into account broker load, the amount of data produced to each partition, etc.).\n\n     ...\n-            RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult result = accumulator.append(record.topic(), partition, timestamp, serializedKey,\n+            BatchAccumulator.RecordAppendResult result = accumulator.append(record.topic(), partition, timestamp, serializedKey,\n```\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/Sender.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-    /* the record accumulator that batches records */\n-    private final RecordAccumulator accumulator;\n+    /* the record accumulator that batches records */\n+    private final BatchAccumulator accumulator;\n\n     ...\n     public Sender(LogContext logContext,\n                   KafkaClient client,\n                   ProducerMetadata metadata,\n-                  RecordAccumulator accumulator,\n+                  BatchAccumulator accumulator,\n                   boolean guaranteeMessageOrder,\n\n     ...\n-        RecordAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult result = this.accumulator.ready(metadataSnapshot, now);\n+        BatchAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult result = this.accumulator.ready(metadataSnapshot, now);\n```\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/BuiltInPartitioner.java\n\n```diff\n  /**\n   * Built-in default partitioner.  Note, that this is just a utility class that is used directly from\n-  * RecordAccumulator, it does not implement the Partitioner interface.\n+  * BatchAccumulator, it does not implement the Partitioner interface.\n\n     ...\n-        // See also RecordAccumulator#partitionReady where the queueSizes are built.\n+        // See also BatchAccumulator#partitionReady where the queueSizes are built.\n```\n\n### clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/Node.java\n\n```diff\n-    // Cache hashCode as it is called in performance sensitive parts of the code (e.g. RecordAccumulator.ready)\n+    // Cache hashCode as it is called in performance sensitive parts of the code (e.g. BatchAccumulator.ready)\n```\n\n### checkstyle/suppressions.xml\n\n```diff\n     <suppress checks=\"ParameterNumber\"\n-              files=\"(RecordAccumulator|Sender).java\"/>\n+              files=\"(BatchAccumulator|Sender).java\"/>\n\n     ...\n     <suppress checks=\"CyclomaticComplexity\"\n-              files=\"(...|RecordAccumulator|...).java\"/>\n+              files=\"(...|BatchAccumulator|...).java\"/>\n\n     ...\n     <suppress checks=\"NPathComplexity\"\n-              files=\"(...|RecordAccumulator|...).java\"/>\n+              files=\"(...|BatchAccumulator|...).java\"/>\n```\n\n### clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/RecordAccumulatorTest.java \u2192 BatchAccumulatorTest.java\n\nThe file is renamed. All internal substitutions:\n\n```diff\n-public class RecordAccumulatorTest {\n+public class BatchAccumulatorTest {\n\n-    private RecordAccumulator createTestRecordAccumulator(int batchSize, long totalSize, Compression compression, int lingerMs) {\n+    private BatchAccumulator createTestBatchAccumulator(int batchSize, long totalSize, Compression compression, int lingerMs) {\n\n-    private BatchAccumulator.AppendCallbacks() {\n\n     ...\n-        RecordAccumulator accumulator = new RecordAccumulator(logContext, 16 * 1024, Compression.NONE, 0, 0L, 0L,\n+        BatchAccumulator accumulator = new BatchAccumulator(logContext, 16 * 1024, Compression.NONE, 0, 0L, 0L,\n```\n\n### clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/TransactionManagerTest.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-    private RecordAccumulator accumulator = null;\n+    private BatchAccumulator accumulator = null;\n\n     ...\n-        this.accumulator = new RecordAccumulator(logContext, batchSize, Compression.NONE, 0, 0L, 0L,\n+        this.accumulator = new BatchAccumulator(logContext, batchSize, Compression.NONE, 0, 0L, 0L,\n                 deliveryTimeoutMs, metrics, metricGrpName, time, transactionManager,\n\n     ...\n-        RecordAccumulator accumulator = new RecordAccumulator(logContext, 16 * 1024, Compression.NONE, 0, 0L, 0L,\n+        BatchAccumulator accumulator = new BatchAccumulator(logContext, 16 * 1024, Compression.NONE, 0, 0L, 0L,\n                 deliveryTimeout, metrics, \"\", time, transactionManager,\n```\n\n### clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducerTest.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-            any(RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks.class),    // 6 <--\n+            any(BatchAccumulator.AppendCallbacks.class),    // 6 <--\n\n     ...\n-            RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks callbacks =\n-                (RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks) invocation.getArguments()[6];\n+            BatchAccumulator.AppendCallbacks callbacks =\n+                (BatchAccumulator.AppendCallbacks) invocation.getArguments()[6];\n             callbacks.setPartition(initialSelectedPartition.partition());\n-            return new RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult(\n+            return new BatchAccumulator.RecordAppendResult(\n                 futureRecordMetadata,\n```\n\n### jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java \u2192 BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-public class RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n+public class BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n\n     ...\n-    private RecordAccumulator accum;\n+    private BatchAccumulator accum;\n\n     ...\n-        accum = createRecordAccumulator();\n+        accum = createBatchAccumulator();\n\n     ...\n-    private RecordAccumulator createRecordAccumulator() {\n-        return new RecordAccumulator(\n+    private BatchAccumulator createBatchAccumulator() {\n+        return new BatchAccumulator(\n```\n\n## Analysis\n\n### Refactoring Strategy\n\nThis is a pure rename refactoring: the class `RecordAccumulator` is renamed to `BatchAccumulator` while all observable behaviour, method signatures, and inner-class interfaces remain identical. The rename is justified because the class accumulates `ProducerBatch` objects (not individual records) in per-partition deques, and its primary methods (`append`, `drain`, `ready`) all operate at batch granularity.\n\n### Affected Areas\n\n**Production code (4 files):**\n- `RecordAccumulator.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulator.java`: The source of truth. Two constructor signatures and the logger `logger(Class)` call must reference the new name.\n- `KafkaProducer.java`: One import, one field declaration, one constructor that creates a `BatchAccumulator`, one overloaded test-visible constructor that accepts a `BatchAccumulator`, and two inner-type references (`PartitionerConfig`, `RecordAppendResult`).\n- `Sender.java`: One import, one field declaration, one constructor parameter, one inner-type reference (`ReadyCheckResult`).\n- `BuiltInPartitioner.java` & `Node.java`: Comment-only changes; no compilation impact.\n\n**Test code (4 files):**\n- `RecordAccumulatorTest.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulatorTest.java`: File and class renamed; factory methods renamed; `new RecordAccumulator(...)` constructor calls updated.\n- `SenderTest.java`: Import, field type, all `new RecordAccumulator(...)` calls, and `RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks` / `RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig` usages.\n- `TransactionManagerTest.java`: Import, field type, all `new RecordAccumulator(...)` calls.\n- `KafkaProducerTest.java`: Import and `RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks` / `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` inner-type references.\n\n**Benchmark (1 file):**\n- `RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java`: File and class renamed; field type and factory method renamed.\n\n**Checkstyle (1 file):**\n- `suppressions.xml`: Three regex patterns that include `RecordAccumulator` must be updated to `BatchAccumulator` to keep suppressions effective.\n\n### Inner Class Names\n\nThe inner classes `RecordAppendResult`, `ReadyCheckResult`, `PartitionerConfig`, `AppendCallbacks`, and `NodeLatencyStats` are **not renamed** \u2014 the task specification only asks to rename the outer class. Their qualified names change from `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` etc. to `BatchAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` etc., but the simple names are preserved.\n\n### Verification Approach\n\nAfter applying all changes:\n1. `grep -r \"RecordAccumulator\" clients/ jmh-benchmarks/ checkstyle/` should return only `ProducerBatch.java` (which has a Javadoc comment referencing `RecordAccumulator` in the context of the old name \u2014 this is a comment-only reference that can optionally also be updated) and no compilation-relevant references.\n2. The full producer test suite (`./gradlew :clients:test --tests \"*BatchAccumulatorTest\"`, `\"*SenderTest\"`, `\"*TransactionManagerTest\"`, `\"*KafkaProducerTest\"`) should compile and pass without modification to the test logic, since only names changed.\n3. The JMH benchmark module should compile with the renamed class.\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " - `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/TransactionManagerTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator` and instantiates it directly",
        " - `clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducerTest.java` \u2014 imports `RecordAccumulator` and references inner types `AppendCallbacks` and `RecordAppendResult`",
        " - `jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2014 benchmark class that imports and directly uses `RecordAccumulator`; needs renaming to `BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark`",
        "+- `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/ProducerBatch.java` \u2014 Javadoc comment on line 530 uses `{@link RecordAccumulator}` to reference the class by name",
        " - `checkstyle/suppressions.xml` \u2014 suppression entries reference the filename `RecordAccumulator.java` in three regex patterns for `ParameterNumber`, `CyclomaticComplexity`, and `NPathComplexity` checks",

clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducer.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import BatchAccumulator.PartitionerConfig(\n                 enableAdaptivePartitioning,\n                 config.getLong(ProducerConfig.PARTITIONER_AVAILABILITY_TIMEOUT_MS_CONFIG)\n             );\n- `BatchAccumulator.ReadyCheckResult`.\n\n### clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/SenderTest.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n- invocation.getArguments()[6];\n             callbacks.setPartition(initialSelectedPartition.partition());\n-            return new RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult(\n+            return new BatchAccumulator.RecordAppendResult(\n                 futureRecordMetadata,\n```\n\n### jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java \u2192 BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-public class RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n+public class BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n\n     ...\n-    private RecordAccumulator accum;\n+    private BatchAccumulator accum;\n\n     ...\n-        accum = createRecordAccumulator();\n+        accum = createBatchAccumulator();\n\n     ...\n-    private RecordAccumulator createRecordAccumulator() {\n-        return new RecordAccumulator(\n+    private BatchAccumulator createBatchAccumulator() {\n+        return new BatchAccumulator(\n```\n\n## Analysis\n\n### Refactoring Strategy\n\nThis is a pure rename refactoring: the class `RecordAccumulator` is renamed to `BatchAccumulator` while all observable behaviour, method signatures, and inner-class interfaces remain identical. The rename is justified because the class accumulates `ProducerBatch` objects (not individual records) in per-partition deques, and its primary methods (`append`, `drain`, `ready`) all operate at batch granularity.\n\n### Affected Areas\n\n**Production code (4 files):**\n- `RecordAccumulator.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulator.java`: The source of truth. Two constructor signatures and the logger `logger(Class)` call must reference the new name.\n- `KafkaProducer.java`: One import, one field declaration, one constructor that creates a `BatchAccumulator`, one overloaded test-visible constructor that accepts a `BatchAccumulator`, and two inner-type references (`PartitionerConfig`, `RecordAppendResult`).\n- `Sender.java`: One import, one field declaration, one constructor parameter, one inner-type reference (`ReadyCheckResult`).\n- `BuiltInPartitioner.java` & `Node.java`: Comment-only changes; no compilation impact.\n\n**Test code (4 files):**\n- `RecordAccumulatorTest.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulatorTest.java`: File and class renamed; factory methods renamed; `new RecordAccumulator(...)` constructor calls updated.\n- `SenderTest.java`: Import, field type, all `new RecordAccumulator(...)` calls, and `RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks` / `RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig` usages.\n- `TransactionManagerTest.java`: Import, field type, all `new RecordAccumulator(...)` calls.\n- `KafkaProducerTest.java`: Import and `RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks` / `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` inner-type references.\n\n**Benchmark (1 file):**\n- `RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java`: File and class renamed; field type and factory method renamed.\n\n**Checkstyle (1 file):**\n- `suppressions.xml`: Three regex patterns that include `RecordAccumulator` must be updated to `BatchAccumulator` to keep suppressions effective.\n\n### Inner Class Names\n\nThe inner classes `RecordAppendResult`, `ReadyCheckResult`, `PartitionerConfig`, `AppendCallbacks`, and `NodeLatencyStats` are **not renamed** \u2014 the task specification only asks to rename the outer class. Their qualified names change from `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` etc. to `BatchAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` etc., but the simple names are preserved.\n\n### Verification Approach\n\nAfter applying all changes:\n1. `grep -r \"RecordAccumulator\" clients/ jmh-benchmarks/ checkstyle/` should return only `ProducerBatch.java` (which has a Javadoc comment referencing `RecordAccumulator` in the context of the old name \u2014 this is a comment-only reference that can optionally also be updated) and no compilation-relevant references.\n2. The full producer test suite (`./gradlew :clients:test --tests \"*BatchAccumulatorTest\"`, `\"*SenderTest\"`, `\"*TransactionManagerTest\"`, `\"*KafkaProducerTest\"`) should compile and pass without modification to the test logic, since only names changed.\n3. The JMH benchmark module should compile with the renamed class.\n",

clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/internals/TransactionManagerTest.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import transactionManager,\n```\n\n### clients/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/clients/producer/KafkaProducerTest.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-            any(RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks.class),    // 6 <--\n+            any(BatchAccumulator.AppendCallbacks.class),    // 6 <--\n\n     ...\n-            RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks callbacks =\n-                (RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks) invocation.getArguments()[6];\n+            BatchAccumulator.AppendCallbacks callbacks =\n+                (BatchAccumulator.AppendCallbacks) invocation.getArguments()[6];\n             callbacks.setPartition(initialSelectedPartition.partition());\n-            return new RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult(\n+            return new BatchAccumulator.RecordAppendResult(\n                 futureRecordMetadata,\n```\n\n### jmh-benchmarks/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/jmh/producer/RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java \u2192 BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java\n\n```diff\n-import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.RecordAccumulator;\n+import org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.internals.BatchAccumulator;\n\n     ...\n-public class RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n+public class BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark {\n\n     ...\n-    private RecordAccumulator accum;\n+    private BatchAccumulator accum;\n\n     ...\n-        accum = createRecordAccumulator();\n+        accum = createBatchAccumulator();\n\n     ...\n-    private RecordAccumulator createRecordAccumulator() {\n-        return new RecordAccumulator(\n+    private BatchAccumulator createBatchAccumulator() {\n+        return new BatchAccumulator(\n```\n\n## Analysis\n\n### Refactoring Strategy\n\nThis is a pure rename refactoring: the class `RecordAccumulator` is renamed to `BatchAccumulator` while all observable behaviour, method signatures, and inner-class interfaces remain identical. The rename is justified because the class accumulates `ProducerBatch` objects (not individual records) in per-partition deques, and its primary methods (`append`, `drain`, `ready`) all operate at batch granularity.\n\n### Affected Areas\n\n**Production code (4 files):**\n- `RecordAccumulator.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulator.java`: The source of truth. Two constructor signatures and the logger `logger(Class)` call must reference the new name.\n- `KafkaProducer.java`: One import, one field declaration, one constructor that creates a `BatchAccumulator`, one overloaded test-visible constructor that accepts a `BatchAccumulator`, and two inner-type references (`PartitionerConfig`, `RecordAppendResult`).\n- `Sender.java`: One import, one field declaration, one constructor parameter, one inner-type reference (`ReadyCheckResult`).\n- `BuiltInPartitioner.java` & `Node.java`: Comment-only changes; no compilation impact.\n\n**Test code (4 files):**\n- `RecordAccumulatorTest.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulatorTest.java`: File and class renamed; factory methods renamed; `new RecordAccumulator(...)` constructor calls updated.\n- `SenderTest.java`: Import, field type, all `new RecordAccumulator(...)` calls, and `RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks` / `RecordAccumulator.PartitionerConfig` usages.\n- `TransactionManagerTest.java`: Import, field type, all `new RecordAccumulator(...)` calls.\n- `KafkaProducerTest.java`: Import and `RecordAccumulator.AppendCallbacks` / `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` inner-type references.\n\n**Benchmark (1 file):**\n- `RecordAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java` \u2192 `BatchAccumulatorFlushBenchmark.java`: File and class renamed; field type and factory method renamed.\n\n**Checkstyle (1 file):**\n- `suppressions.xml`: Three regex patterns that include `RecordAccumulator` must be updated to `BatchAccumulator` to keep suppressions effective.\n\n### Inner Class Names\n\nThe inner classes `RecordAppendResult`, `ReadyCheckResult`, `PartitionerConfig`, `AppendCallbacks`, and `NodeLatencyStats` are **not renamed** \u2014 the task specification only asks to rename the outer class. Their qualified names change from `RecordAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` etc. to `BatchAccumulator.RecordAppendResult` etc., but the simple names are preserved.\n\n### Verification Approach\n\nAfter applying all changes:\n1. `grep -r \"RecordAccumulator\" clients/ jmh-benchmarks/ checkstyle/` should return only `ProducerBatch.java` (which has a Javadoc comment referencing `RecordAccumulator` in the context of the old name \u2014 this is a comment-only reference that can optionally also be updated) and no compilation-relevant references.\n2. The full producer test suite (`./gradlew :clients:test --tests \"*BatchAccumulatorTest\"`, `\"*SenderTest\"`, `\"*TransactionManagerTest\"`, `\"*KafkaProducerTest\"`) should compile and pass without modification to the test logic, since only names changed.\n3. The JMH benchmark module should compile with the renamed class.\n",

+     * when aborting batches in {@link BatchAccumulator}).
